review_text: Had her for ACO321 which wasn't listed for some reason  She is a tough grader but it isn't for no reason, the material is just difficult and requires a high attention to detail. If I'm being honest, I think a lot of these bad reviews are coming form students who did poorly or did not enjoy the subject matter. Massively overhated.

Outdated lectures that overcomplicate simple topics and fail to teach practical use. Assignments are vague, grading is inconsistent, and her expensive textbook adds no value. Tests are mostly open-ended and memory-based. You're better off learning from YouTube or asking your advisor for a different class.

This professor is quite mediocre and overestimates herself, likely because she wrote a book. Her teaching is outdated, lectures are boring, and the textbook is pricey yet less helpful than free YouTube videos. She tends to overcomplicate simple topics, turning straightforward content into long-winded confusion.

Dietrich is a grandma, not a professor  Dietrich should retire... She is far too old and behind to be in any professional field.   She gets zero respect in the class, and for good reason...  It is a truly a shame that ASU entertains people like this.  DO NOT take her and if you do, make sure to report her to speed up her long awaited retirement.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Graded by few things

review_text: Dr Dietrich is knowledgeable about databases but the assignments are difficult I had to rely on her own textbook to complete those and even the book doesn't explain stuff clearly. She is rude and condescending to her students and honestly I don't want to ask questions since she point out you are wrong and doesn't do a good job explaining things.

Dr. D is very knowledgeable about databases and programming, no question. However, she gives very tough assignments and has no mercy in grading. She is notorious for scoffing if you ask a question that's "easily answerable".  Do NOT take her for 240 if you can. 320 was hard but covered a lot of useful topics. Study for the tests or you will fail.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Participation matters

review_text: 3 types of attendance in every class. Vague lectures that show basics, not how to put them into practice. Projects come in multiple parts and each part only has less than half a paragraph of instructions. Tests are primarily open-ended and written so you better have perfect memory. Ask your advisor if you can take a summer class elsewhere.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Hard class.  Lots of work.  Extremely large amount of material to learn in one semester.  She is a good teacher though and is available outside class to help.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

review_text: Dietrich isn't a terrible lecture teacher, but her grading process is literally the worst I've ever seen. She makes the final & midterm %50 of the grade,if you do bad on the midterm just drop the class. The hw coding assignments aren't bad but the final coding assignments are such a jump from what she teaches in class. Take this class elsewhere.

She is condescending and I taken her class for the first time and our first quiz which was focused around python was hard and the quiz was on paper. Our first assignment was difficult she gave vague instructions. Ended up dropping the class a couple weeks after our first quiz. Don't take her class unless if you want to fail and tank your GPA.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

review_text: First day of class was 60ish students, 1 week after midterm results, like 17 students which more than half are failing. If you want to learn, she is not the professor you want.
I've never use the "What-If (My grades are ...)" section on Canvas until I had to take her class. Absolutely demolished.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!!!! YES, SHE IS THAT BAD!!! AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Do yourself a favor and run away from this professor. Super Awful lectures you are not going to learn a single thing. This class is highly based on exams, yes they will be taking in class 70% on exams yep that's a lot. Nothing to do with the study guide.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Dietrich is knowledgeable but does not know how to present it. Examples were minimally useful and class time was wasted. The homework assignments were nonstop and hardly related to the tests and programming assignments. I did not find the study guides useful whatsoever. If you have another option for this class take it or switch to Tempe.

Acts like a know it all and very snobby to those who ask questions. Doesn't really explain concepts in depth as she thinks students would already know the information that we're learning. Extremely tough grader and does not prepare her students for any assignment. Office hours are there so she can belittle you. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!
I wrote the last comment and wish I could delete it.  It's closed-minded and non constructive.  I hope no one cares too much about that and more about growth.

Technology makes students in her class lazy and entitled.  Prof Dietrich delivers content, but it's the students' responsibility to learn.  I'm looking back, because tech workers ask too many of the wrong questions.

Not that great of a teacher, links good resources, but if you don't already know the languages you'll struggle a lot. If you do poorly on the midterm drop the class. Tests are in depth on few topics and the study guides may not be accurate. Assignments are difficult and time consuming. Class averages were D's and F's for assignments and tests.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: She knows the material well enough to teach it, but she should NOT teach it. She only reads from the slides, ridicules those who ask questions. Her exams do NOT cover all material, she chooses a few topics and you better hope you know those inside or out. My class averaged a 48% on the final. I went from a A- to a C+. AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!!!!!!!!!!!

She's tough. Her tests require you to expand on topics covered in class. There is a lot of homework and she requires "high level" questions in class. But if you give her respect, she will give you respect. Having taken computing classes outside of ACO, at least she treats you as an intelligent person. Go to office hours. Read the book. PREPARE.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: I paid for an in-person live lecture class. What I received was a "lecture" on zoom. I use quotes around lecture because she didn't lecture. Dietrich made prerecorded videos to play during class. That meant we couldn't ask question because the class wasn't live and she wasn't responsive during the video. Dietrich is the worst professor at ASU.

Dr. Dietrich means well, but she is not a good professor. I was on the edge about changing my major, but she is what ultimately made me decide to change. The quizzes are all fill in the blank/ free response. The assignments have little to no direction are are super vague. Do not take this class if there are other options available.

If you are searching for a professor you don't have much of an option. Be prepared to do homework that is 5x more difficult than what you go over in class. Quizzes are free response and there isn't much mercy to her grading. Replit is also the horrible place she decided to code in.

Please don't take this if other options are available. Way overly complicated assignments for just being introduced to new languages. Crams as much material as possible within as little amount of time possible. She does not consider student opinions and is not helpful when asking questions. Assignments are terribly vague with little instruction.
I gave Dr. Dietrich the tag of "AMAZING LECTURES" because you'll be amazed how god awful she is at lecturing. She laughs at students questions and replies with "well if you read the instructions".

Prof. Dietrich is rude and doesn't respect her students. She laughs at students when they ask questions then wonders why no one asks questions. She is not good at lecturing and just reads someone else's slides. Boring to listen to and wastes time on pointless matters in class. Do not take her class unless you like torturing yourself.
I had to double my anti-depressant prescription just to handle this class.

Professor Dietrich rambles in class about nonsense. She has no idea what she is talking about and reads the power points word for word. She assigns work for the sake of keeping students busy and not for learning purposes.

She shouldn't be teaching. Her powerpoints are terrible and she just reads off it. She wrote the book and it's just as bad as her in class lecture. She isn't clear about what she wants on assignments and doesn't admit when she is wrong. She is condescending and will laugh at you, if she thinks and answer is obvious. Only difficult because of her.

She is disrespectful as she replies to legitimate questions with "it's just logic and common sense" or "it's in the notes" We, the students, are here asking questions because we have not yet built these logical skills yet. Logic is a skill like math. You also know she not a good teacher when a substitute explained her material better than she did.

Dr. Dietrich is clearly knowledgable, however, she does not convey the material well to students. She is sometimes condescending to students asking questions and does a poor job of fostering a learning environment. She did not handle the COVID-19 pandemic well.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

review_text: considering changing my major or leaving ASU because of this professor. Ask any type of question that she doesn't deem "high level" enough, and you will be belittled and get a sarcastic answer. midterm and final make up over 50% of the grade. The Class averaged D on major assignments and tests. That right there says everything. Take ACO 201 first.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

review_text: Extremely condescending and rude to students. I had this professor before and thought she was okay, now I understand student's hesitance to ask questions because her responses are always sarcastic. She's knowledgeable, but it's overshadowed by her awful attitude. She posts good material on the class website, so you're better off referencing that.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Plan on putting everything else aside in your life to pass class. Long projects given one day, due a week later yet the entire week is spent covering new material that has nothing to do with the current project. The material isn't too hard but the pace of this class is insane. Fail one project or test and good luck even getting a C. avoid her.

She's very knowledgeable in the coding field. Being an intro level class, I feel she expects way too much out of us. I have a background with networking and this class is difficult for me. DO NOT MISS CLASS. if you do you will fall behind!

Her Midterm and Final make up 55 percent of the grade. She likes to give "in class "homework (which you end up doing outside of class either way because of difficulty and time) and out of class programming assignments (Which makes it hard to focus on just the programming assignment). All work is expected to be done alone, so no group assignments.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Tough grader

review_text: Its great that she knows her subject. Berry knowledgeable her field. The thing is, she cant deliver. This is a ACO101 class yet she talks to us like as if coding is already in our blood at what not. Tone it down. She isnt very helpful. When you ask for help, shes very harsh and she points out your mistake as if you know how to fix it.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Dr. Dietrich is very accessible outside of class, however that may not be necessary since she does such a great job in the classroom. Having trouble on an in-class exercise? She will take the time to walk you through the problem. The material in the course is super useful too.

A medium level difficulty, the class requires lots of your time outside the classroom. Homework will help you with exams, make sure to do well on it. Dr. D is a good teacher, but she is a tough grader. Points are taken for every error. Know your material, the class grade is never curved. Your grade might not reflect the amount of work you put in.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Dr. Dietrich knows the material. She understand what she teaches, but the problem lies in her inability to convey the material to people who know little of the material she teaches. In addition, she fails to offer good assistance to those in need, merely offering a blanket statement, such as "We already covered this" or "It's in the book."
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: If you're looking to show up and pass, she isn't for you. She expects you to put in the time outside of class as well as use her office hours and the teaching assistant to learn the material.  Attendance is mandatory. I found her to be fair. Her rubrics or guides before each assignments were not the clearest but she took and answered questions in c

She doesn't teach and leaves the class to learn on their own. By any means, avoid taking this class and having to deal with her horrible "teaching." Her grading sucks and isn't fair whatsoever. Everything is done online and testsquizzes are taken on paper which doesn't help. Reading powerpoints is her way of teaching. I can do that on my own.
Always went to class. Always

Took a database design class a while back (don't remember the class code).  I thought it was great; she was a good lecturer, used a textbook she had authored herself and knew the material very well.  I learned a lot that I'm using in the real world.
No Comments

Does not help much. All she does is take the powerpoint slides provided by the textbook's publisher and read them in class that is all she does. You can bearly understand what the heck is going on with the programing assignments. I basically had to research the materials by myself. Whenever you go for help she would tell you to read the book!
it looks like the ratings have been removed. why?
Unfortunately this professor gets an f for teaching and will run off many students who would otherwise be interested in programming.
No Comments
ONE OF THE WORST PROFESSORS THAT I HAVE EVER COME ACROSS HE DOES NOT HELP ANY STUDENT  HE NEVER RESPONDS TO EMAIL WORST GRADING SHAMELESS PROFESSOR

One of my favorite profs. I came in with no knowledge of databases and was able to get caught up with the class rather easy. First lecture that has been worth showing up to since Covid. Homework assignments can be rather tricky but not the end of the world. Midterm and Final didn't contain trick questions and were graded fairly.

Amazing professor. CSE 512 is a light course. If you already have a knowledge of databases, its gonna be easy. Good assignments and good lecture notes. A few tricky coding assignments, but overall managable. Mid-exam was descriptive and final was MCQ. Would recommend if you are looking for a great in-person lecture experience and an easy A

He makes the class pretty easy and clear to understand, but he feels like he's unprepared for most of his lectures. If you ask him a question he'll often misinterpret it or brush it off. Grading is lenient and the homework is interesting, but they're also pretty cookie cutter so you'll only touch the surface level of some of the tech.

Prof very nice and caring, hates cheaters tho. Lectures not that complicated, but a lot to cram for midterm/final via basically rote learning, very doable to do good on the exams. Projects some were really easy, some decently hard with only a week to do, the hard one will suck but its downhill from there. One of the easiest 500's with a great prof!
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Caring

review_text: He's a really nice guy but just didn't feel like he was prepared. He cares that everyone gets a good grade, but it also feels like you're not learning anything too complicated. The content overlaps a lot with CSE412 (except for the basic SQL part) with some extra information sprinkled in for the distributed part. It's not rigorous at all.

I took his class in Spring 2021 during covid when fully online. He tried to really help at the end of the semester to make it less stressful for us. He recorded lectures but did post them a week later and wasn't available out of class much. I learned a lot in this class and thought it was great as an elective. I would recommend!

He's a nice guy, just disorganized. During Covid he uploaded lectures days after. Students had to keep emailing TAs to clarify vague assignment directions, and shared their responses on Discord. The one time I emailed the professor, my email tracker said he read it 2 hours later, but I never got a response; not sure if that's a norm for him.

I don't know why this professor is ranked so highly! The class is the easiest I have ever taken. Professor teaches basic things that even an undergrad student will know! Cakewalk, easy to get A, A+. Please don't take it as it is not a graduate-level course.

Tries to scare you off with the rules in his first class. You would learn a lot from this class. This professor is an ocean of knowledge. Not the toughest grading but you can lose points if you are not careful with the specific grading criteria and plagiarism.
Very interactive class and communicated a lot with the class. Cared how we were doing and is flexible.

Very interactive classes and interesting lectures with real life examples. clear grading. Subject is very useful for jobs and gives hands on experience on latest things. Only thing i did not like was the assignments and projects are same from several semesters and they need to be changed
Great course. You will learn a lot of details about engineering distributed data systems. The only drawback is that exams can be difficult.
Enjoyed the class. The assignments and group project are really great and gives you hands-on experience. The midterm was difficult but the grading was not too tough. Highly recommended.

He is a pretty chill professor. The lectures are okay. Definitely need to attend and participate in all lectures to do well on the tests. This time, midterms and finals were both difficult. Assignments are doable and really awesome. The project also gives you hands-on experience in Apache Spark and AWS.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

review_text: This class is a must if you plan to pursue a career in data science. Dr. Sarwat is definitely an expert in database systems and that shows in the way he runs discussions in class. The class is never boring since the prof shows how to do database stuff using the PostgreSQL psql interface. The final exam was a bit difficult but not too bad.

Good prof. lectures are clear and in-class activities are engaging. There are 5 prog. assignments and they are not difficult. The prof focus on the practice side of DDS, which is very useful when you apply for jobs. Having a 3 hrs class on a Friday afternoon is not cool though.

He's a cool guy, his lectures are pretty fun, and the homework was clearly laid out. But he created a lot of unnecessary stress with his extra grading policies. For example, it said on the syllabus that if you scored less than 50% on the final you would fail the class. He later relaxed the policies so they were just scare tactics I guess.

This class seems difficult at the beginning but the prof makes it easy. Lecturing style is good and easy to follow. HWs require programming with Python, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB. The project gave us hands-on experience with Hadoop and Spark. The pace of the class can sometimes be slow but thats because Prof tries to clear all doubts in class.
nice, knowledgeable and good lecturer. makes the content very practical. HWs and project are easy but exams are difficult. Attending the class is necessary to get a good participation grade.

Great professor - very nice and smart. The fun part though is when he runs interative lab sessions using PostgreSQL in class. He shows us step-by-step how to write SQL commands. I wish more professors would do the same.

He's very nice, knowledgable, and smart, but that doesn't mean he's a good teacher. His homework and project assignments are poorly explained, and grading is tough. He also has difficulty explaining new concepts: he skips through lectures because he thinks everyone is as quick to learn as he is. Interesting dude, but not for a graded course.

Could have been a great teacher but already 2 weeks in to the Semester, already can't get a reply from him through email after emailing him twice. He's too busy with his twitter presence to even check his emails. Apart from that could have been a good teacher.

Friendly professor. Good lectures. Cheat sheet is allowed on exams, though won't help much. Assignments were very easy, and they are 25% of your grade. Project is okay (code on Scala, so you have to learn a new language), although 10 pages report at the end is not cool.

Among the best classes I took at ASU. The material is very practical and the professor shows enthusiasm teaching it. A lot of interactive demos using PostgreSQL in class, which helps us get a better feel of the abstract concepts. Assignment are easy but the exams are a bit challenging. Overall, a very good class!

ASU should have more professors like Dr. Sarwat. He knows the material very well and shows interest in teaching it. He also cares a lot about the success of his students. The lectures are great and he incorporates a lot of activities / discussions. For improvement, I think the group project could have been a bit more challenging.

A good professor. He knows what he's teaching and will make sure to answer all questions in class. The best thing about this class is the project in which we learn how to run database queries in Spark. Highly recommended!

Sarwat is a good prof. Lots of assignments and project work but you will learn a lot. Midterm was a bit difficult but the final was easy. You will need to attend the lecture or otherwise you may not be able to follow. I would have liked it if the prof covered topics related to big data.

Seemed like he was going to be fine for the first half of the semester. Towards the end he got extremely disorganized and was very unclear when teaching more complicated material. Never gave feedback for why things were wrong. Had multiple assignments where the solutions were incorrect. Spent 30% of lecture talking about random stuff. Bad Professor

Dr. Sarwat is one of the best computer science professors at ASU. He has extreme knowledge on the database and SQL subject and is very patient answering questions during class. He sometimes relates the abstract concepts to current tech news, which makes it easy for us to understand. I totally recommend the class - a lot to learn!
Course Project and assignments are nice. Classes are just ok, nothing extra-ordinary.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: Clear lectures. Useful assignments. Project is highly related to cutting edge computing technology.

Prof. Sarwat is an excellent professor. His course schedule is very clear and there will not be many changes. You will learn many new and hot technologies as a part of his course that add value  to your resume. The course load is very balanced. I would definitely recommend his course.  Attendance and class participation is important.

Course projects and assignments related to latest technologies such as Apache Spark, Hadoop, MongoDB etc. which is good to learn and put on resume. There is only one midterm exam which is easy and no finals.

not so hard grading but definitely not a good prof. u will learn nothing from himattending class. attendance is kind of mandatory since he takes meaningless quizzes now and then. however, projectsassignments  r good but group sizes r 2 much. Avoid if u want to learn!!

The best professor I have seen ever. He had immense knowledge in his field. He scares u on the first day saying subject is very tough. but he makes course a free walk. We had group assignments too. One thing is sure, course project will be your top listing in linkedin profile. Professor makes you work on latest technologies for project .
THE WORST PROFESSOR AT ASU. AVOID HIM.

If you choose your group wisely then this class is not that hard. Almost fifty percentage of your grade depends on the group project. there are 2 exams which are the hardest part of this class. But even if you get a "C" for the exams, you still have chances to get "A" at the end if your team gets full credit for the project! Choose members wisely!

Get ready to watch 2 hours of lectures every week while having do to a large group project, homework assignments and readings in between. Very arrogant and vague when answering any question. Follow to the rubric for everything to the T or he'll dock points for no reason. Seems like he dislikes his job and giving students good grades...

Avoid him if you can. Very irritable, aggressive, and very mean. Spread out many vague instruction on the very first day, but set up the deadline on first  day. You miss it, he kick you out of class. Terrible and careless for students.
rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: He said more than once that he would like to date with Charlotte McKinney in front of students. Heyyyyyy McKinney was born in 1993, but how old is Anderson?? I just felt so disgusting every time he said that.
rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: It is a NIGHTMARE to take his class. The two exams are nerve-wracking because you could get 0 points if you don't follow even one of his instructions. For the project, you can get 0 points if one of the requirement is not met. I have never seen a professor like him during my 4 years ASU experience. Thanks god he will not teach CIS 425 next semester
Thank god the school is not letting him stay anymore!! I have never ever seen a terrible and careless professor like him.
Terrible instructor

Nothing taught in this class, its all copying straight from the instructors code. Students end up being confused when it comes to the Final (take home final) or the Project, which are the only actual assignments that needs to be done at home. Good luck!

There are no assignments or any homework. Almost all the work is done in class, 60% of the grade is all based on copying code from the instructor. Thats how all the classes are. No outside sources, slides or a book to study/read from. Instructor doesn't take the time to explain anything but asks the class to copy after him..

Only cares for the students he "likes". If you were out sick or missed a few days, you will automatically fail.. All the assignments are copied from the instructor in class, not much to work on or to even learn.

Just show up to class and copy all his code from the projector (he codes all the assignments for you in class). Not sure why they are called "assignments" when they are all in class and copied. No effort needed what so ever as the assignment total up 60% of the grade.

No grading rubric, he grades every student different although they are all copying the same code. The whole structure of the class lacks critical thinking and is made not to teach but to have the student copy (60% of the grade are copied from the instructor). Moreover, the instructor tends to not care or reply to emails accordingly.

He fails any student that he doesn't like. All the assignments are "copied" from him in class. He codes everything on the projector while the students copy his code. The class doesn't teach anything. Then the project and final are to be done alone. Go figure. .

All he teaches is copying his code on class and tests are based on how well you copy them. Deadlines are set for nothing. Remember: matter not to submit anything at all if your codes are not working than submit anything before the "HARD" deadline he said- you will not be rewarded for knowing what deadline means. There is no points for your efforts.

He has no material on Blackboard, gives 100/100 to students who technically copy his in-class works, has no objective grading rubric. He grades your work upon what he sees, you got points deduction if your assignment doesn't look like his - dont expect he will look into your code. He always can find some new issues on hw you request for regrade.

One of the better professors I've had at ASU. Tests are tough and tricky but Dennis will work with you to get you where you need to be in the class if you do your part. There was not much out of class work, which is always a plus for me.

Class technically isn't mandatory but all the assignments are done in class so you might as well just go. He will give you 0/100 points on assignments without hesitation if there are mistakes. Offers regrades sometimes if you care enough. He is helpful if you show him you care and ask for help on your mistakes. Only exam is the final - 3 parts.

Most terrible teacher in W.P. Carey ever. Do not help student or even discriminate students. He is too proud of himself and never trying to understand what students' needs. He even do not give make-up exam when student is in terrible sickness.

He is a horrible instructor. There are no PowerPoint slides and there are no books. There is nothing posted on Blackboard. How are we supposed to learn anything? Why is he allowed to teach like that? One of the worst instructors ever. I didn't learn anything in his class ... awful instructor
Best prof ever, take a summer class with him it would be easier.
This class would be better if the University required some basic HTML and CSS before taking it. JS wasn't inline anymore, unfortunately PHP was barely covered. Pay attention and test your work.

Anderson is one of the best professors I've ever had. If you're nice, ask questions, and show you're not a slacker, he's more than willing to help you be very successful. The final is hard so make sure you're doing well on the homework and put a lot of effort into the project. Show up to class- he's really funny though, so it's never boring.

As a class, the curriculum has way too many topics.  As a Teacher, Anderson is outdated on his skills (inline JS, no MVC, hates any IDE).  As a person, he is very rude. He might seem nice and funny until he gets made. He told a student to "leave class and take it agin next semester" because he downloaded a pdf instead of previewing it.

Takes the time to teach you what you need to know not just to pass a test, but to apply the materials. Great sense of humor and takes materials that are not exciting and makes them fun. He really takes an interest in the success of his students and their success. Pleasure taking class with him.

Great teacher, and definitely knows what he's talking about. Quite easy if you attend class and take notes. Wasn't really too strict on due dates as long as it was in by the end of the semester. Don't slack off and wait until the last minute though.

Anderson is a great guy! The class can be hard to keep up with some days, but he is incredibly helpful via email and will not let you get screwed over. Funny guy. You can definitely learn in the class if you are interested and willing to play around with the code during the project or on your own. It's a lot of material, but a good basic intro.

There are a TON of little things that can go wrong in this class that will make a BIG difference in your grade, Professor Anderson is extremely understanding with this and is incredibly fair. Did multiple regrades on my assignments after showing him a very simple mistake was made. You get out of this class whatever you put in.

Pros: Doing all of the assignments in class so you only have to go to class, copy the code that he gave and get 60%. Cons: does not focus on teaching students the coding/web designing materials. He only cares about whether you do what he said or what he wanted. Final test is hard but he might curve.

Tries to fit HTML, CSS, JS, and PHP in one class without divulging in depth to either of the technologies. The class consists of writing code that's on the projector. The design is outdated, uses inline javascript, and outdated practices. Not to mention, the final code is un-maintainable and un-reusable. Doesn't feel like a 400 level class at all.

Easy class but I didn't learn anything. You watch him code and repeat what he types. Not his fault, the CIS department should really split this class in half with the amount of content that needs to be learned. Do yourself a favor and utilize free web development tools(w3school or codecademy) to actually learn while taking this class.

no textbook. There are 8 assignments that you write together as a class, then you have to use those assignments to write your own website, totally on your own. The final is impossible to finish but he curves it. He is a fun guy and is very helpful and responsive to emails and questions. He will also regrade assignments if he missed something.

Awesome teacher!! He tells the class exactly what you have to do for each project.  If you miss or talk in class you will miss a lot and others won't share any info! Easy "A" if you do the projects right after lecture.  No book required.  All from the lecture.  This class took only 40% of my school time. Strongly recommend.

Worst teacher ever. Read from notes entire time.  Never showed them to students.  He just sat there and read of the notes!  Other teachers were amazed that he doesn't allow his students to see the power point slides.  Even the department head thought it was a bad move.  If he doesn't like you personally, you might be screwed.  Not professional
he is not fun at all and he talks so fast. He is not willing to help you either. And he is racial discrimination

His 310 class is pretty straightforward, and the homework wasn't difficult since it's just practice for what you learned in the lecture. There were two Midterms, and the lower grade gets dropped. The final wasn't too difficult as well. The two projects could be done on your own or with a partner. Review the slides and you'll do fine.

Professor Yang is a great choice for 310! Homework and projects are on topic while still relatively challenging. Lectures are informative although they could be a little slow at times, but there's good insight into real-world application throughout. Tests can be a little tricky; use your whole cheat sheet. TAs are also very helpful in this class!

He makes CSE 310 feel so much easier than it is. Homework is super light, with basically 2 questions per week. He's a super chill and cool guy and his lectures are good. Just make sure to study the lecture notes and do good on the tests (which have cheat sheets) and you will be completely fine.

Dr. Yang my goat idk course might be a bit different in the future sometimes his lectures are unbelievably dry (looking at u RB trees) exams are important so def study hard for those but otherwise the course isn't too hard, i think it's a good balance of relatively easy but very thought-provoking assignments

Not much to say about 310, but prof YZ makes it fairly easy for everyone. Two midterms but the one with higher grade is taken, and then a final. Also two projects. Don't know about future classes, but it was pretty chill. He's also chill and his lectures (neither recitation) are required. Just don't forget to study.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

review_text: Professor makes the lectures enjoyable and fun to attend. Explains concepts very well. There were 8 homework assignments Homework is a little tough at times but not too difficult, everything you do seems to be related to the material you learn and doing the homework does help on exams. We got one cheat sheet on the midterm and 2 on the final.

Perception In Robotics. The professor is a nice person and cares about students. However, the lectures were bad. Apart from camera geometry nothing else was explained well. We chose between 6 projects based on his research. We did not use any course material in our project but it was a very interesting one! We did not learn/use ROS.

Perception In Robotics. This course was mainly graded by the final project. The professor was a very nice person, projects are guided by a PhD students. He is very knowledgeable and explains concepts multiple times if needed.Grades depend on the final presentation scored by PhDs, so even if you work really hard, you might get an A instead of an A+.
Hilarious professor, because he's young and gets along with the students. His teaching is very engaging. Projects make you do a lot but are a lot of fun.

He is a really good professor and cares about what he does. This was my favorite class this semester. The textbook is really helpful for keeping up with the content and understanding it better. I do recommend starting on the coding assignments earlier rather than later as they can be challenging. He really cares about the students succeeding.

Professor Yang made data structures and algorithm fun to learn. Rather than making assignments complicated, he made them useful. His lecture slides were also very useful. Great lecturer and keeps the class engaged. Definitely the best CS professor I have had at ASU and strongly recommend him. He made the class very interesting and worth your time.

The professor is fine. Honestly without his TAs this would have been a nightmare. He is kind of lazy, pulling projects/homeworks fron other teaching which can be frustrating. Lectures are normally pretty boring, with tiny writing in a huge class so if you are in the back dont bother going. He can be funny and is a good guy. Grading is fair.

The professor is chill. Both the professor and the TA were understanding and gave some leeway with assignment deadlines in this uncertain time of COVID. There was one take home midterm and the rest of the grade depended on various assignments. For the assignments, most of the things had to be searched on Google and then implemented for results.

In a sea of ASU CS professors who you can hardly understand, with cryptic schedules, assignments, and boring exams, Prof. Yang was so helpful and his lectures were engaging and easy to understand. I never felt like I was being "gotcha'd" like I have had before in other CS classes. This guy is one of the few good professors in CS at ASU.

Yang is a good professor if you can get pass him being a bit disorganized. He posts all lectures online but sometimes after class. He is a bit hard to understand but he clearly shows that he cares for the students and our learning. Good professor, I would definitely take him again.

Amazing professor, spends a good amount of time with each concept and explains them thoroughly in each lecture. Good slides and summaries too, people underestimate that! This has probably been the most difficult CSE class I've taken at ASU thus far, but I feel like I've learned the most.
Makes sure to simplify complicated concepts
shallow content, doesn't give much details,have to google eveything for project

Does a solid job of making complicated and dry topics understandable and interesting. Homework is challenging, but prepares you well for the exams which are of reasonable difficulty. Overall his grading criteria is more than fair, and if you put in the work you will definitely obtain the requisite knowledge to handle other CS classes.

I had a great experience in this class during which my team tried to solve a problem pertaining to athletics. To be honest, I never had any prior experience with python or ROS before attending this class. The class was so interesting that my team started to understand and implement the algorithms (ex: corner detection, optical flow, etc.)

Professor Blanco is one of the best professors I have taken as a CSE student at ASU. He is very passionate about natural language processing and it showed in his lectures. He would stay almost every day after class (along with office hours and Slack) and help students with any of their problems with the coding.

Professor Blanco has provided one of the best lecture experiences especially for a 400 level class. He is active by email or Slack for any inquiries about the class. For someone who knew nothing about NLP before the class, he provided a great introduction to the topic and balanced the workload fairly throughout the semester.

Easily one of the best professors at ASU. Projects/Homework is 70% of the grade; two exams worth 10% each; 5 quizzes worth 2% each and extra credit is offered on top of this incredibly generous grading scheme.  Extremely kind, loves his work. Would definitely take him again.

Best professor I've had at ASU. The course content itself is somewhat interesting, but his lecture makes it more interesting. Quizzes are relevant to exams and assignments are straightforward. It's NLP so there is more than a normal amount of reading for a CSE course, but I cannot stress how much I appreciate the effort he puts into the class.

Great intro to deep learning. No exams and only graded on homework and a project. Was very helpful and accessible in class and through Slack. Clearly wants his students to succeed. Both went into the theory and the practical usage of the topic. Used GitHub for homework submissions primarily which was amazing. One of the best CS professors at ASU.
Quizzes and exams are fair. HWs are a bit much sometimes. Lectures are alright.

He's a good lecturer overall, and his lectures/notes are very comprehensive. His CS courses do tend to be mathematically heavy, so I think will appeal more to people who are interested in the underlying logic/mathematics aspects of CS than concrete programming. Very nice and accessible-- recommend, especially for math majors with a CS interest.

Worst professor I've ever had. Although a nice person, his lectures are horrible, hard to follow, and boring. There is a long homework assignment every week, and they're very hard and he's not helpful. Only 3 midterms that are pretty difficult. Don't take if you don't have to!

Lectures were terrible and hard to follow. Homework grading was so slow. Easier to use online lecture notes than follow in class. Test aren't terrible especially if you've done well on HW. Just complete everything and you can get a decent grade. If you can get another professor though, go for them.

Don't really understand the hate for Prof. Straubing. I think that he is a very nice and engaging professor. Lectures are easy to follow if you just pay attention. Tests can be unpredictable and homeworks can be difficult, but he gives extra credit on some of the homeworks, so take advantage of that. Would definitely take him again.
Is very particular, and will take off points even if your code runs. Often unclear and backtracks on his own assignments. Unpredictable exam questions.

Very smart and nice professor. However, having a mathematics background maybe necessary to succeed in his logic and comp class. The textbook, which he wrote, is full of mathematics jargon that is hard to read without the appropriate background and he moves way too fast for people who does not have that background.
Take, great dude

Dr. Lan is very confusing during lectures. He doesn't give clear directions for what he wants in the lab. He wants you to learn how to "swim on your own".  However don't let this discourage you. This is still a relatively easy class because you can learn everything you need online. Search the web for what you need and you will pass this class.

Dr. Lan is a great professor. He really cares about his students, and his primary goal is that his students learn everything they can from this course. This course is a great introduction to assembly language and computer architecture. There is no homework, but there are lab assignments. The exams are challenging but fair. Exams are also curved.

Dr. Lan has a different teaching style, but he's very skilled and wants his students to learn.  Tip: If you frequently volunteer GOOD answers (and questions) in class he'll be more likely to help you directly instead of making you read your book.  Be prepared to spend lots of time in lab.  Challenging class but fun!  Highly recommended.

This was a hard class for a GE course. One midterm, one final, and a huge website project. Very time consuming class. He's not clear what he wants you to study for the exams and gives you very vague study guides. Okay class passed with a B+ because he curved. He's a very nice and helpful professor, but learning wise not go great.

He is a very nice professor. He makes it apparent that he loves his mom. Which is cute nonetheless. But nice & cute doesn't cut it when it comes to your grade and what you're getting out of this class. Very vague & confusing at times, difficult exams but curves bcos he's sort of a push over. You'll probably get an A or a B if you're computer savvy.

If you will stay in the 'Swimming Pool' long enough and come to class with GOOD questions, it will all be like a 'Mickey mouse' to you! Don't rely on lectures to teach you much, got to study on your own using textbook/internet/partner as your sources. You're on your own in the lab as well. He's pretty cool, gave us a pizza party at the end.

Amazing teacher once you get used to his style! He is absolutely brilliant and has such a deep understanding of computer architecture, but still makes students feel comfortable asking silly questions. Just be sure to be interested in the class and ready to ask all the questions you can!

He's awesome. He requires you to read the text book and gives you a lot of time to do that. And it's worth it. Get the basic ideas from the txt book and his lectures help deepen your understanding (except a few first ones). He's very helpful and the labs are chances to apply what you learn. His teaching style is like slow but stable.
Only one quiz early in the semester. No midterms, no exams, no systematic lab assignments. No practice with problems similar to final exam. Professor missed about 1/4 of classes and 1/2 of labs.
Dr. Lan is awesome! He's extremely patient and very helpful. He makes you work hard for a good grade, but it's worth it. Stay in the swimming pool as long as you can!
the prof is a nice guy, but never really answers questions directly. relies on the book to teach you, but does not expect you to understand it fully, since he knows its tedious. hard class.

He was very nice & if you ever have questions, he`s glad to help. I didn`t use a textbook the whole semester. Very easy if  you take notes. Quizzes every week, but usually one question. Attendance is mandatory. Labs were quick & easy. I APPROVE :)

he is a cool teacher not hard. just show up to class and take him seriously. people never paid attention in his class because they didnt take him serious. but over all he is very nice and test are easy, just pay attention.

This class was much easier than I expected, I do not believe the major is going to be as easy as this class was. There are quizzes almost every week, but they are 1 question. The are like "What does CPU stand for?". Labs were all no longer than 10 min except when the last 2 days of the Web project.

This class is challenging, and it is clear that Prof. Lan wants it that way.  He, however, is an excellent instructor and is very enthusiastic about teaching.  This is not an exciting subject (x86 Assembly), but I very much enjoyed this class, mainly because of Dr. Lan.

Worst Professor! has okayish subject knowledge but doesn't have the ability to teach/explain a concept. teaches very outdated material. had to take it as 201 was made a core course, but bad experience.  Her explanation doesn't follow a flow. it's all over the place. the thick accent doesn't help.

This is a graduate level course, so you cannot expect an easy A. She is very clear about what she expects in hw assignments and projects. However this class is difficult, so you must attend and pay attention through out the semester. Projects are not that difficult if you pay attention. Final exam was directly from her lectures, hw and projects.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

review_text: Tips for passing: Watch the SML videos on youtube by Dan Grossman, watch them on the first day if you can. You will still have to learn about currying but it makes everything much easier. Also take "exact copy" notes because she gives "whiteboard source code" for the hard SML project in class. Good luck
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Caring

review_text: Honestly she seems to be a cold person....I got advising from her at one point and she isn't very warm or compassionate on people's needs......I honestly didn't wanna show my face back at the office again because of her agressiveness Avoid her at all costs....I am glad she isn't the chair anymore

This class ceases being useful after the first part about about lambda calculus. Lectures are confusing, assignment directions are either missing or ambiguous, and the class is not useful in any way. If another teacher were to teach this class it may be salvageable.

She assumes you know everything. Sometimes seems very partial towards the students which she likes. She is knowledgeable but can't teach. Her teaching lacks clarity. Being the chair, she does forcibly makes students take up courses. She gives students pre-requisites. Can give a very low grade as there is no visibility on her grading scheme.

She really cares about her students, but is not an effective teacher. Expect to go to her office hours a lot (hope you don't work for a living!), but unless you press her, she will explain things in exactly the same words that she used in class. Group projects okay, but memorize what you did, because it will be most of the final.
Very caring and helpful professor. She lectures are very organized and well prepared.

She assumes you know everything. She is knowledgeable but cant teach. She makes sure that you loose interest in her class from day 1. I dont understand why this course is a core course as the course material is useless and out dated. Besides 201, she was our grad coordinator and made our life hell by not giving classes which we wanted.
Incredibly nice and helpful teacher. She loves her work, and it shows.

'Futile' is the word which best describes her teaching. Only she knows what she is talking about. Very bad method of teaching, referring only to her notes. The major problem is her accent - it is difficult to understand. She's knowledgeable, no doubt. But not a good teacher. 10/100 for teaching.
I agree very tought to follow. Her notes is all about key words. Very hard for the exams. My GPA will definately go down having this course...unfortunately it is core course.

the professor (cui zhang) is very difficult to follow. she knows what she is speaking. I have 100% of students with negative feedbad. the course is a core couse other wise i would have never taken it.

He is never motivated to teach class. Most students will attend the first half and then disappear because his lectures are poorly drawn and explained . Notes offer no help, reading the book barely helps, coding assignments are in verilog which is not taught in previous courses. If you want to learn , do not take this class.

Worst professor I have ever got in my life,I had to take this course as it was mandatory too, otherwise would have never done such a mistake. Not understood a word from his lectures even while giving my 100%.
He is a funny guy. poor lectures. Waste attending his class. His slides are old fashioned.
Doesn't teach what is important , can't understand most of it doesn't make sense. The answers to his questions are not consistent.

umm, he's a nice guy and all, but the content makes zero sense. He doesnt prepare you at all for the homework that he hands out, and expects you to read a boring book to learn it. Besides that, his programming assignments are challenging and he isnt of much help. Like i said, nice guy and all, but resented the class.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

review_text: The school didn't prepare me to know about verilog but he gives out labs in verilog and expects us to do it easily. AND OMG, his notes, his handwriting, its all super messy! The lecture was so boring that I didn't even go to class anymore. Most of the homework answers can be found online. Also, his study guides is BS!! TAKE HIM, RISK YOUR GPA!!!!!!
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

review_text: Lecture is BORING! His notes are sooo messy, i cannot understand any of it. Midterm is super HARD! Feel free to take him if u want to risk ur GPA. I guess the only good thing about him is that he curves the grades accordingly.
provost magee does not respect student workers
Provost Magee hates student workers

The flipped classroom method, while not my favorite, is well executed. Despite struggling in this class, I ended up doing pretty well due to him dropping a few of your worst grades. This class is A LOT of work (a project every week) but you are given the tools needed. Don't let the first class scare you!!
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: Lots of support (TA hours everyday and extended ones on weekends). Responses to questions usually within 30 minutes. The class is extremely work heavy. You need a lot of hours outside of class. I often spend all of Friday and Saturday finishing up work (in addition to doing work throughout the week). Prof. Magee is great for CS120, though.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

review_text: Professor Magee is great! I had him for CS120, and while I found the class very difficult, the course is so well organized that the frustration came from the challenge of the work, not ambiguous instructions/grading. I felt that he clearly communicated the core concepts of the class and had an effective support structure set up to ensure success.

I enjoyed Prof Magee for Intro to CS. He is very friendly and approachable and is willing to help you when you get stuck. He also explains concepts well. This class does have a lot of daily work - there are pre-class activities 2x a week, biweekly quizzes, and weekly projects (writing computer programs). There is no textbook, which saves $
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: He is an annoying professor. He does not say about participation and attendance yet grades professionalism in the end. He says on the test to be concise in answers yet gives points based on the amount of information student provided. He does not give out much homework for the most of the semester yet throw all remaining homework for the last 2 week

I took his class during the summer. Lecture notes are clear and posted online. He is very helpful. Since it's a summer session, we have a tight schedule and homework due every week. But it's ok if you start early. He gave four quizzes and a final. You can either take four quizzes, or three highest quiz and a final. WOULD TAKEN AGAIN.

This is my favorite professor in Clark's CS department. He is very straight forward, helpful, nice, but still challenging. He does cram a lot of information into a course and goes pretty fast, but if you spend time you will learn a lot. He is very helpful when you ask, and his assignments really relate to the classwork.
I've have Magee for many classes. He is finally turning around and becoming a great professor!

I love Dr. Kokensparger, he's super great, the class is really easy A. its very clear what you have to do, and he's a very, very lenient grader. He's pretty funny for an older guy, but this class is a breeze, and he makes it even better.

Dr. Kokensparger is such a kind, caring teacher who genuinely cares about his students more than himself. The class is pretty boring and uninteresting and is honestly difficult (the tests are very hard) but he is a great teacher who truly does want us to succeed.

He is overall a really nice professor, although is class is very lecture heavy and can be a bit boring. There is one weekly quiz out of 10, and another that is just counted for participation. Projects and Labs are fairly easy and you get a full week to complete them. Tests can be very daunting since there is so much information to learn.

He's very knowledgable and you can tell he cares about his students. This course isn't easy, but not super hard. As long as you put in the work and study hard on exams you will be fine. Attendance is mandatory as you get points every single day if you are there. There is mainly coding projects and conceptual tests.

Overall was a great guy. He's very supportive and open to help anybody who needs it. You also have TA's in this course so they are there to help as well. Really funny guy and as long as you do the readings, you'll do fine on the weekly reviews and the coding.
Super kind professor. Class is not easy but not super hard. Put in the work and you will do well. TAs are available and can help you get through the coding. Dr.K loves what he does and you can tell!

Great professor but the class is so boring. You can tell he is passionate about the material but it can be hard to follow along. You definitely have to put effort into the labs and tests, but if you put in the work you should do fine. Def the best prof to take 121 with

Dr. K is so sweet! The tests were scary because there is so much material to study, but if you try you'll do fine. I didn't pay attention in class because he just review the readings, but the readings explain it better. However, he was still a great teacher! He tells funny stories sometimes.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

review_text: Professor Kokensparger was by far my favorite professor I had this semester. He truly cares about each and every student. We use the book quite often in this course- but you can get by without reading the chapters. The projects can be time consuming but go to office hours for help! Tests aren't too bad and overall a surprisingly interesting class!

Very nice professor. I found the class very easy. There is a chapter of the textbook to read each week as well as a project due each week. The chapters aren't too long, and to be honest if you're computer savvy you don't really need to read the chapters. The projects can be challenging, but you have all the code you need from the slides.
He is a very good professor, but his lectures can run long. He really cares about his students.
Dr. Kokensparger is a great professor. He grades weekly projects or labs as well as a weekly quiz plus 3 tests. Super nice guy and will help you out with your code if you need.

Dr. Kokensparger is a super sweet old man who grew up in an era of rapid technological improvement and is extremely passionate about it. He loves teaching, and lectures always have that energy to them. He's somewhat hard to contact, and will often redirect you to his TA's, but he has tons of students so it makes sense.

Dr.K is so nice and really wants his students to succeed. With that being said, this class is hard if you aren't truly passionate about computer science so you have to work for your grade. For this class, I would definitely recommend Dr.K, his directions are straightforward and he drills the material into you with lectures. Super sweet guy.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Tough grader

review_text: Dr. K is very kind, and he is pretty laid back. The way that my class was set up was with two chapters of reading and a quiz each week, and there were weekly 'projects' that are more just like assignments. There are two tests and a final and occasional 'labs'. If you want to do well in the class, you have to read the textbook and study.

Worst professor I've had at Creighton. The lectures rarely relate to the assignments. They are difficult to follow and not interactive at all. He offers little to no assistance and no way to catch up if you start to get lost. I have never dreaded going to a class more. Taking him is my biggest regret as a Creighton student. Try someone else.

Such a sweet guy. Weekly quizzes that are directly from the book. Only have 2 tests and 5 homework assignments the entire year. The difficulty ramps up fast from HW2 to HW3, but it's not that bad once you get the hang of coding. Definitely recommend kokensparger if you plan to take CSC222
great teacher, lectures can be boring,but crucial to do weekly projects. TA hours are a savior

This class is really fun. For the most part you read a chapter and have a quiz on it the following monday. He goes in-depth on coding and lets you code along with him (explaining error messages too!). He has homework due every other week that isn't too hard. Overall a really nice and caring guy.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Dr. K is 1 of the best professors here at CU! He has such a great personality and really gets to know his students. Class feels very long since its from 9-10:45 and the lectures can get a little boring. Other than that I have no complaints other than he doesn't hold office hours. I recommend getting to know him outside of the classroom!

Took Dr. Kokensparger for CSC 121. Would 100% recommend him to anybody looking for an intro class in the subject. He is super nice and understanding, so it is not difficult to do well if you make an effort to do all the work.

I took CSC 121 once a week and it was long, but it was fine. He split the class up into two parts: an online quiz with a lecture and a coding lesson. There are 3 tests and I did well on them and I studied for about 3 hours before the test day. You should study the end of the chapter T/F for the test and then look over the powerpoint and the labs.

Kokensparger stepped into the class in the middle of the semester. He provided a really good pace - the example problems and homework are hard for beginners, but if you do those you should be well-prepared for the final. A patient guy who would be an excellent prof for intro CSC.
Dr. Kokensparger is super nice and you can easily get an A in his class if you put in minimal effort. His quizzes at the beginning of every class are easy and usually a group effort.
Brian (baby santa) is super nice. Class material is pretty straight forward and he keeps you updated by displaying calendar every class. Comp. science isn't the easiest thing but he's pretty lenient.

Dr. K is an awesome professor. He explains concepts really well, and is one of those teachers that is always wanting the best for his students. If you have any problems, just go talk to him! Super nice and understanding. You won't regret taking his class.

Lectures basically just clarify points from the reading. Lots of super easy labs and projects that you can finish in class. Tests can be hard, but not if you do the study guide and examples in the book. He's super helpful and understanding if you have any problems. Can be boring, but you can get an A with some effort.
Very Helpful guy. He pushes you to work hard but work will be rewarded. It is very important to pay attention in class. Sometimes tests get difficult but it all averages out in the end.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is great. Class is pretty much lecture based, but if you pay attention, then it helps a lot with the tests. The weekly lab is very do-able in the hour he gives you, and he's always willing to help. He also tends to reuse questions from previous quizes and tests on his tests/final, so be sure to reference those when studying.

Solid class but it's easy to lose track of what's going on. Lecture heavy about some tough material. Weekly online labs that are easy enough. 2 test's and a final. Tests are okay. Super easy coding on it compared to the labs and 75% is history. Pay attention in the lectures and it's an easy A
Engaging during lectures, good teacher, just the right amount of work. Not a boring computer science class at all. Take him!!

Dr. Al-Khateeb was amazing! Prepares you very well for tests, and the tests themselves are straightforward-- no trick questions whatsoever. Homework and labs can be a bit tedious, but they serve a purpose! He is always down to help his students succeed. Overall, he is a great professor, and I would highly recommend him for CSC 121.

Very approachable and caring guy. He just wants you to show up (gives extra credit for attendance last couple weeks). Lectures are straightforward, weekly labs, 2 tests, and a final. Tests are 65% history and 35% coding, and the coding is very simple on tests. Class can be big and scary to say you're stuck, I just talked to him after class.

He made the material very clear through his lectures. Doing the weekly projects leaves you well-prepared for the tests. Very helpful with questions and confusion. Tests are not too hard and are a mix between application and content history. For being a required class, he made it interesting and for not previously having interest in this topic.
Great class. Nothing bad to say. He is a pretty good helper and just an overall good teacher. I will say that if you have a hard time with thick accents you may want to consider taking someone else.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: A great caring guy. The class was difficult for me because I am horrible with computers and the projects can be difficult. Get to class because the notes are important for tests and the online coding can be difficult but is hard to remember. Nevertheless, super understanding man and makes it clear that he cares about his students.
Al-Khateeb was a good professor and always helped students when needed. The class itself was kinda boring and hard to engage in but the textbook helps a lot.

For a class you have to take he makes it really interesting and as fun as he can. It is not the hardest course, show up and read the textbook and you will do fine. He is a super caring person and really appreciates people who attend, although attendance is not required. Highly recommend.

He is an iconic professor. I hate coding and struggled with it, but making use of his office hours helped explain the weekly homework assignments and quizzes. Tests are extremely difficult if you're not good at coding. I focused on vocab and short answers. Lectures are mostly based on reading PowerPoint slides and explaining the codes.

The sweetest professor, but this class is not easy. Don't just copy someone else's code. The tests are hard if you don't know the coding. There aren't many tests, but if you don't do well they will hurt your grade. I got A's on all projects, and ended w/ a B. Overall he is wonderful and the TA's are helpful, it's just hard if you don't care ab code
Dr. Al-Khateeb is very kind professor and a relatively easy grader. He expects you to do the work but is lenient. His teaching isn't the most engaging, but I overall enjoyed his class.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is a great professor and he really cares about his students. 221 is an intro class and he understands that not many people have had experience coding before. He is easy to talk to and wants to answer all your questions. Really nice.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is a genuinely good professor. Some of the reviews on here don't make a ton of sense; we did exercises everyday during class to learn the material. The textbook, while handy for practice, is not really needed as long as you participate. Participate in class, and ask questions when needed, and you'll do well.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Al-khateeb is probably the best teacher in explaining material, out of all the professors Ive came across, he was the most caring and helpful teacher of them all, his class is easy if you pay attention and just come to office hours when needing help, his office hours are so helpful. I suggest him to you guys!

Dr. Al-Khateeb was great this semester; I always enjoyed going to class. I felt that beyond explaining concepts well, he was a very caring & generous person. He was available outside of class, and he gives more than enough direction for any student to get an A. As long as you put in the effort to learn the concepts, this class will be a breeze.

Dr. Al-khateeb has been nothing but a great professor, honestly before taking the class I saw quiet bad reviews but I went ahead anyway and took it, it was so worth it, he is so caring and he tries his absolute best to make sure you understand the material. I totally recommend you guys taking his classes!

Lectures were just an exact repeat of the textbook. No coding practice just straight to the assignments after the book. They were graded harshly as well, he did not like you unless you were very good at the class. I know I'm being harsh, but take a different professor for more clear lectures and better work.

Dr. Al-Khateeb is honestly a great professor. He tries to get to know his students and genuinely cares. The projects are pretty easy and if you are struggling just ask for help over zoom and he basically does the whole thing for you. His accent takes a little time to get used to but he has good TAs too.

Dr Al-Khateeb is a good professor who is quite easy to access during his office hours. He tries his best to make sure that you truly understand the material. However, he is a very tough grader, so make sure that you follow all his instructions in labs and tests.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Tough grader

review_text: He's a good guy with a heavy accent. His class is pretty straightforward if you read the book. Lectures can be boring as he explains what you were already supposed to read. Easy A if you put in the effort.

Honestly, all of his classes are really easy. The other reviews were left by people that have no interest or background in CS. If you have any amount of experience or put in any amount of effort, you will get an A. If anything, he's too much of a pushover but he's incredibly kind. Lectures can be very slow and a lot of what feels like busy work
He's okay

I like Dr. Baker as a professor but man this is one of the hardest classes computer science majors have to take. If you work hard in this class, you will be rewarded. If you are coming after Fernandes, it will feel completely different, and the difficulty will increase a lot. I learned the most in this class and Dr. Baker will help you if you ask.

CSC 421: Tough professor, but you learn a lot. Definitely recommend taking her if you are actually interested in coding and want to get better. Uses a textbook, but you can usually find it online for free. Don't be scared to go to office hours. She records her lectures and posts them. Gives about 5 homework assignments with 2 weeks to complete it
Dr. Baker is a really tough professor, but if you work at learning stuff, she's really great.

Dr. Baker knows what she's doing very well. Articulating it to students that have never coded before is where she struggles sometimes. She is very flexible about office hours and is more than willing to help you if you seek it. The homework are very extensive but a lot of learning happens from them. The tests are not realistic either. Nice lady.

Very sweet and intelligent but not great at "dumbing it down" to an understandable level. Super flexible with office hours. Required exercises are tough, homework feels IMPOSSIBLE. Quizzes are probably one of the easier parts. Very nice person but I decided to drop a CSC kingfisher because of this class

Professor Baker means well. However, her ability to articulate concepts and reasoning is severely lacking. She is all over the place during lectures, constantly correcting herself and hesitantly answering questions. Most of the time, it feels as if she hasn't even learned the material herself.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

review_text: Dr. Baker's class is definitely very lecture dependent where I think it would be better if it was more project based. That being said, she is very helpful if you ask questions and easily accessible outside of class for anything to homework or even questions about scheduling classes, research, grad school, ect. Solid choice but you do need to try.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

review_text: She is smart and caring. She usually is pretty good on explaining and giving examples, but she is still pretty new and has so much room to grow. When you don't understand something, ASK. She will explain. Although she is young, I think she could become one of the best professors for the JMC department. DONT TURN THINGS IN LATE.

Professor Baker knows how to code, but that is about it. She isn't really good at explaining concepts and teaching in general. The way she lectures is like a less clear, straight reading of the textbook. Also, grading for homework doesn't make sense, as minuscule and large mistakes drop the score equally. Not a hard class if time is put in outside.

He's not a nice professor at all. He likes to have autonomy of things and makes us feel like his lab students are better than us.   Every time I needed to ask a question he would completely ignore me and give the question slot to another person.  AVOID THIS MAN! And DePaul definitely needs to fire him.
I would not recommend any one to take his class. Horrible experience.
I wouldn't recommend. He is not a good professor. He just spends class time talking about his lab. He does not teach anything and is super patronizing.

I would avoid taking his classes, he's a disorganized instructor and also likes to rant about firing students from his lab. Which is very unprofessional, and uncomfortable for the students in his class who don't work for him. He also frequently interrupts female students more than he does male students, even while presenting.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: I saw this professor berate students for minor issues like not having camera on (not just taking away points, but personally attack them for a sustained 5 minutes) also on a group presentation he spent 15 minutes demanding each person say who should be fired from the group - if someone didn't want to he just berated them until they would.

Extremely disorganized - took weeks into the quarter for him to post the assignments and then acts shocked when students do not know when anything is due. Never responds to emails, he is also my advisor and I have never gotten ahold of him. Class content was so below par, not worth it to take this course from him.
Extremely disorganized, not responsive to email, poor written/oral communication skills - generally class was not worth it due to his ambivalence

Prof. Yele keeps his lectures light and everything he teaches is something you learn when you do the actual work. He doesn't respond to most of the emails. Coursework is very easy and everyone gets an A. He is an okay professor but a great source of help for your career if you maintain a good relationship with him. Overall he's good.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Group projects--Graded by few things

review_text: i havent even taken a class with this professor but I have him as my cdm advisor and i have never been able to reach him. tried applying for the combined masters program and i was told to talk to him... guess im not getting a masters
Has the knowledge but is not co operative. Fookin nonsense.

Loved this course. His lectures are thoroughly enjoyable because he encourages participation from the students and keeps cracking jokes. Best thing is that he always explains concepts from basics and always encourages students to ask questions. Do not listen to the other people, they just dumb. Would love to take another course by the same prof.

I took his class when everything was remote because of COVID. You could tell the guy was knowledgeable about the subject, but the class was extremely un-organized and sloppy. I'm about 95% sure he was getting drunk during the lectures and he would go off on a bunch of un-related tangents instead of teaching.

Most unorganized professor I have had a DePaul. My assignments were not graded until grades were due. There is no clear expectation of what he is looking for. No feedback on the assignments. I would avoid this professor, if possible, waste of money and time.

Must be the worst professor I have ever taken. During class he would interrupt students while they are talking, did not grade any assignments even after entering final grades in the system, did not give any feedback on any assignments. He also did not respond to emails. Also waited until the day grades are due to enter the grades. Do not take him.
He won't answer your emails or be clear about assignments. He will interrupt you. Super patronizing too.
easy class but not an insightful professor.

Professor Yele is very unorganized and doesn't teach IS at all. He just reads/writes over slides. When it comes to posting the lectures on d2l he is always 2-3 days late. I didn't learn anything in this class. The assignments aren't hard but there is quite a bit of writing.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

review_text: Terrible teacher. NEVER finishes grading assignments and the term is over! I asked for feedback about my grade and he claims I didnt submit an assignment which is completely inaccurate. I provided proof of submission and he ignored it

Yele is a specific professor, he is great at having a conversation and getting some feedback for life. But as far as IS201 goes he is unorganized and seems to only care about his projects outside of class. I would take him again, even though he does not allow technology in his class which is something that I dont understand. he is great otehrwise
rating_tags: Participation matters--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Deducing that he got high 600s to low 700s on GRE so smart enough to get a PHD, NOT THAT STRONG. I just found him having these false beliefs of evolution and adaption. One comment he said was "people normally don't mix", it might be true if you're a neanderthal like himself attending really bad colleges with REALLLY bad core values.
went there as a visiting student. you get what you pay for.

Taking IS 201 with this professor and he can be hard to understand at times. Though that could just be the online recordings? Not sure. The assignments are not super hard but it is a fair amount of writing. There so far has only been 4 assignments that are somewhat lengthy but not too bad for almost being done with Quarter.

Very good teacher. He's funny, well liked by the students. I learned a lot about project management in his class. We studied both traditional Waterfall and Agile. Group project (as expected) was interesting and rewarding. Had guest speakers, all of whom were interesting.
