This professor is extremely unorganized. He barely stuck with the syllabus. He responds to emails maybe 15% of the time. Can hardly understands what he says during lecture. Does not respond when you follow up on feedback about the homework. Would not recommend him.

I have taken a lot of online classes and this was by far the worst.  No documentation stating what is required for the final project or presentation.  Hard to even understand him sometimes watching the class online.   Said there would be a midterm and seemed to forget.  Extremely dissapointed with this class I wouldn't recommend him to anyone

Ironic that he teaches a Software Project Management class because he has trouble managing this one simple class. He grades everything half-hearted and provides poor feedback. Provides no written documentation so if youre an online student you're going to struggle. I think he takes on more than he can handle.

Yele is pretty entertaining and funny. He can be unorganized at times, which results in assignments being moved. I only used the book about 3 times, seems like he forgot about it halfway through the quarter. Class was very easy, concepts aren't difficult. He can be picky with grading, and sometimes assignments weren't clear enough.

Very unorganized and never responds to emails. We did not follow the syllabus at all, leading us to skip the midterm and the final even though he said we would have them. All assignments have no written documentation so you better watch the lecture in order to get anything he is talking about. I really expected more out of this class.

Had a grader look over all assignments, which resulted in never getting a perfect score.  Had to contact him to change my grade for multiple assignments, if he replied that is.  Hard to get a hold of.  Was not in his office whenever I stopped by, and never replied to voicemail messages.  If you can avoid the class, do so.  Not worth the headache.
Nice guy and generous grader. Not clear often though.

Yele's is the man! He's funny and smart, and definitely knows how to teach. He keeps the class engaged and weighs heavily on class participation. He'll make sure you understand the concepts and enjoy it at the same time :) great professor!

Hands down the best teacher I took at DePaul. For all the unclear comments, if you listen in class he gives you what he wants in the homework.  Being a graduate level course I don't expect to have the teacher hold my hand. The class was geared as in you listen and apply to answer. Really no right or wrong if you can back up your answer.  Great Job!

This guy is a joke. Assignments were unclear. When emailing for clarification, numerous emails went without reply (probably 75-80%). The responses I did receive were short with simple "yes" and "no" answers to questions that clearly warranted explanations. One of the worst teachers I have ever experience. I learned nothing and it was horrible.
His homework was easy BUT it had errors and sometimes wasnt clear. I enjoyed him though and I would be on the fence about taking him again.
Very knowledgeable professor, but his homework assignments are very vague in what he expects.

Very knowledge able professor. Has a Ph.D. Is a funny and laid back professor. Has a bit of an accent, but is still understandable. As long as you pay attention, participate, and do your homework you'll get a good grade. He
Great Professor with some funny jokes. Teaches the class well. Offers helps as much as he can.
Great Professor. Teaches the material really well and spends extra time with the people who need more help. If you go to class and listen you will get an A.

Prof. Adelakun is friendly, laid back, and a fair man. My only complaint is not of him but of students who did not give him the respect he deserves. Many will mention his thick accent but if you actually listen rather than complain, he is still speaking English and making complete sense. Very approachable in class, helpful, and knowledgable.

Professor is unclear, thinks he's funny but not really, and will call you out if you touch your computer while he lectures.  I found this class a complete waste of time even though I love web design- he doesn't know how to teach at all.  He needs to fix his teaching methods and learn how to relate to students. group project + tests/quizzes.
Great teacher. Hard to understand him cause of his thick accent. Graded fairly but expected you to use the answers from the book. I would recommend him to my friends if they took his course.

Yele was very entertaining through out the quarter. I had a hard time with some of the stuff, but he was pretty lenient on most things. We had like 3 Sarah's or something in the class... and to make it easy he just called every single girl Sarah... it was pretty funny. Go to class.. do the little assignments.. and you'll get an A for sure.
He really knows his stuff, and is always eager to answer questions... Just make sure to ask them.
I felt like I was back in high school. All he does is repeat himself over and over and over and he spends half of the time threatening people who he sees using their computers.

I attended the Brazil Study Abroad trip last December and it was amazing.  The goal was to determine the IT readiness of Brazil via conventional and observational research.  We did site visits and sat through many presentations.  Overall, Yele is an excellent professor and he comes highly recommended.

CTI needs more professors like him.  The most challenging things about his assignments are that they require you to think.  If you're not an abstract or conceptual thinker, you won't make it in his class.  He's fair, and does an excellent job in class.  He was the first professor that didn't put me to sleep, and I thoroughly enjoy sleeping in class

He's a funny, laid-back teacher.  The material is relatively clear and he is an easy grader, but really seemed to enjoy reading our papers.  But because he has a heavy accent, I had a hard time understanding him on COL; don't take him if you can't go to class.

You should take this class it was my true first international experience.Although he was absent for the class because his wife had a baby. The class was fun and I made tons of friends. Be financial and mentally open to a new exciting experience!

Took him for IT215 as well. The best prof at depaul. made me realize i should be in this field. for a lazy ass like me, its an easy A. and he is the COOLEST teacher at depaul--not even. he is def. ur pal. way too much fun. he drags class though. I am taking him for remainin IS courses as well as doing Indpndnt Research on IT with him! :)
great teacher; fun and helpful

the easiest teacher at De Paul...if you cannot take an A in this class you shouldnt be at depaul.he is so cool and laid back teacher,,you wouldnt even feel he is teacher you would feel like he is your friend...I would say take the class with him even if you do not need it...very nice guy.
excellent teacher and all around great guy.  genuinely cares about you benefiting from the class. fun n interesting class. will take again for IS 372.  take him as a teacher, u wont be let down

By far one of the best teachers at DePaul.  Yele will become your friend and teach you what you reall y need to know in the business world, if you allow him to.  The assignments are easy, but Yele isn't there to make it hard, he is there to try to help you, listen to him and you can't go wrong!

Very lecture heavy, but class is relatively easy if you pay attention. There are quizzes every week that are pretty easy, but programming projects every week can be a bit tough at times. Overall not too difficult of a class. Midterm and Final are both oral 1 on 1

Best professor I have had in a while here... Very kind professor who will make any accommodations needed while still preparing you more than any other professor you hear about. In comparison to students I've met in 242 who did NOT have Besana, he has me much better prepared than it seems others do.

Overall really good prof! He is pretty funny in class even when the lectures can be dry, very open to and looks for class participation when he asks questions. Mini code modules, a coding assignment and lab per week. The midterm is one on one in his office, but not difficult if you prepare yourself in class. Final is on pencil/paper.

This teacher is very difficult, but is very nice and helps you with the coding. Some of the homework is weekly coding labs and a programming project due every week. But, the midterm was a 1 on 1 live coding based on the previous lectures and will ask you how/why the code works. The final is a 3 hr pencil paper no computer/no notes final.
I wouldn't have passed the class if it weren't for him, he actually genuinely cares about his students and he goes Indepth with his explanations and doesn't assume you already know how to code.

Don't let the difficulty fool you, he is difficult. Take the sacrifice and know it'll be tough! You'll get through it! Go to tutoring if you struggle, and don't be afraid to talk with him. You'll be a much better programmer. Even though I did have quite a panic attack from the final took, the class was highly informative. Can't say enough about em.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Incredible professor for CSC students that need to take an introductory class. Yes... it will be difficult as programming projects require you to think outside of the box, however, you will have an edge on other students once you understand the concepts he is teaching.

He really seems like a nice guy, but the weeding out is real for him. People accused of cheating made me have a nervous breakdown mid quarter because I assumed he'd come after me unjustly. Codelabs are vauge, quizzes are all over the place. I did learn a lot from this class, but my mental health certainly suffered through it.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: I took him because I saw he had amazing reviews, and he was nice at first but he was way too much. He acts like we're senior engineers at Google or something and expected us to know answers with no time to think. Also if you cheat do not take him. He will make you cry and rethink your life.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: Does a great job at breaking down concepts and the weekly programming projects are challenging (but not impossible) and require you to piece together new material + reinforce old material. You will have to work hard and genuinely put in effort; definitely a "weed out" professor but you'll learn a lot. Super accessible outside of class for help.

This course was terrible and nothing like the reviews this professor received. There was a lab and project due weekly with smaller assignments also due. Taking this online was terrible especially because his lecture videos are were old and not up to date. DO NOT CHEAT IF YOU TAKE HIM. He thought I cheated and would not let me explain myself.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

review_text: You want to become a coder? Is this your first coding class? DO NOT TAKE HIM. An intro class made me feel like I needed to know 5 years of coding to excel in this class. You have labs, projects, and code labs due weekly. Estimated time to complete his weekly assignments took nearly 18 hours a week. DO NOT TAKE HIM, TAKE MSI. Vidoes are not helpful

Good guy, he will make you laugh in class, and cry and curse your existence while you work on the assignment. Overall, I got a lot of value for the money, I felt that the difficulty of each assignment was slightly beyond my immediate reach. Take him if you want to understand and learn.
The homework for the class was tough but I wasn't a CSC major. He was always available to see during office hours. He sometimes called on people during class so come prepared.

Fantastic intro to programming class. He made things clear to understand and his lectures were informative and interesting. He provides timely feedback and is willing to look over your code. Awesome class.

Loved him! He does a great job explaining topics in layman's terms -- his lectures are interactive and informative.  He is also super funny.  The homework assignments will push you, but aren't unreasonable, and he is always willing to point you in the right direction if you show him your code.
Not helpful, shows powerpoint slides all class and works in groups but not very useful and is strict on neatness of hw/ will take points off for smudges.

class is semi-interesting and fun, but the homework is tedious, the projects are difficult and the directions for them suck, and he grades really hard on tests. this class will keep you extremely busy!

Great professor but anal about neatness/work! Easy material but not an easy A. Quizzes every week which arnt hard; lab projects every 3 weeks; Midterm and Final, which is cumulative. If you have the time for this class, I would recommend it; however, if you have a tight schedule and need a lab class, this might not be the best choice.
He is the bomb!!  Very interested in his class and his students.  He really wants the students to learn.

The professor is nice but do not take this class if you have alot of other course work. There are quizzes every monday, time consuming assigments due wendsday and labs due every 3 weeks. Very anal about neatness- so again, if you will be in a hurry to get work done, DONT take this class. Not very easy. But nice guy?
Great Professor.  Very interested in the subject matter and very informative.  But his class will not be a cakewalk!
great guy, personable and always willing to help.

He was great!!! I am not a CTI major or anything close to that and I pretty much understood the class well because he was very clear and patient.  He truly helps his students understand the material because it can get confusing.  Be ready to spend A LOT of time on his homework...

Great Class, fun professor.  At first he may come off as cocky..well,come to think of it, he actually is kinda cocky. But he definitely knows what he's teaching, and makes the class as enjoyable as he can.  PLUS, he's kind of nice to look at.

Very Very helpful, clear, nice powerpoints. give extra credit, lots of work though, but worth it. Good professor ever at Depaul. One thing is, he could remember everyone's name in class within 2 weeks. Awesome professor! also, he will never give up some lower grade students. he will keep you up.

This is the first time Prof.Besana taught GPH259.  His lectures were very good, and he explained everything very well. However, the homework took an EXTREEMLY long time.  Be ready to spend 2-3 hours on homeworks and 5-6 hours on projects.  Then again, this is a lab class.  Good teacher, but lots of homework.

explore-digital divide. boring subject but Besana is fu*** awesome!! very well prepared and careful at detail. very helpful in and out of class. the class had that weird bonding thing, i still talk to people from it. class subject is a bit lame, but besana is just an overall awesome guy. my fav prof

DO NOT TAKE HIM! He sucks at teaching Java on an intermediate level. His howework assignments are very hard, and he doesnt explain all the material needed to get the homework done properly. Only 4 people passed his class out of 25 enrolled, so go figure. HE SUCKS!

Awesome! Explains things well so that you will understand. Don't listen to the post that says you will fail if you didn't take IT130. I never took an IT course in my life and I got an A in the class! You just need to enter the class with the sincere willingness to learn and study.

Great instructor.  He is very clear and you will learn a lot! Great choices for assignments and programming projects. Lectures are interesting/intense. I guarantee you won’t fall asleep! Please don’t take this class if you are not a CS student, or if you haven’t taken IT 130.  YOU WILL FAIL!

Must Take w/ Him! if you do all the work, you won't get wrong!! He is a good professor, explain things well, willing to help! Easy Grader! Fair Midterm and Final. To be honest, every java class has a lot of work. His hw were not that long! Most were short program.

Dr Besana is one of the best professors I've ever had! Explains the material in a very clear manner. Always available to answer questions and is very helpful. Grading is very fair. I highly recommend taking a course from him! Hopefully I'll be able to take a future course taught by him.

Prof. Besana is one of the best teachers at DePaul. He makes sure everyone knows what they are doing n is funny at the same time.  He is also very good on the eyes.  And he has a HOT accent to go along with it.  Definitely take a class taught by him!!

Terrific teacher.  He knows how to explain everything very effectively.  If you really care about your grade, then there is no way for you to come to class, pay attention, do the homework, and not get a B in his class.  He is the best teacher I had at DePaul.  I highly recommend him to everyone.
excellent teacher, good at what he does. makes sure everyone is on the same page at all times. i would highly recommend this teacher.
He is a very good professor. He has a clear mind and teaches very well. He is an easy grader too. You will learn a lot from him and get at least B+ even you are not very good at JAVA.
Awesome teacher, makes sure everyone is on the same page before moving on.  Very nice outside of class, if you really want to learn, take this class.
great teacher, as long as you listen to the lectues you can do well on the homework and projects. No late work though
He is a good teacher. No late work allowed though. I think he is ****.
great teacher
He makes the class entertaining and makes sure everyone knows what he is talking about at all times.  A great teacher.

Such a boring professor, basically rips lectures off straight from Apple's Developer website for tutorials. You could honestly read the tutorials and figure out what you need to do without wasting 3+ hours of your life a lecture. He's also extremely stingy when it comes to grading the final project. Would not recommend even for the SWE minor.

His lectures are painful to listen to. His English is not the best and he frequently stumbles over his words making him hard to follow. He explains iOS development like a textbook would, a bad textbook. Even at 2X speed his lectures feel slow. It's like he is talking in circles to take up time. Topics that can be explained simply in 5 min take 30

Somehow he can afford a green screen, but his microphone is terrible. He tries teaching a programming course through PowerPoints. Extremely boring PowerPoints. When he does demonstrate the code, he doesn't demonstrate it in its entirety. Homework guidelines are vague. It just doesn't seem like he puts very much effort into this course.

iOS dev class. his quizes are absolutely the worst. You really have to dive deep into his lectures and powerpoints. You get 1 try. I failed all but a couple of quizzes. The hw and final project will get you atleast a B if not B+. Hw/final proj are doable, but if you miss one line, you'll get dinged hella points. good luck

I don't understand the complains here. This professor is amazing. He spends so much effort in creating his slides and puts all the sample code online for you to download and mess around with. He explain things with extreme detail, it's impossible to finish the lecture feeling confused. HW that you can actually learn how to use Swift. BEST.

Can tell he enjoys iOS development. Lectures can be dull, but are clear and he gives you everything you need to know for assignments. Difficult to get a hold of and doesn't respond to emails or Google Group questions after first 2 weeks. Assignments/Final Project are very reasonable and he gives you plenty of time for it. Quizzes are tricky.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Not easy, not too hard. The weekly assignments are slightly more complicated versions of sample apps. The weekly quizzes are challenging (range from 7-20 questions). Not hard on grading assignments. No midterm or final. Final project involves a lot of work but it's fun. A little slow with email responses but he eventually get some back to you.
rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria

review_text: It was a capstone class and we had given a real life challenge by ALLSTATE insurance. Prof. JIA was awesome. There was nothing like assignments, exams or course. We have to submit a runnable demo every week with a weekly blog report. This class was all about project, android and IOT. He will guide you, lead you and teach you managerial skills.

Teacher is passionate about iOS development. You will make a few cool applications. Since there are many prerequisites, the class moves at a fast pace. Lectures are boring, you don't have to go. Everyone sits on their computer anyways. No textbook is nice, especially in a fast moving area like app development.

Interesting assignments, lectures can be hit or miss.  Quizzes are not reasonable and are designed to trick students rather than test them.  He has a grader who has done a poor job, both incorrect and slow grading.  His slides and sample code are helpful.  He means well and I enjoyed the class, but there are improvements to be made.
Good luck.

2nd class with him.  He's perfectly fine in weekly classes.  Good luck getting a hold of him outside of that.  I sent exactly 3 messages about serious class topics (detailed issues I was having with an app, a quiz being graded incorrectly) which he never felt the need to answer.  Paying 3K per class, I should not need to beg you to listen to me.

Really not crazy about this guy, he mostly reads slides and his lectures are extremely boring.  His tests are difficult and he doesn't put enough emphasis on that which he tests on.  Most homework assignments are not very helpful with regard to the testing material.  The textbox is nearly useless and is not an easy read either.

The final exam was a complete nightmare.  He tests on material only vaguely related to what we spent hours going over.  Teaches you one thing, then exams you on something else, and then wonders why he has to curve it because everyone failed.  I learned a decent amount from the class but the exam was COMPLETELY absurd.

Absolutely horrible. Made class extremely hard. Class averaged 60% on quizzes. It was not yet an awful class until the exam. It was almost 20 pages. There was nothing in lecture/quizzes that prepared us for many of the questions. I took tons of notes that ended up being  useless. He is average as a teacher and truly horrible as an exam maker.

This professor is organized, passionate, and cares about his students. The only downside (which could be an upside) is that he just lectures and there is little class participation. He follows the textbook to the T, but he makes things very clear. The topics that he talked about were asked on my latest interview. I would take another class with him

Data Structures. One of the worst classes I've had in my life. Lecture often didn't prepare us for the homework. The final was a disaster. No one finished it. I think uncurved, the majority of the class failed it. Wish I had saved my money and slogged through the Sedgewick book on my own, since that's what class amounted to. Nice guy, awful class.

Watch out for this professors tests.  If it is a Java course you have him for, his tests will be fully focused on Java code and none of the concepts or definitions that are being taught in the class.  Reading the book isn't going to help you, but understanding the concepts he is teaching in the lecture and applying it to advanced Java programming.
Don't like his old school teaching style. Not cooperative and you have to do the majority of the work by yourself. I can just get the book and struggle though it all by myself.

Awesome professor. Explains everything in a very simple and coherent manner. Knows what he's talking about and is very fair when it comes to grading. I can safely say I learnt quite a bit from this class and the assignments he gives prepare you for real world scenarios. Looking forward to taking his Agile Development class next semester.

Constantly extends the dates for assignments which is cool when you're 18 but when you're an adult, working full time, and taking grad classes at night, you expect a real world schedule that doesn't benefit the lazy, it's maddening. If grad school is your bridge to the professional world, his assignments bridge away.

Jia is a top notch professor at DePaul and you can tell he has a lot of passion for software engineering. He tends to side track a lot and has fairly high expectations for students to learn on their own. This class is half coding half written assignments. Barely touches the surface of unit testing. Lots of discussion on theoretical principles.
Also SE431

Great teacher. My only problem was I already knew a lot of the material--despite that he kept it interesting and made learning the stuff I didn't know fairly simple. Other students seemed to like the class as well.
friendly, smart
wonderful teacher, great communication, very thorough exams, learned a great deal.
Explains concepts very clearly

Professor Raicu is awesome, and very passionate even when teaching intro level courses. I now work in Data Science, and I still use tricks she taught years later. A lot of people didn't like her because the content was tough, but because the content was tough and I learned it well, it gave me a super strong foundation for other DS courses.

Condescending, talks down to students, reprimands, does not always treat students with respect. Too much lecturing, goes very fast. Accent can be hard to understand at times. Expects participation and will call on students regularly to test comprehension.
Great lectures, a lot of information in one class. Grades fairly. Final was everything we went over during class
The class topics were repetitive and not essential

Daniela is a touch teacher.  She knows what she's talking about, but it is a lot of information to take in and can be confusing.  In my opinion, the textbook is not very helpful.   The homeworks can take a very long time.  Just really make sure to go to class, take alot of notes, and it will help you with the homework.  Her grading is lenient.

Great teacher. Her classes are not easy, but she will make sure that you know the material and underlying theories. Pay attention, take an interest in the subject, and study hard and you should do well.

Dr. Raicu is one of the founders of Predictive Analytics at DePaul. She is very knowledgeable about the subject. Despite having lengthy lectures, they're interesting, beneficial & dense. If you're a PA student, I would highly recommend taking this course with/after taking CSC424 in order not to be confused. highly recommend taking course with her.
One of the worst classes I've had in the MSPA program. But it's required. Get ready to be confused a lot.

Daniela is a really good professor and passionate about her work. She emphasizes concepts and understanding of the material so make sure you understand why you are doing something instead of just regurgitating examples (which you shouldn't anyway). Learned so much this quarter.

Professor Raicu is passionate about this subject, unfortunately, that passion does not mean she can teach effectively. The prepared materials don't reflect the material that seems to be important to be successful in the exam.  On top of that, the only exam, the final, is 40 % of the final grade. Be very prepared. It will make or break your grade.

Wasn't what I expected - this class is mainly statistics and learning the mathematical process of data mining. She was extremely passionate about the content, had lots of examples, very willing to work with students/extend hmwk, and did lab tutorials. Midterm and final are mainly theory based! Make sure you understand concepts, partial credit given

Prof. Raicu is extremely helpful, passionate and knowlegable about her field. Fair grader, gives extra credit a lot. Doesn't take attendance. Your whole grade is based on your midterm, final and homework. No textbook. She gives students one-on-one attention during class and is very helpful. Boring lectures.

She's very intelligent and knows her field well, but is not clear in teaching less leartn individuals. For her Data Analysis II class, she used powerpoints that were a bit confusing; I found nearly all my help in the textbook even though the book itself was one of the hardest I ever used. She's very friendly and willing to help though.

She is definitely knowledgeable, but that doesn't mean she is a good teacher. It's unfortunate, because her power points are hard to follow without her direction. Secondly, the text book is probably more useful than her direction, however she doesn't use it all. Basically, I ended up teaching myself the class.

Passionate and very good at her field, encouraging students to do research with her.  Excellent slides, fair homework assignments and exams.  One of the best teachers at DePaul.  You will learn a lot in her classes.

She makes you calculate formulas by hand(without using functions on caculator) homework takes days this way. If you understand what the formula does, I do not see why you have to do it by hand. For an INTRO class Excel needs to be used more than the Advanced SAS program.
NOT helpful at all. The tests cover material the text doesn't. Neither are her powerpoints any good. The class was a waste of time.

Handwriting on whiteboard is hard to read. Needs to improve on explaining requirements to complete SAS assignments. Lecture is VERY organized. Makes time to help students. The book for the course needs to be replaced or revised.

Easy if you do the work and get lucky with a decent group for the final project. Professor repeatedly made it clear that he is very busy and the class suffers for it. Poor communication, very few assignments, and grades took forever. The prerecorded lectures and 4 hws are good, but the group project is poorly executed. Overall would not recommend.

Horrendous in terms of help and being available to his students. Never responds to emails. Lectures are common sense, redundant, and very theoretical. All coding and syntax will be learned on your own time, he teaches the most basic functions yet the homework is 20x harder. I do not understand the need for this professor nor class.

He gives unlimited attempts to pass quizzes and posts answers so it's an easy 100%. For midterm, he allowed a double sided page of notes. Midterm curved. Instead of a final, we did a group project. Overall, very fair professor. Awesome tutorials. But tough homework grader. Clear structure. You will pass if you do the work!

Dr. Brown's class is tough but fair. He has detailed modules with high quality lectures, weekly tutorials and M/C quizzes. He's always happy to help via email. There are 5 HW assignments and a final. If the subject matter is new, watch AND take notes on the modules. Start the HW ASAP, passing the class depends on its completion.

This is a fundamentals course but taught as if we already knew the information. When you reach out with questions, he tells you to talk to other students instead of helping. I spent an average of 30-40 hours on homework assignments. Half of the class failed the final, and he sent an email telling us it was not his fault people failed.

Dr. Brown was awesome. DSC 465 was fully async - and he had detailed modules set up with high quality lectures. He's always happy to help via email. There's a quiz every week, a midterm, 4 large HW assignments and a group project at the end. If you watch all the lectures, you'll be fine. Definitely start the HWs at least a week in advance.
rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: To be totally honest, I think he's a fine lecturer. The workload is insane and disrespectful to anyone with a job (most graduate students). He warns in the syllabus that this is a lot - but I don't know any grad students who have 20 extra hours for one class. He even says don't take another class with this. Most of us don't have a say in that.

Not a very good professor. Disorganized lecturer, slow responses for online students, and an inconsistent grader (never knew what I got wrong on exams which make up most of the grade). He definitely favored in class students. Would not recommend. Go with someone who knows their stuff.

Dr. Brown is a great choice for 465 due to his expertise in the field. Concepts are fairly straight forward and grading is fair. There are four homework assignments, a midterm, and group final project - which all make for a very manageable workload for a graduate level course. He's very accessible and always willing to help.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Group projects

review_text: Professor knows the topic very well but the "labs" were not really labs and we were told a few times to just read online how to graph on our own time. Slides only included graphs and barely any text so studying for a midterm was very impractical. Prepare to spend a lot of time working on the homework assignments.

He is disorganized at best. The homework never accurately reflected what was gone over in class. I wish we had gone through more material because the first 5 weeks was insanely slow. I was happy that R and SPSS were used concurrently but he could have taken more time to learn R himself.

when he showed us how to make a dendrogram that superimposes boxes that show how many clusters were made in R...i was floored. in a good way. He's either a really good R programmer (which I suspect) or he's done heavy preparation in making the coding portions of the class really good and useful.

I'm taking this course in tandem with CSC 424 (in R) and I really like how it fill in some of the holes from the other course. This quarter, Eli is also showing us how to do things in R, which is REALLY NICE! So right now, I like learning R from two angles. The homework is manageable and the lectures are good.

I took IS 467 with this professor. From my understanding it was his first time teaching this class, he did OK. There was too much repetition on some topics and too much time spent 'advertising' other classes. Approachable if you have questions, but a bit confusing in explanations. Lectures were behind and we did not get through all material.

Gopal's a good professor, but he gets a bad rep because Discrete II is a HARD class. He knows it's a tough course, so he adjusted the grade scale so each letter is worth 10 points less (D=50-59, C=60-69, etc.), and gives partial credit for multipart test questions, which saved my grade for the course.

Mr. Gopal is a good professor with great intelligence. His only limitation as a professor is he lacks patience at times for his students. The material is challenging and a lot to remember though if you do not get too comfortable and lazy then you can pass the class. Had a C- middle of the semester ended with a B. Recommend staying consistent.

Amazing teacher with an excellent grasp of what he teaches. Funny guy, too. Hard material != a bad professor, so a lot of y'all need to quit whining. If you study and practice regularly, as well as make a good cheatsheet, you'll be fine. The only actually hard topic in this class is induction and it gets easier as you go on. No required homework.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: The issue with Gopal can be summed up into one sentence: He has the curse of knowledge. He is BRILLIANT, but he lectures like students have the same degree of understanding and experience as he does. He will do some things super fast and will do things in his head with vague explanation.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He understands the subject well but doesn't teach it well. I was often left confused on the subject. Tests are 65% of the grade so if you fail one, you're in for a very tough semester. Even though I wish I could grade him lower he is understanding of outside circumstances that caused me to miss an exam. I know other professors would not excuse me.

He is undoubtedly brilliant but expects his students to match high levels of understanding.This results in a noticeable cycle of frustration between him and his students. Ensure you have a light semester when taking this course, as it demands a tremendous time commitment, 1/2 class dropped or failed. It becomes easier as time goes on so persevere.

Exams are 65% of grade. 2nd exam was online w/ answers being an int; all or nothing. 8 q's, miss one, that's a B. The average for this exam was 35.5% This is a required course, Gopal is the only prof teaching it as of recent year and failing half. Geoloc-based attendance app. If called on and not present, absent for the day and 2 or 3 more days
rating_tags: Participation matters--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: This class might've been the entire cause of my stress this semester. Extremely difficult class with tests being 65% of your grade(!!). Class averages? Out the window. Almost always failing. He knows what he's talking about but makes things extremely confusing.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He legit doesn't know how to teach. No study guide, no practice questions, no examples no nothing. Very tough grader and doesn't want to acknowledge his mistakes even if its so evident. Gopal made me cry in class for FORCING me to answer a question I did not know the answer to because I have ADHD and learn slower than he teaches.

He knows his stuff, but he has a tendency to over-explain it and confuse you rather than educate. He will lead you on thinking you got something right, only to say it's wrong and waste your time. rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Im normally an A/B student. The class average is like a 65! It's so bad that his syllabus has a special rule: 61 = C. 85 = A. Sure I'm passing, but I feel very discouraged not understanding half of my exams. Needs more homework with detailed questions, or real exam study guides. Normally he just lists the chapters.

Dr. Gopal is one of the smartest professors in the department. He does a very good job at explaining things and if you put in the time to learn the topics on the study guide, you will pass and do well. Very lenient grading scale where 85+ is an A but that is because the class is very difficult.

Very hard to grasp anything said or understand how (or if) its applicable. He gives impossible tests that make up 65% of your total grade. Average of our first exam was a 39 but obviously that's because we aren't trying or applying ourselves and not at all because Gopal is an ineffective teacher who doesn't prepare his students for success. AVOID!
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: This class is very difficult regardless if you put in time or not, it requires outside supplementation for sure. Gopal was the worst teacher I've ever had. Tests counted for 65% of your overall grade, and each question was 6-8 question of long proofs and equations. He's also confusing in lectures, and unhelpful in office hours. Almost everyone in the class failed. I will never take a class from him again.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He doesn't explain lessons well at all. It's all powerpoints. Over half the class failed the first exam. The tests and hw are confusing to understand because directions are unclear. Gopal is a great prof. Had him in person for 2400 and online for 3584 and he does a great job explaining the material. Not too much homework either. As a bonus, his grading scale is very lenient. 85+ is an A!

WOW!! He's not very interesting, and has a bad accent... Homework was neverending, and the test are difficult... If you don't mind doing a lot of studying, its cool.  Otherwise beware.
very hard to understand

disregard last evaluation...Gopal is a cool guy, hes laid back. Jokes around in his india style accent.  Although some assignments are hard, got the computer lab in austin to get help.  He's better than any computer science teacher. I reccomend highly.
wow interesting if you can understand the guy, he doesnt help you with much, and if you go to see him, he just runs you around in circles, all I can say is couse shopper beware

A fantastic teacher for a complicated class.  The material is not easy but Gopal is very good at explaining it in such a way to help you understand.  This class is a pleasant challenge to those who may consider majoring in CS.  He will prepare you for future programming assignments.

Be prepared to spend the majority of your time in class not learning, but hearing him toot his own Softwar Engineering program's horn.

He wasn't the best with presenting info, but he would definitely work with you if you went to him and asked for it.  Very good to deal with outside of class.    We referred to him as the Lost New Kid because he always stood in the front of the class with his hands on his hips with a big smile.  I kept expecting him to break out in song and dance.
No Comments
He may know the subject but he does not know how to present it to a class. It would be better if he would do more examples in class.

lots of people don't like him. i think most of these people have never worked in a cs job before. a lot of the stuff he teaches you seems stupid ie doing documentation, but chances are you'll be doing A LOT of that in the real world.
Smart but lacks any relevant knowledge.  Very unpersonable.  Short tempered and can be very tough to deal with.
Nice guy, but has a very free form style and it is hard to figure out what he expects from you. Make sure he knows who you are and that he likes you and you should do well in his class.

Dr Gudivada is a great profess in charge of teaching a rather difficult and mathematically loaded subject. Watch the lectures, take notes, do the practice problems and homework you'll be fine. He'll work with you if you're having trouble understanding. I took a point off because I do think the math could be taught in a more abstract manner.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

review_text: Easy Tests and Assignments. Need to put an extra effort for programming assignments. Easy to Score A grade.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

review_text: amazing lectures, can score well if you understand the basics and follow the assignments.

I have taken Information retrieval class, his programming assignments needs a lot of work but worth the time. His written assignments are quite straight forward. overall I am satisfied with the course
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Inspirational

review_text: he teaches very well and the assignments are very good. very flexible, attendance not required, can score well if you listen to his lectures

Took Information Retrieval course this semester which was pretty interesting and lot of new things to learn. Teaches Really good and even his assignments are easy to score a good grade. If you are looking for A grade, then make sure you take his courses whenever offered.

I took Machine Learning last semester and that was a fun class. I learnt alot from his class and also from his assignments. Gives sufficient time for assignments and postpones the deadlines if needed. Teaches really good and gives good grades.
explains well and gives good grades
he teaches well, his home works are useful to get a clear idea on the topics present in the course. attendance is not mandatory. he is caring and flexible for students.
rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: I love Dr. Herndon. He is one of the best professors you will ever have. He is easily available outside of class, he is constantly looking for feedback during his classes, and trying to improve his teaching. He is one of the most inspiring professors I've ever had. His classes are difficult but we are CS majors, so it makes sense.
This class has a final project instead of the final exam and a lot of practice activities, but you need to pay attention and do everything on time.
Lots of hands on homework and a final project, no midterm or final. Very fun class. 10/10 would take Dr. Herndon again.
Took this professor twice, really enjoyed his classes and felt level of difficulty was perfect.

For all the effort he puts into teaching, this should be an online class. In person he explains nothing, just has in class exercises that last a few minutes and expects you to know the language he just told you about but didn't teach. In this class you learn about multiple languages but he gives zero instruction on how to run them yourself.

Not good at explaining new concepts, explains in very confusing ways. Briefly skims over any subject, then is confused why you don't know much about it. Tests are weighted so heavily that other assignments barely matter, and if you fail either the midterm or the final, it's over.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Tough grader

review_text: Professor Herndon is very dedicated to his students & research! He gives straight forward lectures and always clear explanations with the opportunity for questions. If you study and participate you will do well.

Herndon will hit you with assignments from other professor without giving the assignments they had leading up to it. He also went over a programming language very briefly and then made over half the final exam be questions about that language specifically. He expects you to understand without properly teaching the material
Great lectures that helps you understand computers much better.
Awesome Lectures that helped me learn a lot for my major

The professor is easygoing and his classes are well paced. The material isn't difficult to learn, but he also includes real-world examples to help better understand the topics. I definitely would take him again for any class that I can.

Best teacher I've had in school period. Enthusiastic, loves his job, and helpful. Not only that, but his teaching style genuinely helped me understand the material and even after a year of being in his class I can still remember more than half the concepts that he went over, which is not something you can say for a lot of teachers.

He ends class early, but if you want to better understand what he teaches, at the end of every class, he offers a worksheet that he goes over. Plus, he provides tests from previous semesters (with and without answers) to help you study for upcoming tests. The only issue is that it can be hard to follow along when he lectures with slideshows.

Mr. Tarnoff is easily one of the most intelligent people I have ever met, and a fantastic teacher. That being said, his class is not easy. If you're not taking notes you're going to fail. He has previous exams available to study from so you know what's going to be on your exams. Lectures are engaging, and he makes a point to include students.
Always had amazing lectures with plenty of outside sources to study with.

Tarnoff is an amazing professor. His lectures are interactive and fun. The topics are difficult but he makes them easier to understand with his lectures. Tarnoff teaches a variety of classes, but 1900 and 2150 shows me that he is someone you absolutely want to take
rating_tags: Participation matters--Amazing lectures --Graded by few things

review_text: Pretty much all bases are covered when it comes to how you may learn best and what he offers. This class has a reputation for having boring subject matter, but I actually liked it a lot and thought it was fun. The tests can be tough, though.
Gives very helpful practice tests and makes the subject material very clear. He's also super nice and cares about the material he teaches.

Instead of lectures, Tarnoff has you watch his YouTube videos he has created of the lectures. Turns hard concepts into easy to understand visuals. Take this professor if you have a choice because he cares a ton about teaching you the material.

Tarnoff is a super cool guy. We were required to watch his lessons on his YouTube channel at our own pace followed by quizzes and tests. The material we covered was usually very hard concepts to understand, but he explains everything very well. He was always very helpful and always had a positive attitude.

Tarnoff is probably one of my favorite Professors I've encountered at ETSU. The topic he teaches can be dull at times, but he makes it interesting. There's ton's of real-world examples he has on D2L. There is no reason to fail an exam, he uploads the previous exams to D2L.

He genuinely cares for his students and for what he is teaching. He could definitely have a way better/higher paying job in the field, but clearly loves teaching. He is a very nice and sometimes humorous professor. He gives copies of previous exams so you know exactly what to expect. This class is easy to pass if you show up and actually study.

Great prof, cares a lot about students, and gives solid lectures. Be forewarned that his test can be brutal, but he balances it with the test not being worth as much as most other classes. Do the labs and homework and you'll be fine.

Mr. Tarnoff is my favorite professor that I have had at ETSU so far! I feel like, since 2150 sort of follows 1900, if I would've had him for 1900 then that class would have been 100 times easier. He provides ample materials for studying for exams, and has the best and most descriptive lectures you could ask for!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

review_text: One of the best professors I have ever had! I highly recommend taking his classes when they are an option.  I had him for computer org and I don't think many people could make such a boring topic as interesting as he did.  He feels bad because he thinks he is boring you, but if you really pay attention I promise you will love his classes!!

A lot of students like Tarnoff. He is very relate-able and you can tell he works hard to present himself that way. Teaching is great, tests are fine. Not too streesful but not a breeze either. His class inspired me to pursue some electric circuit building. Would recommend.
Took him for Computer Organization. Very knowledgeable, very funny and makes learning the material a breeze. He wrote the textbook for the class but gives you the PDF for free. Would take again.
Tarnoff is the man. His server side course is not easy but you will learn the material if you want to make an A. He gives little extra credit, but if you work hard you will do well.

Tarnoff is a great professor and person.  I took him for multiple classes throughout my stay at ETSU.  His iOS class set me on a professional career path.  He was also a fantastic advisor.  I would recommend taking as many of his classes as you can.  He is not a classically trained programmer and honestly, it's a nice change.

Probably the best teacher in the department. Very helpful, tries to bring a bit of humor to otherwise dry material. Willing to take time out of class to explain complex concepts 5 different ways if necessary. If you get the chance to take a Tarnoff class, do it.
Great professor! He is very helpful and has an interesting personality, which helps keep me interested even when the lectures are not interesting.

Take him for every class that you can, he is the best in the department. Explains things 10 different ways so everyone understands, study guides are last semesters actual test (and yours are very similar.
Hands down best teacher on campus. Excellent sense of humor, does a great job covering some complex, boring material. Stops class to address questions. If you have the chance to take him do it!

Great teacher! Usually keeps things pretty interesting in class. Doesn't take attendance, but I would recommend going anyways. There are very few assignments throughout the semester.. tests are easy if you look over the old sample tests he posts!
Likes to have fun in class. Learn a lot without too much work. Best part is he post's old tests online with answers for you to study. Highly recommended!

I enjoy his class. Tests are easy if you study the provided study guides (ie. previous years tests). Quizzes are sometimes tricky but are rather straight-forward. He makes jokes about computers to keep the material interesting. Great guy!

He is an absolutely stellar teacher.  Very clear about what he expects and doesn't mind taking time out of class to help with work.  Reasonable and keeps lectures at least somewhat interesting with stories.  Took him for 1250 and 2150 and he is my favorite CSCI professor.
David Tarnoff is a great teacher.  Some of the material covered in Comp. Org. is a bit dry, but he makes it interesting.  I really recommend taking this professor for any class he is teaching.

Fantastic professor.  Has a lot of experience in the hardware field, and it shows.  Really knowledgeable and yet relates extremely well to students.  Interesting even when teaching extremely dry material.  Self-admitted obsession with NAND gates not a detriment.  Wish he taught more courses so I could take him every semester.

David "NAND gate" Tarnoff is awesome teacher. Although I found the subject interesting to begin with, he makes the subject as interesting as possible to the rest of the class. He integrates real world applications into his teaching. Looking over the old tests was helpful, so make sure and do that. Quite humorous too.
Mr Tarnoff is great his classes are useally very enjoyable. Never thought binary would be so easy.

Great teacher! Probably the best in the department. He's easy to understand, interesting, and always willing to help students. His website is a great reference even after you've finished his class. He provides you with everything you need to succeed.
His teaching methods are very straight-forward and comes right out of the book that he published for the class. His humor is always nice to have when its early in the morning.

Attendance is recommended because of the subject, but not a chore since he is committed to making the class as interesting as possible.  Pay attention, ask questions, (he is always available)and you should do well in any of his classes
Great teacher!  He is always there to help.

This guy is amazing. He understands lecture is boring and he does everything to make it enjoyable. Plus his illustrations are spot on and his teaching style is top notch.  He cares more about your comprehension of the subject than your test scores. So less tests and more learning. Seriously... take this guy for everything you can.

Tarnoff is GREAT!  Some of the material covered in his 2150 class can be confusing, almost arcane. However, he explains it all very well.  He is helpful and understanding, as well as funny and entertaining!
I have this teacher right now and I think that he is the best teacher I have so far. Very funny and explains everything to the last detail. I hope that I can have him for my other CSCI classes.
Take this professor, you won't regret it.

Tarnoff is one of the best teachers in the CS dept. He explains everything very thouroghly and makes sure every students gets the concepts before moving on. I wish he taught more classes so I could avoid the other ****'s that should bow to David's feet!

Both classes cover hard material, however, he does a great job of explaining everything. He reviews the material often and ALWAYS stops for questions. He also has a good sense of humor which is hard to come by in a computer science teacher. Great guy I would def. recommend bc he makes it as interesting as possible.

David Tarnoff is the man!  Clearly explains concepts and goes through numerous examples.  Does, in fact, include humor in lectures.  If he didn't they could get boring.  I gave easiness a 4.  His exams for comp org are not easy easy, but not difficult either.  Just understand the concepts.
LUV HIM!

Funny guy. His lectures are pretty decent, he uses alot of jokes and humor while explaining things. Always has something funny to relate his lecture to. His tests are a bit difficult, but he is more than willing to help with anything you have trouble with.

He'll explain things a hundred different ways to make sure everyone gets it.  His lectures aren't boring b/c he uses a lot of humor while he's explaining things.  Lab assignments aren't too bad, but his tests are a little more difficult than they should be.
Teaches EVERYTHING on a very low level.  Understand NAND gates and FLIP-FLOPS, or you will be in trouble in ALL of his classes (even programming classes).

Tarnoff has about 10 different ways/analogies he uses to explain anything, so if you don't get it the first go 'round, he'll find a way to help you understand. Great personality, fun to joke around with. Makes a rather bland subject interesting.
Hes great!I think he is the most organized and the most smartest of all teachers in the computer science department. He know it all!!!, just be astowned at the amount of material on his website.
I thoroughly enjoyed being in his class. Very nice person, knows his stuff and always willing to help. Good sense of humor.
knows his stuff inside and out.  very willing to help out
No Comments
friendly, willing to help students out, makes and hands out great notes, no surprises on tests. Makes hard to understand concepts relatively easy to learn.

By far the hardest class I have taken throughout my academic career. 5 exams needing perfect syntax, labs that must be checked off by Roach, lectures that will put you to sleep, and questions asked are responded with "it's in the notes." If you are a robot and have a perfect memory, this class will be easy. For everyone else, not so much.

Most difficult and unenjoyable class by far I've taken here, but if you put a lot of time in you'll pass. Research your other courses and don't take this in conjunction with anything else difficult or you will be miserable. He can seem really condescending when giving you answers to things you get wrong but he's not out to get you or fail anyone.

Dr. Roach isn't a bad professor, but there are some things missing. I thought the tests were overly hard compared to the lectures, and we weren't given much to study off of. The biggest issue is that it feels condescending at times when you ask for help. If you work hard, it's not the end of the world. But you really have to work HARD.

not really interested if you learn or not, doesn't really understand how to teach but he is a good instructor tho,  very good at just making instructions. Students would have do this typically more on your own, he gives help but he isn't welcoming

A challenging class that requires critical thinking and time devotion, but will make you a better programmer at the end. Do the labs to completion every week & save them for reference. Use lecture/lab slides to get almost everything needed to do new labs/projects. Study for tests based on given outlines. Start on projects as soon as assigned.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

review_text: He's tough but you learn a lot. Had him for CSCI3110.

Had this teacher for multiple classes. Verdict: AVOID AT ALL COSTS!!! 100% HONESTY, he is one of the hardest teachers to please. Grading is WAY TOO CRITICAL, no feedback for assignments on what was wrong or why, has THE WORST late assignment policy(30%day), & lectures were USELESS for his UNNECESSARILY HARD assignments. Would not recommend EVER!!

With little knowledge of PHP coming in, I was at a loss for the first half of the semester. Learning hard code construct is to be done on your own time, and you'll be looked at sort of like an idiot if you ask for help often enough. Labs weren't graded, but many tests and projects throughout. Really, he's okay, minus the syntax issue.

I went to his office several times.  He never once helped me and instead answered my direct questions with a question.  He WILL not help you with homework or labs at all.  Be prepared for a terrible class.
He's okay.  I think he's interested in me learning the subject.

he is nice as a person.but he shouldnt teach.i dont recommend this proffesor if you really want to learn something but if you care only about grade then you have to put on your test paper or in your project absolutely whatever he told in the class.your idea and way wont give you good grade.he is so far from real life software developing(i took 1250

No focused on lecture,he lost point of lecture and spread the lecture ,doesnt follow a way to teach.he mixes the lectures.sometimes he wastes the time and talking about previous easy lectures appereantly he comes unprepared.lectures and quizes not too bad but he always find a reason to not to give you the point.when you ask he even cant explain why

I really like Prof. Roach.  He is from Jamaica, and has a noticeable accent, but not something that is hard to overcome.  I found the class very easy, but part of it is probably my past programming experience.  Tests and quizzes aren't incredibly difficult.  I would recommend him.

Nice guy, but tends to go through the material too quickly.  The projects are extremely detailed requiring information we have not yet covered.  Lenient grading scale is a plus though.  I would really only recommend this prof. if you have prior programming knowledge.

Had no trouble understanding his accent. But his programming projects were ridiculous. Took way too much time; spare time that a full-time student shouldn't have anyway. Projects often needed stuff that we hadn't even covered in class. Often singled students out and displayed their mistakes. Lenient grading, though.

He's from Jamaica so there is a small language barrier at the beginning. Quickly overcome that. He jumps around on hard topics and reiterates the easy topics. Avoid this prof if you actually want to learn the material. Take him if you want an easy grade.
Took Mr. Roach for the game programming class. He's a nice guy, knows the material, and is willing to help (and NOT hard to understand).
Once you get used to his accent, you don't even notice it.
He clearly knows and loves the subject, and does a very good job of conveying his knowledge to others. Thorough and informative, even if his tests are a bit inordinately long.
Very helpful professor, takes the time to make sure students understand the material

Notes are clear and to the point.  Very knoweldgeable and conveys points effectively even with a strong accent :p  Only con is that the programming tests were all written and no lab, but that is probably a departmental issue.

Very effective teaching method.  Tests are extremely vague and unclear.  Midterm consisted of creating unspecified Rose diagrams and a req analysis. Final had 55 points on two questions...  Didn't know them, got screwed.

I heard he was a pretty good undergraduate professor. Mr. Roach is a HORRIBLE graduate professor. Has no knowledge of the subject and is very disorganized. A complete waste of time and money.  Very disappointed
A little hard to understand, but a nice person and willing to help
OK, If you can understand his slur of english it might be an easier class.
Explains concepts. Prepared for class.
Very helpful. Classes are very well prepared. Engages students.
Really loved this class!
Great Course.  Has high expectations, but I got a great job based on the things I learned in this class!

I was dreading having to take this class, but it was actually pretty easy!  Not a bad teacher at all; lectures are usually pretty boring but the tests come primarily from the powerpoints. Use good grammar and spelling and the assignments won't be a problem!
Great Teacher, this is not just an easy A however he is great. Better than most teachers.

Very interesting class, though it was also very intense. 3.5 week schedule with four 5 page papers, one 15 page research paper. Dr. Pittarese's standards are very high for papers so be prepared to spend time editing and reediting your papers. Dr. Pittarese is tough, but fair. Overall good teacher.
Very informative class. Be prepared to exhibit a professional attitude in this class.

I really enjoyed his educational style, and he is very clear about what he expects and what you need to do.  Keep track of his labs and study for the quizzes, as they are the harder parts of the class as compared to the tests.  Make sure to do your best on the project as well.  Highly recommended as a CSCI elective.
My favorite class so far at ETSU. Fun simulation. Practical for future experience. Helped me a lot in job interviews. But yeah, I agree with the last reviewer, if you're not in class you do miss out.

CSCI 3720 was an absolute waste of time, he teaches you nothing, it's a data entry class. If you miss one day of class and he has a quiz on the day you miss 10% of your grade, if you walk in after he hands out quizzes again you miss the quiz. Oh and he starts class earlier than it's supposed to. This class was a waste of my time

I would recommend Dr. Pittarese for CSCI 2910. Straight-forward in his class. If you need help he is always available. Great communication skills. I had no prior knowledge of PHP. Lectures are recorded in case you have to miss class. I agree with the two previous comments as well. A+ Teacher!!

Taking Server Side Programming with Pittarese was fairly straight forward. The previous comment holds true. Fast moving, but if you understand logic that you should have learned in 1250, you will be alright.
Simple class. No textbook used. Instead you use php.net documentation. Moves pretty fast but easy to understand if you have your CS logic down. Quiz at beginning of every lab.

Informative, fun, and challenging. Lectures give all information needed to do well on tests. Labs are very extensive (and may often turn into homework) but are great practice exercises and good reference for projects. Prof provides clear expectations for what should be done on tests/projects. Class requires decent understanding of SQL & ERD.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

review_text: I made an account for this, so that means I care. Bennett was a god send for my grade. He is beyond helpful outside of class. Would smash 10/10

Bennett is a great dude and a great teacher. He tries to avoid powerpoints where possible, but the lectures where he does are incredibly boring and dull. He'll help you outside of class if you approach him. He has a great wit and is very understanding.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Caring

review_text: Brilliant, yet bestows his knowledge in an easy to digest manner.

Bennett knows his topics are boring and hard, and tries to not make them that way. He's definitely helpful when you have issues. but you have to ask him. He won't come to you. When he grades things he's very descriptive and points out issues/suggestions. I definitely love having him as a professor. Yes, the classes are boring, but he's a great guy.

He tries to make discrete math fun, but it just cannot be.  He is super nice and his class would be fine if it weren't math.  Some of the sections are probably hard to teach.  He will go over things as many times as you ask, but you probably still won't understand.  He is always willing to give extra help if you request it.

Dr. Choi is the best, and he truly cares about his students and provides a solid foundation in research methodology. The workload is a bit heavier than typical 300/400 level courses, but it is manageable if you show up to class and talk often with him/TA. If you put in the effort, you'll walk away with a high grade and a great project.
Dr. Choi is great if you want a rigorous but not punishing experience in research practicum. He is super personable and helpful outside of class. I can see why he is a popular professor.

Excellent professor. The best class I've taken at Emory. +AI practicum is incredible, highly reccomend.  You'll do a lot of work, but if you can do that, it's incredibly incredibly rewarding. This man pushes you in a way no other professor has done so far for me at this school. Joined his lab afterwards.   Take the class.

Dr. Choi is probably the most popular CS professor at Emory and most feedback on him are mixed, but in the end folks I know don't regret taking his course. Working hard is just his creed in life so he also pushed it upon his students, but if you reach out to him he can always help you out/give extensions.

No tests, only coding quizes and assignments. Great but difficult professor and requires a lot of work outside classes - pays off for later CS classes such as CS326, other CS253 sections w/ easier classes suffer later on

His lectures are good but he just reads off the textbook(a website he wrote). Graded by 10 quizzes and 3 hws - quizzes are not difficult but hws literally take a week to complete. Every time ppl complain abt workload he'll dismiss them by saying "welcome to my life :)" This course makes you wonder if you really wanna be a CS major.
Looks like he's been through a lot at UPenn
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He is a nice person overall, but there is no need to make a 2-level course so hard, and his TAs managed to make it even harder by their grading criteria

Decent lecturer, cares a lot, BUT the amount of work this class takes is INSANE  He asked in class if this takes up more time then every other class combined, and 3/4ths of the class raised their hands. Prepare to spend 10-15 hours a week on this class if you take it. Also, IntelleJ and the gitbook takes a hugeee learning curve. Very hard at first

Expect this class to be hard and expect to spend a lot of time on it because it is designed to be that way. Being responsible about when you start assignments and getting them done is imperative to your success in this course. Falling behind will make the class exponentially harder. I found Dr. Choi and his course to be incredibly rewarding.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lots of homework

review_text: Expects us to have a way deeper understanding than we actually would coming out of CS 171. To pass you either have to teach yourself, or already know the content for the class beforehand. Examples and textbook make no sense to a beginner.

It is understandable that he makes this course so hard because he clearly wants to increase our level, but with all his useless reading of his show-off code the entire time, this course brings only physiological aversion.

Professor Choi's lectures consist of him reading from his own GitHub book, which lacks information and clarity. The quizzes and homework are insanely difficult. His code is hard to read especially for someone who just took CS171. Overall, I think this class makes students question whether they want to do CS or not.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

review_text: His "textbook" is essentially pages of code and very short comments. The code is written to industry standards making it confusing to understand. It's not rare to spend hours understanding his code, syntax, or whatever random System methods he used before beginning an assignment. Office hours are PACKED, extra credit near end of sem though.
Prof. Choi is very passionate about his field. Love his lectures. He is also super accessible in office hours and cares about this students. Definitely my favorite professor last semester!
CS 385 - AI Research Practicum is probably the best course in the CS department! Dr. Choi is an amazing professor!

Dr. Choi proves himself that he is the hero we need. The class material is super easy and comprehendible even with a negative IQ. As a Python beginner, quizzes are generally doable and don't really require much knowledge from DSA. The group project plays a dominant part in the course, so be prepared to cram and grab some tissues if you need to cry.
Professor Choi is a very nice person outside of classroom. He really cared about students' learning. However, his assignments could be difficult and take a long time to do but not impossible.

Dr. Choi is certainly a tough grader. But apart from the fact that you might receive low grades, his class is perfect. Projects are difficult but they will get you well prepared for the coding interview. He is also very helping and cares about his students' learning. You will end up learning a lot. Not the hero we deserve but the hero we need.
He's a good teacher but the class is really hard, he teaches off a git book and he just read it in class word for word.

Not clear about group semester project and Doesn't matter if u do 100% on all the HWs, project is most important thing. HWs are very challenging, and he is available but his office hours are so full it can be difficult to get enough time with him for help. Def ask lots of questions bc his coding is pretty complicated. He's good only if u ask help.

He teaches in a way that is only understandable if you already have a strong foundation in data structures, so if CS 171 is your only prior experience with data structures this class can be challenging, but NOT impossible. Just be prepared to learn concepts by yourself by watching youtube videos, reading articles and asking questions!

His class was taught in an industry-preparation way rather than an actual theory-based way. The way he/his TAs answer the students' questions was very ambiguous and did not really help. It was really challenging to understand the algorithms with his textbook, and therefore additional web search was needed, which took extra amount of time.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Dr. Choi's class is the hardest CS class I've taken. He is really smart, and he does care a lot about how we do. That being said, I think for most people, this class is too much. The "textbook" that he uses is one that he created and to do the homework/quiz most of us find it insufficient and have to consult the web. Only take if u have extra time.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: He's rlly smart -> can be condescending; students are scared to ask questions/help. I go to office hrs a lot, found out he's a genuine guy who cares about the students learning. The TAs respond to community questions more (not good answers tbh). Quizs and hws pretty difficult/overwhelming. Recorded lectures are good. Craves student participation.

He works so hard to prepare lectures that goes over Computer Science concepts. The main complaint is homework assignments/ tests are too difficult. He's truly inspirational and get students to code. He's the best professor I've had at Emory. He gives CS students wise advice in his office hours on how to approach their career. Definitely take him!
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

review_text: Best.

One of the better professors in the dept. Problems are relevant in the CS discipline and in CS job interview questions. Wish he taught more courses and made it a bit more difficult. Homeworks are fun and lectures are concise and helpful. Take this course!
Not the hero we deserve but the hero we need
Best thing to happen to Emory CS.
Very knowledgeable in NLP. Wished that this class was more focused on machine learning. Definitely a unique elective course worth taking.

He is one of the greatest CS professor at Emory. He is brilliant and really cares of his student. He makes sure you get the grade you deserves. He is always there if you need help with course material.

Arkady is alright, but could really reduce the amount of work needed. I get it's grad level, but c'mon. Exams are a curve ball. He curves the grades and that's important.

review_text: I took Deep Reinforcement Learning in Spring of 2020 with Professor Yang. The class is challenging, but I learned a lot through the lectures, readings, and homework assignments. review_text: Worst class I ever took. She yells at people for asking questions, usually doesn't understand the questions, and assigns triple the work any reasonable cs prof should. Avoid her.

Grace is a great teacher. She cares about the students. If you have any questions she is actually more than happy to answer. However, do it in the class or office hours since she is active in research, so usually, she is busy with her research. Also, you need to be eager to learn things, not just wait for someone to put it into your mouth.

She has no ability to convey clear logical thoughts through her lecture. I would be able to learn way more if I take this course on other online learning site. She curves, meaning if you earn 90% of total grade you still could get a B. Most importantly she hates students asking questions. She literally looks down on student. Good luck with Grace!

There are a lot of salty people from COSC 150, she's much better in Big Data (her area). Projects can be tough, but she gives you starter code to help you. Exams are difficult (long and tough grader). She's not the best at understanding your questions in lecture, but contrary to other posts, she does care a lot. Lots of chances for extra credit.

Big Data is a manageable class. The key is to install an IDE for Scala and Spark programming early on, as 70% of your grade comes from projects. Lectures can get dry, but attendance is not required (unless there's a Piazza quiz, often pre-announced). Class is curved down due to grade inflation though, so do the extra credit to stay ahead.
rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--EXTRA CREDIT--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: You need to be prepared to ask her a question. This could be stressful. And she gave a lot of homework. Only take her class if you really want to learn things. I have learned a lot tho.

However, it's really hard to get your questions answered in class or office hours because she already expects her students to know what she is teaching or to figure it out -- so her class is really difficult if you are not a computer science major. She can also be insensitive sometimes.

Lack of clarity about the course content was unlike anything I've ever seen. Lectures abysmally uninformative and she did not know how to answer Q's at all. Deliberately ignored Q's on class blog leading up to the final & stormed out 10 min after the exam began leaving us alone w/ the TA b/c she was annoyed @ our confusion. Unbelievably opaque.

We've never had a test in this class (all group projects), but whenever people ask about the final she just says to go look in the textbook and that she expected you to have read the textbook. There was no required textbook, or readings. Also several times she made up mandatory deadlines the day before, so be prepared for surprise all-nighters...

Two days before the final: I think providing sample question is for high school kids. And you are not. As my students, I think high of you and I expect you to read textbook and understand the world that you live in. No sample question would help with you. It will only limit your imagination This may work with a really good professor. She is not.
