Prof. Yang is unclear and has zero ability to explain the concepts she's teaching. When people ask questions during class, she gets mad and starts making even less sense. Her grading is often arbitrary and when I try to talk to her it feels like she doesn't understand what I'm saying. Avoid her at all costs!!

I dont know if shes better with more advanced classes, but she was clearly uninterested in teaching Advanced Programming. She was completely disorganized and didnt answer questions well - more often than not she seemed offended that we werent understanding something. We have a final exam in a week, which she has not discussed at all.
I love Grace's class. I started to like machine learning and big data since her class. Tough professor with a good heart. Cute ; )
Grace is AMAZING. I've learned so much from her class. Very inspiring lecturer.

Ug, Yang isn't great. Lectures are so boring, most people don't attend. Her powerpoints are good, but she can't explain anything. Midterm/final are fair. The weekly programming projects are killer and you def. need the TA's help. I would avoid her, unless you are a comp sci genius or already know all things Big Data related, in which case easy A.

I absolutely adored this man, even if everyone in my class lowk hated him. You'll probably not have a good time with him if you aren't a CS enthusiast since he expects you to alr know stuff, and assignments genuinely get so tough during midterm era. His lectures 50/50 on actual teaching and funny stories about his life. Hes such an experience IMO.

I went into this class with my only experience being CSPC 121. He is the nicest man ever but, his lectures are all over the place. The Exams are very random with content and we never go over any syntax so learning to do the projects is very difficult. If you are a beginner AVOID THIS CLASS or you will suffer the same fate as my GPA.

Oh De Palma. What a jolly old man. The learning is secondary only to the vibes. Prepare to hear some stories about Bruce Lee and how he doesn't like Jeff Bezos. Also there is semi mandatory halftime yoga. You will be confused and you will be amused, but at the end of the day, you'll find yourself with an A. (I'm starting to sound like him)

I took CPSC351 with DePalma. He truly has no regard for anyone's energy or time, wastes class time with ridiculous stories, turns away people waiting for his office hours, is very vague on homework prompts, and still grades very harshly. What's worse is he has received feedback like this for years and hasn't changed. DON'T take him.
People be hating on depalma, but the guy is an experience. He constantly does random bits and having stretching breaks is great, wish i could take more of his classes.

DePalma is the funniest man I have ever met. He is super passionate about what he teaches but must have ADHD or something. He goes all over the place and teaches ok. Office hours are a 1-2 hour wait time. Allows you to turn projects in a second time for full credit. Will help you if you ask. Easy homeworks once a week. You'll need to self study.

I can easily say that DePalma is the most interesting man I have ever met. I love him but can not say the same about the class. He's very excited about the content but can get caught up in that and forgets that students aren't on the same skill level as him. Always go to the TA's they're awesome. Plus for taking breaks every class to stretch.

DePalma is literally insane. He is so funny but his class is so disorganized. He usually showed up a few minutes late & very flustered. He told us crazy stories about his life & then tried to teach us some. He is very smart & knows what he's talking about but its rough. I've waited for 3+ hrs at his office hours on Friday nights, TA's are the best.

De Palma didn't let us use computers in his *computer science* class so take that as you will. He gives pseudo code for almost every project though so make sure to take notes. If you have questions about an assignment go to the TAs because you will probably be waiting for at least an hour for his help. Always resubmit assignments for better grades.

One of the most intelligent and thoughtful professors here on campus. He would often go on tangents, though they were always interesting. Although the weekly homework assignments can be challenging, if you start on time and take notes in class you should be fine. Homework and note heavy. quizzes are not bad, as he always posts a study guide.

Dr. De Palma is a professor who teaches in a very engaging and thoughtful way. However, he sometimes goes off on tangents, such as spending 30 min on Dante's Inferno or why colleges shouldn't have basketball teams. Also, ALWAYS TAKE NOTES!!! You never know if he's explaining a future project, and sometimes he gives you all the code for it.

The class was very disorganized and frustrating. An example of this is that the assignments typically would build upon the previous weeks assignments. However, we would routinely not get our assignments graded for several weeks after they were turned in. Another problem was that he did not really teach us anything. I taught myself everything

Depalma was a disgrace to the university. Class was not super hard. He was lazy and did not bother to properly conceive programming assignments before assigning them. We would catch errors in his code and/or instructions and instead of taking responsibility he would blame us for not catching them sooner. Lectures are nonsensical and disorganized.
If you want to learn about anything except the course material, take this guy. I have no idea how he still has a job here.

He lectures very well as he teaches through doing projects with different concepts in them. Furthermore during COVID he did not have any tests and only weekly projects. It is recommended to see him during office hours before submitting projects because when you mess up on something for a project you will get a lot of points off.

He's a quirky guy. No tests, just projects, but he reviews content for projects several weeks before he assigns them, isn't that clear during lectures, sometimes very confusing. Be ready to wait during his office hours and stay for the whole two hours unless you get there first. Wouldn't take again, but fine class.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

review_text: You will have to do things Depalma's way if you want to get a good grade. Be ready for an unprepared and unguided lecture. His office hours are no help and he can be rude. Avoid his classes if at all possible.

Class was I switched from computer to electrical engineering to get out of it. Doesn't let you use a computer in a computer science class rants about phones and tells pointless stories most of class is constantly late but yells at you if you are. and grades unfairly and unreasonably tests are very easy though.

does not let you have your laptop out for a computer science class. yells at you if you are late. lots of random stories that are off topic. you will have to teach yourself most of the material unless you go to office hours. coding project pretty much every week. he loves to talk and he will talk the entire period.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

review_text: He doesn't teach you what you need for his projects in class so you basically have to teach yourself from the internet, his example code that doesn't always work, and long office hours with huge lines (since he doesn't teach what you need). He tried tests and some stuff on it he never taught. I will avoid him at all costs in the future.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: De Palma is a very kind, funny, and helpful professor. He cares about your progress and is willing to help you (highly recommend visiting office hours). This being said, this was a tough class. Some assignments I spent a good chunk of the week trying to understand. Be ready for a challenge.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

review_text: Great professor

This dude is kinda a nut. He hasn't gone a class all semester without telling some ridiculous story that is completely irrelevant to what we're learning. He often repeats himself and gets confused. The subject is hard enough without his teaching. Tests are easy and homework can be made up so it's not hard to do well but you have to teach yourself.

He thinks his class is very easy, but it's not.  He lets you take home tests and gives an oppurtunity to resubit homework for full credit, which is great.  Unless you go to his office hours, his teaching is hard to follow

Depalma is a great guy. Rambles quite a bit during class, and tends to make mistakes while explaining some things, but he knows his stuff and is more than willing to admit when he's wrong. Has tons of office hours (be ready to go to them), and can resubmit assignments for full credit + work in groups. Love this class.

He gives terrible directions and jumps around a lot. His projects make little sense and taking this class made me enjoy computer science less. Multiple times he referrs to CPSC 122 by "Abstract Data Structures" (which is the course title for CPSC 223) and would claim that we should already know C++, which is exactly wrong.

Depalma is one of the most entertaining teachers Ive ever had. His class is relatively easy since his homework assignments can be turned in again for full credit, and the final project was relatively breezy to pass. A very helpful guy who is passionate about what he does, but tends to ramble a lot (which can be funny and confusing at the same time)

He's a really smart professor and holds a lot of office hours to help out, but he's absolutely crazy and will get off track a lot. Sometimes it's funny, others it's just annoying. He gives 2 tests and basically writes the answers to the test on the study guide. If you can deal with his personality then he's fantastic.

This class is an experience. Depalma is a peculiar and unique individual. He always has the right intentions, but I'm not sure that he is the best teacher. It takes a while to get your questions answered, because he frequently has to work things out on his own before he can respond. Expect to spend a lot of time at his office.

Keeps class interesting through the use of life metaphors and lessons. Always willing and eager to help his students. Very passionate about computer science and life. He improves as the semester goes on. And remember there is NO free lunch. Code till' we die!

Dr. DePalma is definitely a very smart professor, but he severely lacks in his ability to exchange new concepts to students. He very often jumps directly into advanced theory while assigning next to impossible homework questions. His tests are take home, but expect impossible questions. He has a tendency to go on tangents, which can be funny.

Although Depalma is not the best teacher I've ever had, he is certainly not the worst. Very nice guy, his content and assignments are made much clearer if you go in during office hours and ask every question that you need. Tests are straightforward as long as you do the study guide. You will learn and do well as long as you put in effort.

Really nice, personable guy, but his I found his way of teaching to be very challenging. His panacea for every problem is to go to his office--and wait with a half dozen other students. Although he is super flexible with due dates, etc. Overall, a hard class.

Depalma is a nice guy, flexible, and helpful, but I think I'm not familiar to his teaching method. He always assigns reading and expects that you know every thing about each command before coming to class. His class could be easy if you have background about python or Java.

Absolute worst professor I've had in CS. Seems to be familiar with high-level concepts in his areas of research, but stumbled through explanations of algorithms and low-level ideas. Not nearly enough programming assignments, and all learning was done outside of class. If you take him, you can expect a decent grade, but you won't learn anything.

Great professor for upper level classes but would steer clear of him for 121, if you are an experienced programmer hes great. He's quirky but cool and while his proofs are hard to understand he answers questions well.

I really like him, I think if you are a girl or have Italian heritage taking his class your in a good spot because if you are a girl you are in the minority with engineering so he will remember your name and he loves Italians. If you go to office hours he will help you but he leaves at 6. It is suggested to put in at least 6 hours a week.

Before taking his class I was really interested in Computer Science as a major, I had built my own computer, etc. After his class, I never want to see a line of code again because it will remind me of him. By far the worst teacher I've had, worst class, and my biggest regret of college. Please, please, please, do not take him.

He is a very nice guy but his ability as a teacher is not great.  Friends in other 121 classes were creating codes for finals that we briefly covered in the first couple weeks of class!  Only way to pass with more than a C is to visit during office hours but there is usually a line of 6 or more students. He tries to be fair but he falls short.
Entertained and educated. I enjoyed his classes.

Depalma is probably the worst teacher I ever had at GU. I feel sorry for the incoming students. He has no teaching style or ability. Even when he is wrong or makes mistakes, he refuses to listen to your input.
Depalma is terrible!!

This guy is smart and he's really good at what he does, but he is not a good teacher. He can't explain anything very well and the only way I learned anything in this class was from other students who had already done Python. Overall, my recommendation is to avoid this professor.

He is terrible at teaching, he doesn't explain anything. He expects you to know everything coming into the class. He is willing to help outside of class, but isn't very clear when he does help you. I wouldn't recomend this class.
Horrible Professor :(
Depalma is the worst professor I have ever had at GU. His tests and assignments are extremely hard. AVOID!!

A very nice guy but absoulutely horrible teacher. It is almost impossible to learn anything in his class unless you already have some sort of programming backround. The average on the midterm was a 52% and thats no surprise because he does not explain programming at all in class and his homework is very hard. Definatly would not recommend him.
He is a very energetic and helpful professor! He is always willing to help outside of class. I recommend his classes!

He isn't a very good teacher. Class consists of him posting code up on the screen every day. If you show up, you get an extra 12% to your grade, so attendance is somewhat mandatory. He has absolutely no life, so he will think up random and crazy homework assignments that barely anyone will get. Avoid.

Very difficult class compared to the other comp sci classes at GU.He gets very enthusiastic about his lectures, which makes the class much easier to get through.Tests are open book, open note and open computer and students are given a study guide before the exam, which makes them passable.Great guy and very willing to help students.

Worst CS I have ever had.  He does NOT TEACH. HE copy/pastes code from the textbook to the computer and shows it during class.  If you don't already know how to code well, you will screw yourself by taking depalma.  All he does is hack at other peoples code and try to make it do slightly different things. Terrible idea. Then thinks up impossible hw

He made a class that should have been easy into a ridiculously painful ordeal. He way overdid the assignments. Only way his class could be worse is if he didn't get excited about the material. And he doesn't understand a lot of the stuff himself, so of course he can't teach it very well. Just shows examples and expects you to do crazy assignments.

He is self-taught, doesn't concern himself w/ the theory behind/foundations of CS. Just throws code (examples from text) onto screen. Everytime a Q is asked, responds "I don't know;" his way of finding out--take out portions of code and see what happens.  Haphazard, unclean approach to CS. Didn't learn anything new; struggled through projects.

Although Prof. De Palma is quite hilarious and says all kinds of random funny things, his teaching style is really not the best.  He basically shows code on the screen and talks about it, which can be really boring and not very helpful.  Fortunately this class is not hard to begin with, otherwise it might be a problem.

Took DePalma's course offered on the convergence of technology and communications.  DePalma was tremendously engaged with his students, and offered great insight.  I highly recommend this professor's communications offering.  I have never studied computer science with him, and his approach to that field might be very different.

I for some reason couldn't stomach DePaulma. But that's beside the point. He's an intelligent man, but unfortunately doesn't possess the greatness of what we call good-teaching-ability. The geekiest kids seem to take after him though, maybe they thought his weirdness matches theirs. Too bad there aren't too many choices here at this school.
Hard to understand, gives hard labs every week, if you have no knowledge in C++, don't take him
EXCELLENT professor. Makes C++ fun!

(CPSC193) Honestly she's such a ball of energy and a really nice caring person (though sometimes a lil passive aggressive) Class is light work but she does assign these super long readings that are strictly graded. However she teaches great and makes hard concepts really easy to understand. Overall tho definitely take her if she is ever an option.

There was a lot of long homework assignments and quizzes every week but no tests. She's a really nice person but I don't care for her teaching styles. She accused me of cheating multiple times when I hadn't and instead of talking to me she just took points off until I had confronted her.
Very amazing professor 10/10 would take again! Had her for First Year Seminar (not an option on here) and it was a very enjoyable experience
Great teacher. Easy class and very fun to listen to. Highly recommend.

Gina is amazing! Very smart, very organized, and super passionate. Her class was not easy, but if you stayed on top of the work load it was definitely manageable. Gina gives you all these sneaky assignments that build on each other so at the end of the class you have a super awesome comprehensive data science project that's great for resumes.

Gina is the best CS teacher in GU!!!! She is so patient and very kind. If you have any problem, you can ask her, whatever this problem belongs to C++, java, or python. I love her sooooooo much and I want to take her class again

Gina is the reason I've stuck with computer science. I've taken multiple of her classes and each one has made me a better coder and has challenged me more than the last. Her classes are difficult but incredibly rewarding and she is by far the best professor I've had at Gonzaga.

All the other comments are right on the money: Gina is an awesome professor. This class I took with her was about data science, which is a CS field I really struggle with. She went through each algorithm in detail and made sure to check in with students (including the ones on Zoom). The PAs were complex, but Gina was there to help :)

I didn't know how to code before her class. She made everything clear-cut and understandable. Short quiz at end of each week, large data assignments due every couple of weeks, project at the end. Super welcoming and helpful during office hrs and nice when it comes to deadline extensions. Challenging but everything was meaningful and manageable.

Gina is super sweet! Love taking classes with her, because you learn a lot about both the class topic and general CS topics (APIs, version control). Her remote learning-accommodating lecture recordings are super helpful for programming assignments. Assigning big PA during final project development has gotten rather stressful, though.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

review_text: 2nd class with her, gina is awesome, my favorite professor probably ever. really smart, organized, and good at what she does and cares a lot about her students. i learn a lot, am challenged but still do well in the class, and she makes class fun and interesting and engaging. I would take gina for any class. TA grades data assignments for her.

Gina is a really good teacher, and challenges you a lot. She has high but very clear expectations & is very communicative and accessible over email/office hrs. She is rly good at explaining concepts but can be a bit intimidating/passive aggressive when answering a question. Tests are pretty hard, PAs are fun but challenging. lots of chances for EC.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Dr. Sprint is maybe the best CPSC teacher I've had at GU (next to Bowers). She definitely makes you work, but she wants her students to do well, and she works just as hard, if not harder than her students to see them succeed. She is unbelievably proficient in the concepts she teaches, and I feel like her class has been more than worth my time.

Gina is by far the best CPSC teacher at Gonzaga. All of her lectures in class are easy to understand. Also, she gives you so many resources and source code that you will always be able to figure out how to solve a problem. Wish she could teach every compsci class available
rating_tags: Amazing lectures--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Gina is awesome! She's super knowledgeable about all things CS. The homework got a little tricky and time-consuming. A lot of material was covered in a short amount of time, but it'll make you an Android expert by the end of the semester.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: LOOVE GINA, she's amaz-balls. TAKE HER CLASS. She's amazing, she will make you love computer science and help you believe in yourself. If I could be best friends with Gina, I would.

Gina is a GREAT teacher and really makes you learn the material. There are weekly/biweekly programming and "micro-assignments" that take time but help you review the material - plus there's extra credit for nearly all of the assignments that help boost your grade. If you're going to have a CS teacher PICK GINA she's the best!
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Be prepared to google/youtube everything. Her lectures are utterly worthless, and I would often find myself googling the topic later, simply so I could understand the material.

I was excited to take this class thinking I would learn a lot. I was wrong. All we did were brain dead reading assignments and Lab reports that felt like a tutorial from a blog post. The Lab Report prompts often had mistakes in them too. Avoid her if you actually value learning. She'll also round your grade down after claiming to round up.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

review_text: If I could give Zhang a 0/5 I would. The lectures are so bad that its legitimately a waste of your time to go to them. Youre better off watching a YouTube video on the tutorial than sitting through a lecture with Zhang. Also, her PowerPoints are useless and provide no context or help. However, you can somehow get an A while knowing nothing.

Topics covered in this class are not easy, but she explains them well. Take notes in class and make sure you understand how to implement the various examples covered. The test material is literally the same as the quiz material. Homework assignments are hard but she provides you with the code to start them. You deserve the grade you get.

I did not expect it to be easy, but I did not expect the disaster of a semester I got in Zhangs class. Try learning a subject completely foreign to you in severely broken English. The communicationlanguage barrier was so awful it made the class pretty much impossible. Also I learned absolutely nothing in lecture and she made comp sci uniteresting

Professor Zhang definitely knows her material. That said, she is absolutely impossible to decipher. She hardly speaks English, making it very hard for someone who doesn't already understand the content to make sense during lectures. She also calls random people constantly, and expects you to know the answer without hardly teaching it yet. Stressful

Professor Zhang knows her stuff! She is quick-witted, funny and gives interactiveentertaining lectures. Some material is difficult, but she is happy to help you through it. I highly recommend her higher level courses. She doesn't get mad easily, but don't use electronics irresponsibly during lecture. (Tests may seem difficult at times.)

Overall disappointed with the course. Tests and quizzes were usually fairly difficult. Assignments were often not clear and hard to learn from since she would usually just give us code and make us work with it. Also posted misleading grades online, since the final grade was weighted differently, which caused my to do much worse than I expected.

Dr. Zhang is a great professor. She knows her stuff.  She talks a lot about ideas and implementations. I learn a lot about C++  and implementation of data structures since she always involve students in her class.

While Professor Zhang's lectures are relatively clear, she severely lacks the ability to communicate what she wants for assignments. I would be unsure of what she would ask for and receive almost no help during office hours (she would continue to be ambiguous). Tests are inconsistent and you don't learn a lot of coding. Overall not a good class.

A subject matter that should have been very interesting was made uninteresting.  Zhang isn't a bad lecturer, but can be unclear at times particularly when giving assignments or tests. There is very little work, however some of the problems in the homework and tests are over material not covered.

Granted this was her first year of teaching so there was a lot of learning for her to do. Her tests seemed a bit unfair at times due to the wording (unclear), as the language barrier presented a problem at times. The grading of tests was also inconsistent. This was her first year teaching so she will learn with time. She cares about improving.
She spends a lot of time teaching the conceptual side of data structures, but she does not really help that much concerning the coding aspect of the class. A 1.5 hour class that feels like 3.

You get as much effort as you put in, that's about it. The lectures are straightforward, and I highly recommend writing notes for each lecture (I use Onenote and download the lecture slides, which he gives, no textbook is needed!). There are quizzes (all extra credit), and labs (2 required, 2/3 extra credit)—lots of opportunity to get an A.

Cybersecurity is a very tricky subject for me. But Professor Liu designed this course with absolute perfection. If you don't work at all then you might get into trouble having good grades, but if you pay attention to given material and lectures, then this course is very rewarding. Assignments and projects are very fun to do. Definitely recommend.

Dr. Xingya Liu has been one of my favorite instructors so far. I took his class as an elective based on a recommendation, and it turned out to significantly enhance my understanding of my core classes. He provides excellent feedback, responds promptly, and assigns some of the most enjoyable and engaging projects I've encountered while learning.

Unfortunately, my experience with this professor was not ideal. The lectures often lacked clarity, and the teaching style made it difficult to grasp key concepts. Additionally, feedback on assignments and exams was minimal, leaving students unsure of how to improve. Communication outside of class was also challenging.

Dr. Liu is an exceptional educator and one of the standout professors at Lamar University. His vast knowledge in the field is complemented by his genuine commitment to student success. Dr. Liu's teaching style and methods are engaging, effective, and tailored to meet the diverse needs of his students. He should receive a raise.
10/10 professor cares you learn the material and will reply by email very fast.
Worst Professor ever
rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

review_text: Thoroughly enjoyed Dr. Liu's Computer Networks class, delivered online. He effectively conveyed intricate network theories with clarity and simplicity. The course was engaging and provided deep insights, enriching my understanding even through a virtual format
rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Dr. Liu's Analysis of Algorithms class was outstanding. He simplifies complex topics, making them easy to grasp. His interactive lessons with real examples boosted my confidence in computer science. Highly recommend his classes for clear, effective learning.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Tough Grader

Professor Xingya Liu's Analysis of Algorithm lecture was exceptional. His depth of knowledge, clear explanations, and engaging teaching style made complex concepts easy to understand. The real-world examples and practical exercises enhanced our problem-solving skills. Overall, an outstanding learning experience.

Amazing professor! Professor Liu was such a knowledgeable teacher in the classroom. His lectures were well organized and paced. Homework and projects were easy to solve if you follow the lecture learning. Slides and videos were carefully edited for the students. Grading was fair. He cared about the students and was a good person.
The projects help with learning the material, the homework are explained to better understand the material, and lectures are well done. Great guy and great professor.
I truly appreciate you for helping me in many occasions. Thank you very much for the course. I enjoyed every minute of your lecture as well as your marvelous sense of humor.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: A good professor who knows what he is talking about. Very patient and will provide answers to any questions that the students ask. Attendance is mandatory and points are deducted based on how many classes you miss. There were a couple of projects, but they were not too difficult. There is a quiz for every chapter and some homework.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: Won't ever regret taking this class. He's fair, gives great lectures and though there are lot of assignments, you'll have a very high chance of an A if you complete them on time. Learned a lot about Java from his course and his lectures are very good at explaining the course work. 100% worth it.
Gives a lot of work
friendly guy, do the work you will do well. show up to class for bonus points etc.

This is an easy A, he talks really slow and the content is not that hard. I felt like I wasn't ready for the midterm until our review day(really good review day). After reviewing what he said and watching back our lectures I got a great grade. Just study hard for a few days and you can learn weeks of content for sure. Also do project extra credit!!

Interesting course. The professor goes into detail during lectures and grades assignments respectably. The work leaned more towards the useful side than trivial memorization. The final wasn't mandatory if you had enough points for your desired grade.

Teacher is always available to help, even outside of class. He has great sense of humor. Gives great real-life examples and reviews for exams. Goes over homework/project/test/quiz questions with the class. Overall a great professor.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: His lectures are very helpful in understanding the subject matter. Projects and assignments make you learn a great deal. Dr. Liu provided feedback to all our queries and assignment. Moreover, he is always available for students to clear their doubts.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes--Amazing lectures

review_text: He is a really great teacher, always willing to help, and cares more about teaching with a lot of practicals.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Excellent professor, very knowledgeable and a fair grader (points based grading system). This is a lab heavy class. Dr. Liu gives the opportunity for extra credit by completing additional labs. Easily accessible outside of class. Textbooks can help but I did not use since he teaches from his power points/slides. Solid choice for a CPSC/CS Elective.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

review_text: He explains a lot of stuff that isn't on his slides. He goes into more detail than what the slides do during class. 4HW 20pt project 10pt (go over a lab of ur choice) mid 15 final 25 quizzes 8 (xtra, usually in class) 8 labs (10pts ea), have to do 3 and the anything else is extra credit. labs can get difficult, but he can help. Lab heavy class
He is very knowledgeable, yet need to work on delivery/ presentation stuffs. Subject wise Good.
rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

review_text: Class was graded by almost purely exams. First exam was 10%, midterm -20%, and the final was 50%. All exams are comprehensive, covering over 1000 pages of material and no study guide. This class was very difficult for anyone who has exam anxiety.

She pretty much can't teach anything. Half of the time you're trying to figure out what she is trying to say, this problem is compounded for the online course due to bad audio equipment. Don't expect her to go above and beyond for you. For example, if you run into technical difficulties with the online course, she will not help you at all.

This class for the most part is teach yourself. Textbook is required and programming assignments/projects were done online through Revel. Zero supplemental material or online lectures. Prof. took over a month to grade the first two tests and I didn't receive my final exam grade back until after my final grade was posted. Mediocre class at best.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

review_text: Took online due a covid, no virtual lecture like other professors, didn't offer any help on content or questions on homework, online book thru pearson was only teaching material you get. I got stuck on an programing assignments she told me not to email her with question but email her grader, why did i pay for the class if she didnt teach.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

review_text: Make sure your psychic powers work before you select this course, because you will have to take a quiz before you know it exists. Then, when you're told, the time to take the quiz is over. The tracks will be covered when you try to show her in online recording where she went wrong because she has academic freedom to fail you however she pleases.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things--Beware of pop quizzes

review_text: Professor Wang is the best professor I've had at Lamar so far. She answers emails, grades things on time, gives clear feedback and-- most importantly-- offers abundant extra credit (i.e. you can get 200pts on a 100pts assignment). No tests or finals, just a few assignments that she grades very generously even without the extra credit.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Dr. Wang is a pretty straightforward professor. You're pretty much on your own to learn the content, but she does provide online office hours once a week. Assignments are on Revel, but aren't that informative. Be ready to do your own supplemental learning if you're having a hard time. One major con: she can be painfully slow at returning tests.

I took her for CS I Lab. I can't really complain. She's foreign so the accent is thick, but you can tell she likes her job as a teacher. She's helpful with students, and her class was easy. She's a good choice.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Tests? Not many--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

review_text: If you know Java this class will be super easy. Going in I have never learned any type of computer coding and I made an A in this class. Make sure you talk to other students and get help from the ones who know what they're doing. Also you can google some programs and find the answers online. Only bad she is foreign so there is a heavy accent.

This class was very helpful especially since i was a new programmer. If you pay attention in your 1336 class i promise you this class will be easy as she offers a lot of extra credit in the first couple of assignments so its very hard to fail this class. You would have to be virtually  retarded not to pass this class. new programmer or not. :)

She's Chinese, so it's a little difficult to understand her sometimes. Her rule: if you make a B or higher on all the tests and do all of the lessons online, you don't have to show up for class, but if you don't make those grades, attendance is mandatory. She goes through the lessons step-by-step and makes it easy to understand.
Kind of hard to understand but really helpful.
I took class online. Somewhat weak grammar but very responsive to questions/concerns.

I took this class as an elective, as a senior accounting major, and this is the best elective I have chosen! No exams, no quizzes, only hw due every Sunday. You will watch videos, some of which are long but interesting, and answer questions that follow along with the videos. I recommend this class, even if you are not a computer science major!!

Timothy has an issue with admitting when he makes mistakes. You the student will be held accountable for his mistakes. Don't bother taking any of his courses if you need feedback or any sense of direction he will not offer assistance and will tell you that you will fail.

This is for COSC 1324. You watch videos and write essays. Some of the videos are really long however, but it's a simple, straightforward, class. This isn't an EZ A class, you get a grade based on how well you followed the prompts/rubric.

Dr. Roden is practical oriented teacher. Most of the students are not good at programming. Programming does required time and effort. Dr. Roden has solid background in programming. He teaches programming rally well . Exams are moderate and it supposed to be.

Dr Roden is solid teacher. He gives good programming lectures and takes time to really explain his thinking in terms of the code he's writing. He probably won't be the easiest or nicest teacher you've ever encountered, but he will make you a better programmer. Read the syllabus and follow the instructions on assignments and you'll do fine.

Lecture class over programming fundamentals. He just gets in front of the screen and talks about the chapter that y'all are going over. He makes the test the night before, and usually goes off of what he discusses the class before(review). Not that bad of a teacher, but then again, this is a lecture class so it's just boring.

I don't think there's nothing wrong with Mr. Roden. He knows what he's doing, and he does examples of programs related to the chapter, so it's easy to catch on if you're a good learner. Problem is that his lectures are very slow.. He gives plenty of grades, and is an easy grader. Do the assignments, and get a good grade if you follow the rules.

Very Supportive professor to students. All his assignments are practical. Course structure is good. Even though if students not performed well for any given work, he encourages and provides good support for completing that work. Accepts new ideas, respects students work even if it is small. Has good coding skills,Innovative.

Dr. Roden is by far the best teacher I've ever had! I've learned a number of different course topics from him and have understood all of them. He's very helpful and appreciates it when students come ask him for help. I have a hard time understanding why everyone else has given him such low ratings. If you're in to CS though, then take Dr. Roden!

Writes all his tests the night before, and has a hard time admitting when he makes mistakes.  Grades for assignemnts were pretty fair, attention to detail is a must.  Lectures were bland, but he very rarely took attendance and posted the powerpoint slides online.

Mr Roden is really nice. But his class is very very confusing. If you have no computer science background then dont even bother. you will sit there and not know what he is talking about. I ended up having to drop and change my major. but if you need help you can always contact him and he will help you.

Mr. Zhang's teaching ability is simply phenomenal. His 15-year-old son (or 14, I don't really know) is so handsome and wonderful. I just loved talking to the both of them, and his son helped me with his class and other things as well. ;)
Mr. Zhang is a wonderful professor and has a great class. His handsome son in 9th grade in high school gave me all of the answers to his work.
Great and technical deep dive..
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

review_text: The class itself is okay but I feel the professor is unorganized in a sense. It's almost the end of the semester and discussion boards were added because of online students not viewing lectures. Everyone should not be held accountable for this.

8-week course over the summer. This was a difficult class, however Dr. Zhang did an excellent job of explaining the mechanics of different algorithms in his lectures/videos. The homework and tests were doable if you put in the work. In addition to Operating Systems, this was one of the most useful undergrad classes I've taken.
Poor style of teaching. Comes with slides in the class and finishes the class by reading the slides.
Graded by few things

Dr. Zhang is a great prof and is always available to talk. I took him for Data Structures and he was good at explaining tough subjects in that class. Every lecture he would start by reviewing the previous lecture (MWF class) and that helped a lot. I rarely missed classed but when I did the reviews he did came in handy. I'd recommend him for sure.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Zhang is a good teacher, albeit inexperienced. Although, he does makes up for his inexperience by curving students' grades. I believe that he really is trying to become a better teacher. He explains things slowly but clearly and is there when you need help. His English is not as good as some, but I do believe it has gotten better.

He is a brand new to teaching the class was assembly he was very reasonable with the amount of work he assigned and with his test. His speacalty is image processing and was way better then the last teach I took the class with. If you have to take the class I would recommend him, also no book which was nice and he had really good detailed slides.

A lot of work but not terrible. The tests were challenging because the terminology was difficult to understand. The questions were multi-answer questions that would mark off points if you selected a wrong choice. So if there were 5 choices and you selected 3 chooses, but only 2 were right, it would count against you. Likewise if it was opposite
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

review_text: Great professor! Organized, knowledgeable, and provides clear guides. Exams are challenging but fair. Keep up with the work and you'll learn a lot.

I took course 5340 with Zhao and she was very snippy. The assignments are easy, but the midterm and final were difficult. Especially those that were multi-select. She takes points off if you select the incorrect answers which was very odd. I barely ended up with an A and thankful I do not have to take her class again!
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

review_text: Hardest part of this class was the midterm since it was 50 questions multiple choice. She guides you in completing the assignments by posting videos of her TA working on the assignments. Final was hands on and reflects the assignments completed throughout the course. 10/10 recommend there is a lot of guidance.

This professor will test you over 250 pages worth of textbook material in 3 weeks, and then refuse to elaborate on what you got wrong. She proctors 5 point quizzes, also will not let you see your mistakes on those. She does allow a student assistant to grade and give help on the homework, but can't guarantee the homework is being done properly.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Graded by few things

review_text: The material is straightforward. Be sure to pace yourself in this class. As long as you don't fall behind and you actually try...you will be fine!

Great class! Everything was in a simple layout. She provides support and instructions for all of the assignments. The material is easy to read and relates to the real world. A lot of easy-to-follow hands-on assignments. Two MC exams. Yu is awesome! I wish she taught more classes at Lamar!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

review_text: Only grades in the class are 3 exams and a project. If you do poorly on 2 tests you're likely to fail
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Professor Nash don't miss. I have had him for 1351, 4330, and 4101. He always delivers at the same level of brilliance and passion. Make sure to take him before you graduate.

Nash is one of the best professors you can take at LSU. The class is very straightforward. If you pay attention in class and review his slides, you will have no problem doing very well. Lecture almost always ends early. The hardest part of the class is the three coding projects he assigns that should be easy for a senior CS student.

I believe he is great in the Intro to CSC classes, but Software Development isn't really where he shines. He makes you practically start a business since investors will come at the end of the semester for the top 7 or so groups. He focuses more on you starting a business than actually teaching Software Development. Take if you like business classes

Nash is one of the best professors at LSU - he really wants to see his students succeed. He was always happy to give feedback on our project, helping us to get a better grade and produce a fully-functional, marketable application.

Best teacher in the LSU computer science department.  The class is straightforward, hit your milestones, present your project, and study for the two exams. His lectures are essential to understanding the material quickly, and he will always tell you what's on the exam in class ONLY.

Dr. Nash was great. He really cares about his students and encourages them to see the big picture and focus on learning concepts instead of just memorizing code. My only complaint with the class is that it is extremely test heavy but he is still by far the best option for 1351. Lectures were very thorough and he kept them interesting.
Dr. Nash's energy is unmatched. 4330 is more of a therapy session for me at this point. It's the only class that I feel like attending. Other Comp Sci professors should take notes.
Passionate about computer science. He's always willing to help if you have questions. Tests and assignments are easy enough as long as you pay attention in class.
Many good things about him are mentioned in other reviews, but I love how he cares about the future of the students and inspires them to get internships and see the big picture.
The greatest computer science teacher I have ever taken at LSU by far and I'm a junior.

The only professor at CompSci who grades fast. We get our grades the same day of the exam. Assignments are cool and exams are fair. It is refreshing to have a professor who is predictable and easy to talk to.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

review_text: Here is the deal, don’t take anyone else other than Nash for 4101. It will save your life for Compilers with Baumgartner. Lectures are very clear and exams are easy. I’d say he has the best structure in the CS department.
Best professor in the CS department. If only the rest of the department had his passion and teaching style... :'((

Amazing teacher! If you show up to class and pay attention, you will pass. The one thing weird about his teaching style is that he does not allow the use of computers in class, but it works out well. My main advice is to get a cup of coffee before class and listen to his lecture.
The best CSC professor at LSU!!
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

review_text: Doctor Nash is the best! He is such a great professor and he truly cares about his students. His lectures are clear and easy to understand and his class overall is a fair level of difficult.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Lecture heavy

review_text: Nash is easily the best professor I've taken so far at LSU. Very funny, knows the material very well, and he programs in front of his students. The material can be difficult and the tests aren't easy, but if you follow his advice and practice coding at home, you can get a good grade in this class. I wish he could just teach all of my csc classes.

Nash really deserves the hype everyone gives him. He's clearly used to people coming from Kundu having learned nothing, so he makes sure to start at the basics. Very passionate, if not a little quirky. The only bad thing I can say about him is that the class can be pretty boring if you already know some of the concepts. Great problem to have, tho

Professor Nash is one of the best Com Sci professors at LSU. He is hilarious and his lectures are very engaging. Labs are super easy and he does most of it during class. Tests are all multiple choice and its very easy if you pay attention in class. Also gives great advice about what you need to do to succeed in the industry. Take him if u can.

Nash is a fantastic professor that really cares about his students. His lectures are very engaging and he always welcomes questions. The midterm and final are 100% multiple choice questions, which helps because they make up almost all of your grade. Nash doesn't follow a textbook, so you won't have a book to help, but you shouldn't need one anyway.

He's easily the best CSC professor at LSU. Labs are adequate challenges but follow his lectures very clearly. He gives advice about your career, scholarship opportunities, and if you get a good grade (A) in his class you can be a TA. Exam ?'s are from the PowerPoint lectures. Lectures are amazing, he's funny too - you can check out his twitter.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures--Caring

review_text: Dr. Nash is one of the best professors I had at LSU. He truly cares about his students. He is super responsive on emails and is willing to chat anytime about the course or your career path. His tests are pretty hard but if you study and ask questions you will do fine.

One of the best professors you could ever have in the Computer Science department, unlike *cough* Kundu. His lectures are amazing and clear and is highly realistic with what he expects and what you'll do as a Comp Sci Major. He gives great advice and industry examples. Overall, take him for any class you see him lecture.

This man might be one of the best CSC professors at LSU. He not only has a great sense of humor, but he also is very smart and does a great job at teaching the class. He also takes the time to talk about the industry, which is very valuable for students when deciding if they want to continue their major. Nash is a must-take and you won't regret it!

1351 with Nash has exceeded my expectations so far. Dude is seriously smart and extremely kind. Ive never thought Id say this about a Comp Sci class, but I actually look forward to this class. Never seen a professor this organized or passionate about teaching. Clone this dude and make him teach every cs class please.
Love this man.

Excellent lectures! Extremely accessible outside of class and always answers questions satisfyingly. The tests are easy if you know the material. During pandemic, labs are worth very little of the grade, so your fate lies in a midterm and a final. You don't need to attend but he gave extra credit once for attendance so...

Best professor at LSU. Doesnt bore you with powerpoints and texbooks and instead teaches you all of the concepts directly and in a challenging but very engaging mannor. Actually really looked forward to doing the labs every week as they were satisfying to solve and really help you learn the matterial in a practical way. Easy A if you are engaged.

Nash is one of the best CS professors at LSU. He has great knowledge on the topics he taught in Software Systems and he passed the knowledge to me successfully. His exams were pretty hard but he provided the sufficient materials to do well in them. He is a great guy and also always available outside of class. I highly recommend him.
rating_tags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Graded by few things

review_text: 4330 with Nash was the only class that I regularly attended (on zoom) this past semester. It felt like a lengthy job interview preparation class. The materiel, especially after the midterm, was challenging. Do not pass on taking Nash if you get a chance. I had him twice so far (1351), and would def take him again if possible.
The best professor at LSU, just do what you are supposed to do, and you will be rewarded. Really fair professor!

He's a great teacher, but be prepared for the workload you'll have in CSC4330. There is a big semester long group project where you make an app and a couple group assignments where you write specifications/testing documents. Make sure you find a good group, otherwise you'll be working overtime in this class.

ME student taking 1351 for my CS minor. The class is challenging for none-cs majors, but thankfully I had Nash. He is definitely one of the best LSU has to offer. DO NOT taking this class with anyone other than Nash. You will learn, laugh, and most importantly code. Take 'em.

Ive learned the most about coding with Nash. He really cares about his students and isnt afraid to tell you like it is. He also takes the time to make sure you understand the material in office hours. Sometimes he stresses oit if you dont understand though.
Great teacher and you can tell he really cares about the class. Study and code by yourself for an easy A.

Im retaking this class with Nash after failing miserably with Brener. Nash is a brilliant teacher, he's very organized and his material is clear. He codes in class which really helped me to code on my own for the first time. The labs are challenging but we get help from the TAs and the SI. Dude is seriously funny. Take him.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

review_text: Alright, though I put non-mandatory attendance, it would behoove you to go to class. You get quiz grades for it, based on how NASH feels. He's an amazing professor, is always willing to chat with students after class or during office hours. Will coordinate with students to help, and is VERY responsive to emails. A treasure at LSU
Nash is an amazing professor and he really cares and is enthusiastic about the students learning. If you can take him - do it you will learn so much.

Nash is a breath of fresh air for the LSU CS dept. He is charismatic and brings life the class material. He makes complex material easy to learn and leaves no ambiguity when it comes to what will be covered on the midterm and final. He is passionate about us learning to become competent software engineers and shows it in his work.
rating_tags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Amazing lectures--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: I took this class on the spring not the best time to take it. it was hard to find a good project group. Nash is a very good teacher he keeps the class interesting and has lots of  stories to tell, avoid this class if you dont like working with people.

Dr. Mahmoud is different from other professors in CS. I like his class so much. He is very entertaining and makes the class fun to attend.  I have never seen him upset or in a bad mood.  I like his accent and the way he tells stories, very funny guy.

Just had Nash for software systems design.  I have learned a lot in this class. Nash is a very good teacher.  He is very helpful, especially at office hours, and often responds to emails very fast. I didn't like the pop quizzes though! If he is in a good mood, then its basically  a stand up comedy show!  He's a cat person though!

I took 4330 over the Spring. Nash was the highlight of my semester. I wish I had him for some of my introductory classes. I agree that this class has lots of homework. Just avoid last minute submissions! Overall, very funny, hilarious actually, very respectful of everybody and really wants you to learn.

Nash is probably the best professor I have ever had in my college career and definitely one of the most knowledgeable. He knows his stuff very well and he can relate to students. Tests are easy if you attend class, lots of homework though but that's the way this class is anyways. Great sense of humor, makes the class fun to attend.

He's better than the alternatives in this department, but he purposely makes the class harder than it needs to be. He'll ban convenient fixes and mandate you take the hardest route to reach an answer. Also, if you don't get at least a 70 on the final, you are capped at a C- in the class.  "Just think about it" is his go-to help.
The computer science 1 instructor you want. Explains topics in deep depth with examples. Genuinely cares for his student's success and encourages questions

Dr. Barbosa was an excellent professor who helped me understand coding despite my lack of experience. He dedicated time for questions in the lab, after lab, and during office hours. His lectures were mostly clear, though sometimes a bit dry. My only complaint was the heavy workload.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

review_text: This class covers quite a bit of content in a short period of time, however Dr. Barbosa tries his best to help everyone understand the material. If you study the textbook, slides, lectures, and utilize office hours you will be fine. The workload is a lot if you procrastinate. In my opinion, Dr. Barbosa is one of the better options for this course.

This class covers a lot of content, yet Barbosa was committed to making sure that everyone understood the material. You'll go from having never touched python to writing some 50-20 line programs. Don't fall behind because with the speed of this course you'll be struggling to catch up

Professor Balbosa received his PhD from University of Alabama Huntsville where classes are taught by graduate students rather than professors. He told my daughter who an experienced programmer that she could not program. His hateful arrogant statement sent her into a panic attack which was not necessary. Most of the students in his class failed.

Dr. Barbosa was an awesome professor. His lectures were a little lengthy (an hour and 25 min), immediately followed by lab, which finishes as soon as you get done with the coding. He and his assistant lab instructors were always open and willing to help, sometimes even staying an extra 30 minutes in lab to help out.
He tries his possible best to make sure every one of his students understands the material covered. Always willing to give feedback concerning codes.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

review_text: Dr. Barbosa was genuinely amazing. He lectures very well, sets his expectations for you early, and is always available to help. Some of the material was difficult, but he tried so hard to forsee where students would have issues and always addressed them early. Participation does matter, attend lectures and labs and turn in the HW and you'll be set.

Dr. Barbosa is an amazing CS teacher! You really have to make an effort to understand the concepts and think about them, but he is accessible in office hours ready to help. Some of the problems used in class are used on tests but with different applications. Study the book for vocab, the lecture for coding practice, and you will be all good.

One of the better professors in the program. He covers alot of material but puts alot of effort into facilitating learning. Good attitude, explanations are thorough & clear, goes over things multiple times, answers all questions, publishes good lecture slides, has a test review day, assists with projects. Hard class but he gives you the resources.

Sal Barbosa is a great professor who is very knowledgeable on the content. The class is structured in such as way that If you go to class and do the weekly coding project you will NOT struggle. The class does have an hour long lab after the lecture that caught me off guard the first day. Note: The textbook is necessary don't cheat, he will find out

Dr. Barbosa is an amazing teacher.  You have to come to class and pay attention to lecture to pass the tests. It isn't one of the easiest courses to go through but you do learn a bunch! (his favorite car is an rx7 first gen).
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Professor Barbosa is great and I really learned a lot. The projects got a bit difficult but it was all do-able and explained well.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: Awesome professor. He really loves what he does, he wants you to learn and be just as into it as he is. Really accessible outside class, Gives great help for labs during office hours. One note, DO NOT be late for ANY assignment. Basically no make ups are allowed. But there is a reasonable amount of time to do everything.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria

review_text: It is very important that you attend class and pay attention while you're there. If you miss class or don't pay attention it will negatively affect your ability to write the required programs in the lab attached to this course. The tests Dr. Barbosa gives are very straightforward, and you know what to expect on them.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Lecture heavy

review_text: Dr. Barbosa is an amazing professor. If you just come to class, pay attention, take notes, and do the work he suggests you'll pass with no problem. His class is structured in a way that if you do what you're supposed to, you'll be comfortable enough with the material that you won't need to study much for tests. O highly recommend him.

One of, if not my favorite, teachers. Algorithms is an exceptionally difficult class with a lot to cover so be ready to learn a lot, but that's what your paying for. I found the tests crazy difficult and the programs were real hard too, but, Barbosa made a really hard class so much better. GO TO OFFICE HOURS for help, and go early for projects.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

review_text: Good professor, will answer questions the best he can; there is a large disconnect in lecture material and test difficulty; lectures cover everything at a surface level, but tests go very deep. No final, just a third test during that time slot; I'd take him again, but I'd take his tests more seriously by reading the book and looking over HW/Quizzes
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

review_text: I would not recommend Dr Barbosa as a professor in an online environment. His powerpoint presentations are mostly definitions with minimal insight to the information necessary to work with the code yourself. The other half of class is used for lab, which doesn't prepare you for the 3 exams that account for 30% of your final grade. It's very hard.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

review_text: Computer science isn't an easy subject, but Dr. Barbosa is careful to make sure his students understand the material. The lectures are essential to the class and your learning, so if you feel lost or confused, use him as a resource. I've had friends who have taken other professors and struggled, so I highly recommend this one.

I couldn't pay attention because of how condescending he lectures the class. The OLA assignments aren't helpful for learning code, even starting with Python is a bad curriculum. Even so, Dr. Barbosa was redundant in each session and didn't cover new information in good depth.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

review_text: Is this class hard? Yeah kinda. Does Dr. Barbosa explain things well? Absolutely and even if there is a topic that still isnt clear, ask and he will do everything in his power to help you understand. He makes it known on the first day and reminds you continually that he wants you to succeed and genuinely means it.

Barbosa is an excellent lecturer, encourages you to ask questions, and really wants his students to understand the material. You still have to work for it, but his lectures directly correlate with the book (he uses the textbook power points). One of my best decisions was to take him for my first ever Computer Science class! Would take again!
He's the best computer science professor that I have ever taken. Very approachable and always there to help. highly recommend him.

He's a great CS1 teacher. He really tries in algorithms, but most of what I learned about algorithms came from the book, youtube, or Dr. Pettey in Discrete Structures. The first half of the semester was pretty much CS3.

I didn't get textbook and was fine because everything was on online slides and it is a great class for notetaking. Lowest test score was dropped which was really good. Pop quizzes stopped being hard when I read last weeks notes start of every class. I really liked his code examples during class, and there was always good feedback on anything wrong.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Barbosa is a great teacher, and I would highly recommend taking his class if available.  Makes it easy to take notes, which really help out with the homework. Follows the book as well so if you miss you can read the chapter to get an idea of what to do.  The tests can be challenging,  just make sure to read the questions thoroughly.

I found that reading the book helped a lot in this course. He follows the textbook, so that is super helpful. He's great at explaining things, and he won't make you feel dumb for asking questions. He is pretty funny, even at 8 in the morning. The multiple choice questions on his tests can be pretty difficult, but the textbook reviews help a lot.
Sometimes his quiz and test questions threw me off, but overall he's a decent teacher and his homework is fairly easy.
Tries to cram two lectures worth of material into one class. Has not taught for very long.

Dr. Al-Tobasei cares for his students. Java exams were programming questions on paper which makes it difficult if you're on a tight schedule or can't allocate enough time for CSCI3033. Attending class gives you a participation grade. During the Fall 2025 exams, he allowed a 1 page (1 side) handwritten, cheat sheet. Getting the textbook helps a ton!

Tobasei is a good guy. He understands your issues and is reasonable. He grades fairly and this course is very easy. Lecture is okay, not very engaging but is clear and explained well. He wants you to succeed and is a good intro to the computer science department.

His assignments and requirements are unclear at times. Reads off of powerpoint slides the whole time and goes over pre-made code. Exams feel like it was made the night before. I thought I would've enjoyed learning a new language, but his teaching style made it difficult.  If you have the option, take someone else.

Avoid...lol. Lectures are very dry because he is reading straight off of the power points. With that in mind, go ahead and pull up the youtube channel, "Programming with Mosh" and I guarantee you will learn way more there than attending class. Unless if you're in a bulking season from all the stress eating you're going to do, take another class.

Probably one of the worst professor I have taken. His lectures are quite dry and his style of teaching is ineffective. He basically reads off the powerpoint and and goes over pre written code.  His quizzes and OLA instructions are full of typos and he is never clear in his instructions. He is worse than robert smith for sure.
Nice professor and gave average lectures. Reasonable OLAs. Tests weren't too difficult and took from projects, homework, and quizzes. Was a pretty lenient professor when it came to grading.

He was a great teacher for an introduction-level class! He didn't let how much he knew get in the way of dumbing it down for us. You do mostly labs during the lab time that seldom bleed into homework and then a handful of homework assignments. He WILL assign quizzes over the weekend and forget to mention it so check D2L and be aware of that!

Professor Al-Tobasei gave great advice to complete the assignments when they were given. The assignments felt like a fun puzzle. If you like a good challenge, this is the course for you. If you can do the assignments, the exams are a cake walk. The PwrPt contain most of the info to code, but the book was nice too.
His finals are very hard, and that is all it takes to determine whether you can take future classes.

Professor was really helpful, if I ever had a problem with something instead of telling me whats wrong he would lead me in the right direction of fixing the problem in the code, take advantage of the online powerpoints, and always check every weekend for quizzes

Java was not too bad. Group project was either a JavaFX or Android app. He will assign quizzes on d2l and may not necessarily tell you about them but it's good practice to expect a quiz or two on the weekend. Seemed to be a lenient grader with OLAs. Biggest advice: Tests are based on questions from the book. Might be helpful to *acquire* it

I took his C# class (CSCI 3037) offered in the Spring. The class consists of a group project, tests, quizzes, and homework open labs. He taught the class well and was very strict on attendance. His accent isn't the worst but it gets hard to understand sometimes when he starts to mumble, but he is willing to help and has an attitude sometimes.

If you have problems in this course go to his office hours! I had problems turning in CLA's and I went and he helped me fix my issue. This is the same for any assignment that you are struggling on or think is graded unfairly/wrong. Also check d2l over the weekend because he has a habit of just assigning things.

Awful professor I hate him. He is tough and his exams are stupid hard and a harsh grader. Doesnt give a damn about you. And tutoring never helps He told me to go to tutoring . I went to tutoring asking for help and told me if I dont understand what is it asking for they cant help me and kicked me out. He never responds to emails.
rating_tags: Test heavy--Beware of pop quizzes--Tough grader

review_text: Pretty decent tbh. Id say hes tough only bc of the labs which you can seek help from lab assistant. But other than that make sure you read the textbook chapters it will help A LOT. And before each test, which is only 2 not including final, practice your coding bc the coding questions are worth a lot of points! Overall really effective teacher
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

review_text: If you are going to be remote, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. We were half in class and half on Zoom, and if you were on Zoom, it's safe to say you weren't going to learn ANYTHING. The equipment that was provided to him was not good at all, and he did not know how to operate it well either. Save your money and WAIT.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Pay attention in class and read through the chapters before hand. He is Lazy when doing final grading , does not own up to mistakes. Untruthful.

Dr Al-Tobasei is a nice guy, he's understanding and teaches well. I had some trouble in the last half of the semester/course but i think thats because that was right when we started to do online classes

Rafet was amazing, it's going to be near impossible to find a teacher as dedicated and inspirational as he was. I think it's safe to say that Dr. Tobasei changed my life. Not only did he teach Computer Science material during class, but he also taught us valuable life lessons. I've never met a more well-rounded and intelligent person. What a guy.
Tobasei is a swell guy. He makes occasional spelling errors but to say that his class has inspired me would be an understatement.

Rafet is honestly an awesome guy. Computer science isn't for everyone, but he is so incredibly helpful and is willing to do just about anything to make sure you get an A. The tests are super easy, and he drops the lowest test grade at the end of the semester. He's super nice- I'd take him before anyone else.

Very nice guy, always willing to help. He does have a thick accent which makes him very difficult to understand. The material is not very difficult, so if you don't mind learning it on your own the class isn't bad at all.

I took this class over zoom. Very easy class. One assignment per week on one of the Microsoft softwares, and they are very straightforward and easy. She even does them during class with you, so if you get a low grade on an assignment, it is not her fault. Would take her again!
rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: Professor Gu is great. The class is very easy, you are only given one assignment per week. Most of the assignments are facile tasks in Microsoft programs (excel, word, pp). The coding portion of the class can be difficult but her lectures give good instructions on how to complete them. Her test can be difficult but most do fine.

Dr. Gu is the best!!!! She is so nice and makes the class super easy (like she literally shows us how to do our homework step by step). There's only 1 assignment a week and a test every few weeks (the tests are really easy too). Definitely take her :)

I loved Dr. Gu! She covers a wide range of material in the computer orientation course. I even learned so much about topics I thought I was already proficient in. She thoroughly goes over all material and even shows you step by step how to do the labs. She often finished lectures early, but stayed on to help students with labs and homework. 10/10!

I took her online Computer Orientation class. Very nice. Attendance was mandatory. I love how she goes through the lab step by step explaining, so I could follow along. Not a lot of professors teach this way, and it is a big way that I learn. I appreciate it!

Such a nice lady! I wish we could have got to know her in person. If there's any confusion, she is always open to answering questions and explaining steps of the labs. She shows you exactly how to do the labs so it never felt like there was any homework. The only advice I'd give is to study for the closed AND open book tests.

There are 12 labs and 4 exams. She drops the lowest one of each. One day of the week she does a lecture style class and the other she actually does the entire lab.( I hardly attended the lectures and watched the videos the night before the lab). So as long as you follow her exact steps you will get an A on all the labs because she does them on vide

Super easy A! Professor Gu is extremely kind and explains everything in depth making it easy for you to understand. It's also all online so it was nice being able to just go grab a snack, etc. The only thing that might hinder some people is she does have a thick accent, but that wasn't an issue for me and I don't think it would be for many others.

All classes were on Zoom, so it allowed me to grab a bite to eat. Near the end we just delved into code which was mostly HTML and CSS, mostly was easy as she gave us resources. I'd recommend using Komodo Edit, as just using Notepad won't really help much.

She is very kind and the tests are very easy and open-book. There are a lot of labs but they are all fairly simple. She will drop the worst lab grade that you have, and the worst exam grade that you have, so if you made all As, you do not have to do the final lab or exam.

Yi Gu was absolutely amazing. The labs were super easy, and despite the class being on Zoom, she did an AMAZING job teaching the course and not making the class meetings boring. She definitely needs a raise because she cares about her students deeply.
She was super nice, understanding, and easy. We met once a week and she does the assignments with you during class. I hardly even listened and passed with an A.

Yi Gu's teaching gains a large amount of strength from her ability to find trouble spots in each of her students. Simply put, if Professor Yi Gu continues to place her best foot forward when giving valuable lessons here at MTSU, her students will forever do their best to mirror her image of passion, care, and overall effectiveness in the workplace.

This class is honestly really simple if you took any kind of computer class in high school. It's basically a class on how to use Excel and PowerPoint, with some extras. Dr. Gu did a good job at explaining the material. Tests were straightforward and all multiple choice. The final was the only one with short answer but she allowed a cheat sheet.

Overall a great teacher. She does have a bit of an accent, so listen carefully. She will answer any questions you have, so if you're confused actually show up to the labs (attendance not taken on lab days) If you do all the labs and review material for the tests, it's an easy A-B.

Very sweet woman. Just a little eccentric with her microphone box. Super thick accent and hard to understand. Can get off task easily. However, tests are simple if you study the guides given. Very easy A.
She's very kind, but has a little bit of a thick accent. Assignments are super straightforward and easy.

Dr. Phillips is a great professor but this class sucked the soul out of my life. There are just too many assignments and they can be difficult, especially of the OLA's. The exams are so long that you don't even have time to go back and check your work. 10 homeworks, 4 OLAs, 3 exams, final. You are expected to write a two page report for each OLA.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Test heavy

review_text: Exceptional teacher - one of the best I've ever had. This course was possibly the hardest CS class I've taken, but I learned way more than any other CS class too. The projects were super helpful - very clear grading criteria and organized course materials. Exams were similar to homework and everything was based on lecture material. Great class.
