rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Undoubtedly the best professor in MTSU

I took AI in my last semester of college. 3rd hardest cs class I took, but I feel as though I learned A LOT. 4 projects, 3 tests, final, and 10 hw assignments is your grade. Each project you're required to write a minimum 2 page paper. Tests are based on lecture and HW (study assignments). Be careful with test 2. Grading is very thorough but clear
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Dr. Phillips is a very thorough instructor with a great and sometimes subtle sense of humor. Very patient with questions and prefers to ensure most everyone understands a concept before moving on. His classes aren't super easy, but that's because he mostly teaches higher-level courses, and he teaches them well. I quite enjoyed my classes with him.

Dr Phillips is widely considered the best professor in the department. He teaches some challenging classes, but he's fair and consistent in them and clearly communicates what you need to do to succeed, both in general and in his rubrics. You can expect him to give detailed lectures and to be knowledgeable about the topics. He is very supportive.

One of the best professors in the program. Very systematic and thorough in how he explains things, but still keeps it clear & simple. Doesn't have any noticeable attitude problems like other professors in the program. His class can be tough but you will learn alot. My only criticism is the projects can be confusing depending on your background.

Professor Phillips is my favorite Computer Science professor at MTSU. His 4350/5350 classes are undoubtedly difficult, likely more difficult than most 4000 level courses. However, he has clear expectations for projects and tests. If you are looking for an easy upper level class, this is not it. However, if you really want to learn, this is for you.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

review_text: A lot of work to do in his course. Every homework, project, exam are challenging, and they are not as he explains in the class.

Dr. Phillips is the worst professor in computer science. 4 projects. 10 homework. Difficult homework. 3 exams, every 6 chapters have a comprehensive exam about 15 pages. Final exam. Include 3 exam+homework+projects. He forced us to come and made us think that this was the university's decision.

Dr. Phillips explains difficult concepts very well and makes them relatively easy. He is very well organized and has clear grading criteria. If you take the 3130 class, my biggest suggestion is don't fall behind because all of the labs are used for the final project. As long as you can keep up with the labs, I highly recommend Dr. Phillips.

Dr. phillips instantly became my favorite cs professor and in my top 5 for all. He has great lectures, if you pay attention in class his test become much easier, and building a Simple computer in his class sounds daunting but he supplies great tutorials to follow to be able to do them!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

review_text: I Dr. Phillips for CSCI3130 and was very excited to have him for CSCI4350 and was not disappointed. I highly recommend Phillips for any course.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

review_text: Dr. Phillips is my favorite teacher so far. He really does know his stuff and will take the time to explain anything to you very well. Understand the course may be difficult at times, however with him you will succeed if you put in the effort.

Dr Phillips is a fantastic professor. His class is very organized, and he is exceptional at explaining difficult concepts. His tests are reasonable in difficulty and length, and he also gives partial credit. Take him if you have the chance!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Phillips was a fantastic professor. He has a a very good tone, speed, and volume to his voice during his lectures and his explanations of course content were well-articulated. The labs for the class were quite challenging at times, though. Overall I would say that he was a stellar professor and look forward to taking his A.I. class.

Trash. Worst teacher of all time. Only grades were two tests which were made to trick you to the fullest extent. He gives extra credit for showing up. You don't learn the material, you discuss the material. If you're good at remembering EVERYTHING, you will be fine. He says a lot of taunting things as well. Take someone else to actually learn.

Only 3 tests including the final, pretty funny and made a boring topic as interesting as he could. Study guides looked much like the test, so it was not bad at all to prepare for them. Go to class to have a good understanding of the algorithms.

Dr. Hansen was an awesome professor. The lectures were long but they were interesting and had good visualizations. Only graded by a midterm (or project) and final (or project). You're given free reign of the topic and approach, for the project, and he seems to grade on effort so it's not too bad.
Super easy, just did the projects a couple days before they were due.

Dr. Hansen thinks of teaching as a competition. He said that if everyone fails then he has succeeded. Lectures are boring, content is confusing, and Hansen is mostly unhelpful. review_text: Barely teaches material. Gives accurate practice exams. normally gives extra credit. Graded on only 2 Exams (Midterm and Final). Very nice. I hear he's better in other classes though. Study what each algorithm does and its time complexity and you should be fine.
rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: The professor knows a lot about the material and stays after class to answer questions. review_text: Only grades are midterm and final. Absolutely do the extra credit. Hit up classmates who've taken the class for old tests. Have a good understanding of the practice midterm and final. Two tests are entire grade. Doesn't give things to practice on. He himself doesn't fully grasp the material.

**** THIS REVIEW IS FOR COSC 486 (unsure how to add courses)  Class was difficult gave only exams as major grade calculation. Exams were also on paper written no tech and no calculator allowed. Take his classes if you want to learn the subject really well
He helped me understand this alot, me personally I would take him again and he is an amazing teacher.
He is amazing, I highly recommend him because he helps you out alot and is kind.

take this class if you are looking to pass and not learn anything. he doesn't grade anything, once you submit that's an automatic 100% so no room for feedback. also, he can be very condensed and loves to argue with students
Very lazy teachers, doesnt teach just cancel classes to work in problems with no explanation

He is very helpful and thorough.  Starts the class at a level that everyone can participate and be knowledgeable of the topic. He is very fair.  If he could teach all COSC classes that would be great!

Steiner is tough with her students she will say Comp Sci isn't for you if you don't understand coding right away. review_text: Dr. Esther Steiner is an excellent, organized, and clear professor! Very surprised to see she doesn't have a higher score. Her Canvas page was so organized. Information easy to find. review_text: Gives a lab assignment weekly, although lectures usually covered the topics and any questions on these assignments.

Dr. Steiner is a great professor. She teaches everything so well it's pretty amazing. I'm not saying the material will be easy, but she's definitely a professor that will teach you the material well. review_text: I took this class online so I don't really know much about her personally. One time I did go to her office for help on a lab and she quickly sent me to the tutoring center. However, this was an easy A class and is great because you don't have to go to a lab. The lab instructions were clear but if you're using a mac look out for outdated info.

You should attend the lecture, but the lab sessions aren't really necessary unless you need extra help with the assignments. The TA's run the lab, but the prof. Is easily reachable via e-mail or her office.

I signed up for her course, was really unhelpful, asked if i could use a mac, says no says need a windows computer. First of all teachers need update with their technology these days. I could do MOST of the assignments on the Mac computer. Also I asked what assignments do we need the windows for, didn't know, yet you teach this class every year?

Not help at all from this professor. She tells you constantly  use the tutors but they don't help. She also tells you that the test is the study guide. That is lie. This class says no programming knowledge needed, that its a basic course. That's a lie. Its an advanced java course that you need experience for. Most students are already programers.
horrible!!!!! do not take her!
Great advisor, great professor. :)
Very helpful, and she cares about the students
Very helpful and very clear.  She's the CS department advisor as well.  Wish she taught more classes
Great advisor!  An asset to the CS department.  Wish I had her for class.
Helpful and clear. New class so things may change a little. Not someone who would try to screw you over.
organized, clear expectations, tests are easy (relatively) if you go to class and do the labs - nothing unexpected will show up on tests, very helpful, kind, clearly wants you to succeed
Very good professor. Extremely useful and helpful towards students.
Very helpful, friendly.

Find someone who had this class before you and ask to borrow their notes. You won't have to attend if you have the notes, plus the powerpoint is online. The labs weren't as bad as they seem, try and get more than one done at a time. Must have the book to do the labs!
Steiner gets pissy about people talking in class, but her bark is worse than her bite.
take the web class if at all possible- its great

Listening to a person in a coma would be more stimulating. Went to the class once & all she did was read slides that make up the online notes. Occassionally she gave info that wasn't in the notes, but easy to pass the class and never attend. If you really don't know about PCs, it might be useful.

One of the best teachers in the CS department. I took her for the first class of my major (cs 171). I just wish the department wouldn't be so stupid and make her teach non CS major classes (such as C++) rather then upper level CS classes.
WebCT course not really on online course even though it's supposed to be
If you have any knowlage of computers skip this class.
Evil woman...let me tell you!!!!
Dr. Steiner makes her classes interesting.  Knowledgable in her teaching and the projects are stimulating.  Would recommend any class she teaches.

review_text: Great professor. The course is pretty hard, but the topics covered are cutting edge. I recommend it if you like math and would like to deepen your knowledge in probabilistic inference and advanced machine learning techniques.
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: He is one of the smartest guy I have ever met. He thinks fast, talks fast (but very clear) and is very passionate about what he is teaching, which encourages you to keep up with him and want to know more. The course materials are well planned, it covers the basic graphical model stuff.
not recommend to future students. The course is tough and hard and the professor cannot make it understandable.
boring professor, very hard homework problems and some even undoable.

This guy still participated in mapping out the terrible syllabus even if he is not teaching the machine learning course. The instructor is his classmate and exactly a weird guy to avoid. do not take the course.

Can you imagine 26 homework programming problems of machine learning to be done in one week? if you do not want to get a very low grade, do not take his course. For students with data science major, i think you should copy some answers from other students. do not waste too much time on his course.

The materials are hard and his teaching is even worse than any other professors. In this case, you have to learn by yourself and spend almost all your spare time on this course. The bad thing is that Sontag always assumes everyone only register 1 coursesemester (i.e. his course). If you can get a B, then you are in the top 5% of students.

I work very hard for this course, 8+ hours per day and 6+ days per week. However, I got C- finally. In my opinion, a good course should be the course which could let students who are cleaver get A, who work very hard get B, who never cost time on it get F and others get C and D. But for this course, only very hard working clever students may get A

Don't take this class if you are looking for something easy or something to fulfill some requirements and don't take this class if you aren't willing to work hard.   If you are interested in the material and put in the hours, the class is quite rewarding and covers a large portion of the relevant material in probabilistic graphical models.
The material is difficult. But if you go to office hours, start homeworks early, it's doable.   Easily one of the best professors I've ever had.

Professor Sontag may assign a lot of homework and readings, but you actually walk away with a clear picture of the material.  His lectures are easily the best I've attended in Courant.  He seems to genuinely care about students in his classes.

Can you imagine that a professor can make more than 15 out of 40 drop the course? Sontag is such a guy. His slides and homework are copied from other institutions since he knows nothing about the course materials. The NYU curriculum committee checked his course after final and he is now in a very embarassing situation. He may lose his position soon

To be honest, it's not an easy course, but a challenge, speaking from a CS background student.  The professor is smart and helpful, so you can definitely learn a lot from him. It'll be better that you have some math background before taking this course.

Sontag thought he knew everything, but truth is....I cannot remember how many times he got stuck on the platform doing math derivations because this embarrassing moment happens all the time. Anyway, he seemed to enjoy his frequent interactions with the front-row noisy noisy girl, which makes this course a must if you want to ruin your college life.

He is, unfortunately, too fond of his own inventions and usually makes things more complicated than they need be. I wish the homework wasn't presented in terms of Sontags confusing non-standard formulations. You will have to do a loooooooot of homework to get even a B. An A if you were a computer working 247 at the library.

Tough instructor. Never recommend to future students. He was always confident with his hard homework but actually his homework was copied from other universities, such as Stanford and UMass. He is also a weird guy since he enjoys contradicting to himself. Cannot understand why this guy can be hired by NYU Courant.

Tons of homeworks with little instructions, and the scope is way beyond what is covered in lecture notes. Extremely intelligent man. It's not easy to earn an A, but if you do you really feel like you have earned it. Pay attention, take good notes, do the homeworks yourself, and think like a computer scientist and you'll do fine in this class.

This professor impressed at the beginning. He was always on time, and also talked about stat-of-the-art computer science research. But deeply frustrated after the first test. It's hard to recover to A level once you failed at the beginning. His grading was tough. Sometimes he was not patient during his office hours. I will avoid him.

The toughest and most abstract course you will ever take.  Johnstone makes it even tougher.  Acts like this is the most important class ever when it is utterly useless to most of us.  Tests are really tough and he expects you to know way more than you should. Still, he's a nice guy and has some interesting points.

Keep a tally of how many times he adjusts his belt/crotch during class. I dare you. Besides that, you had better study. For CS250, TAKE RANELLI. For CS350, be on top of the material, because he will make you feel like a d.a. if you don't, in front of the whole class.
This is a good teacher, but he assumes you already know more than you do.  His tests are very difficult.

Johnstone really knows his information and does a good job of explaining extremely difficult information in understandable terms. However when you do not understand a concept he will ask you questions to try to lead you instead of just answering the question...not always helpful. When he BUGS HIS EYES OUT, pay attention, it will be on the test haha
Numerical computing AND graphics. Excellent instructor; presents material well. Is helpful but could could work on social skills. Still, I recommend any course taught by him.
Johnstone is a very arrogant man. He often compares his 350 class to his middle school child. He thinks he's god's gift to computer science. I think he's just a jerk.

First of all its just a bad class in general. Very abstract concepts that are boring and don't always seem to apply to anything. As far as he goes, I found him pompous and arrogant. He taught the material well (much better than the awful book) but I generally disliked him as person.

This is a difficult and abstract course. Dr. Johnstone presents the material very well. Try your best to keep up at the beginning of the semester. It will make the later part much easier. The homework is important!! Skip it and you are almost guaranteed to fail. Review you homework before the test. Skipping class is not recommended.
He is a good professor over all
good

JJ is a smart guy who hates dumb kids, and thats about it. If you think you are a little slow with understanding new material, do not take him... oh and the frequent crotch fiddling is quite distracting too.
Teaching methods rock...he expects you to be on the ball at _all_times. by all means, take johnny johnstone

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock

Graded by 2 easy projects, HWs, and exams. Does not finish the entire course on time, and he may cram towards the end. Double-check his answer keys for mistakes. YT is a great resource.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Didn't learn much really. He knows his stuff is just a bad teacher. Made me go to his office hours just to tell me since I was a non traditional student I wasn't going to be good for the program. Wish he followed a book so we could of at least looked at examples
rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Read the reviews. You're not going to learn anything from this man. He's a terrible teacher 20 years behind modern tech who rambles for half the class. You have to ask questions 2-3 times to get a coherent answer. He doesn't want to do any work so he gives easy As to make students like him. I've had him 3 times and he's the same every time.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Textbook is free, he does ramble in his class sessions. There are less than 5 assignments (2 coding, 3 exams). Due dates given when he gives the assignment (2-ish weeks prior to due date).

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: I have taken a lot of Dr. Chiang's classes and while he is very nice he is very, very hard to follow. He constantly get sidetracked and also writes over the top of slides so the lectures are impossible to read. I pass his classes fine, but I can't confidently say I have ever learned anything. Prepare to learn on your own.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Unfocused lectures with many unrelated, personal anecdotes. As a result, class quickly lags behind syllabus schedule, so tests will cover chapter(s) not yet discussed. He legitimately struggles using a PC, so he often forgets to share screen with online class and often talks off microphone. Openly laughs about not responding to emails. The worst.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Dr. Chiang is a good guy. He is always willing to answer questions, however he does not get straight to the point, and will leave you still a bit confused. rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: He is funny and kind. However he talks fast and covers the board, asks questions and answers himself, goes on tangents and its difficult to follow. He also turns on the projector over his notes so it is so difficult to pay attention to either. You can't see the program or the notes. He tries. not bad, but not the best.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Cheerful, even funny sometimes. He knows his stuff.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: I had Chiang for Data Structures. I already learned the subject on my own so the class was very easy and boring. Chiang is the worst programmer and teacher in Computer Science at UALR. university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Dr. Chiang is probably the worst professor in UALR's computer science department. His English is broken and he goes on weird tangents during lectures, although he can be a riot at times. If you go to him for help, don't bother because he'll just waste your time. The tests are weighted too heavily. Don't take him if you can.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: He admitted to relearning Assembly Language while we were taking the class. Besides the incoherence of the third project, the class went along smoothly. He curves every test and will still give you an A if you have an 88%. He can at times be difficult to understand because of his accent, but he tries and usually gets his points across fine.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: Had him for Programming 1 and Assembly. Very helpful teacher who will always go the extra mile to help. Goes along with the textbooks in class but homework and projects comes from him. Tests come from his homework and projects. Sometimes hard to understand and might it take a few reiterations for him to understand a question.

university: University of Arkansas at Little Rock
review_text: He likes to ask tricky questions in class, but tests come from the lecture/slides.

If you're just here to learn then take the class, the material is very interesting. But man the grading was rough I swear the people in this class are smarter than the average cs class the median was always so high in the quizzes...
Fun class, interesting concepts, and he is one of my faves.

Professor El Abbadi is a joy to have a class with. He genuinely cares about the material and is great at answering questions. His lectures are quite interesting, and the class is mainly project based which helps you learn. TAKE HIM IF YOU ARE ABLE!!!

please please please take this class with Professor El Abbadi before he stops teaching it. It is incredibly clear how much he cares for his students and his material - the class is interesting, and you can expect fair (not too hard) items on his quizzes. He is passionate when teaching and responds very quickly on piazza. Great guy, great class!!!
One of the top CS classes at UCSB. Assignments are fun, lectures are enjoyable, and midterm/final are replaced by quizzes.

This class was extremely enjoyable, although it was a decent amount of work I still found the material and lectures digestable. Abbadi is super enthusiastic and makes lecutures interactive. The projects were the best CS assignments I have ever had and you write everything on your own and can choose the language to do it in. AMAZING, must take.

I have never felt as passionate about a class as much as this. I hate learning a bunch of abstract concepts that I feel like I'll never use again, but the structure of the class forces you to put the concepts you use into practice, which makes you understand them on a much deeper level. Straight up sparked the joy in learning in me that I lost.

Great class that actually made me care about distributed systems. The projects were genuinely difficult for the first time in college I enjoyed every single obstacle that I encountered while doing them. 4 quizzes in total instead of a final/midterm allowed me to stay on top of the material periodically rather than cramming and helped for retention.

A pretty solid prof who is excited about the material, gives good examples, and is very responsive to questions during lecture. Attending lecture is very important though, and I made the final much harder for myself by missing the last few.
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

review_text: Took 171 with Amr last Spring. As an alum, I can easily say he was my favorite professor. I often struggled to connect with the material in my cs classes, but Amr made the topic of distributed systems engaging and just fun. Even projects seemed more like solving riddles than actual homework. Undisputed GOAT.
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

review_text: Loved this professor. Programming assignments are challenging but very interesting. Tests are tough but completely fair. You learn how to use a lot of data structures and it can get a bit confusing. Take good notes (especially on time complexity), do the homework, and start the projects early and you'll do fine.

Amr is such a great guy, and he is super passionate about the subject. The programming assignments and tests were pretty difficult, but I feel like I learned a lot from this class. Overall, I would highly recommend.

During my journey through UCSB's undergrad, I have had very few CS professors that have showed me the true joys of learning; Professor Abbadi is one of those few. If you see that he's teaching a course, take it. Go to lecture and start the PAs early. His tests are hard, but not unfair. Overall, one of the best in the CS department; top 5 easily.
Great professor. Projects involve a lot of moving parts in your code, so get started early to catch bugs (because there's going to be a lot)

He's a pretty good lecturer, makes the material interesting and gives good explanations. You can tell he's passionate about the subject. Wish he was a little clearer on his projects; some of them lacked detail and required some revisions/additional details by the TAs, but with the help of the TAs all of them were doable.

Better learn those useless proofs; be prepared to not be able to apply things you learned. If you are looking for a real distributed systems class that will prepare you for the workplace, you won't find it here. Lectures are boring. The professor expertly avoids useful distributed systems concepts to make sure the students get little out of it.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

review_text: This entire class is 3 projects and 3 tests. The projects aren't terribly difficult, but the tests are. He expects you to know the details of proofs.

Excellent professor, explained data structures in a more succinct and elegant way than I had seen previously. Projects were a lot of fun. Tests were a bit more challenging than expected but that did not take away from the overall value of the course. 10/10 would take again

He explains clearly and goes at a slow enough pace so that if you space out for a little while you're not completely lost. He gives weekly quizzes that force you to keep up with the material but aren't bad if you come to lecture and do the (not graded) homework assignments.
Awesome class
Really good lectures
Prof. Amr El Abbadi was an AMAZING lecturer and very patient. (very rare in academia). I am a fairly slow learning and I appreciated that he took the time out to help me. Highly recommend.

Being someone with disabilities, she was very understanding. She is willing to help anyone but you have to do your part as well. The attendance rule sucks but I haven't had to read the book at all! She has very simple power point& they are easy to follow. She tells you that the class is hard and it can be. Just do every out lab& you will do fine.

She would not give my grade in her office. I'm suspicious that she gives what grade she wants at the end. She doesn't answer questions in class or labs. In lab sections, she is just sitting in lab, it is better if she does not attend labs. So that we will be and feel comfortable with TAs. Quizzestest are sloppy, graded inaccurately.
One of the best teacher i have ever had
Defenitely the best Computer Science Teacher and one of my favorite teacher
She is an awful teacher and I am afraid that she will drive students away from the computer science field because she has no clue!
Very sweet, fair, smart lady. Refreshingly bubbly personality. Enthusiastic. One of my favorite CSCI Professors!
Dr. Sun is wonderful.  She's very helpful, she makes study guides for her exams, and she doesn't give a lot of homework.  I recommend taking her for anything you can.

The previous comment was to say the least unfair, hateful and most likely an outlet for frustrations of a failing student. Dr. Sun is a very nice, knowledgeable professor and knows computer graphics well. She will answer all your questions in regards to the tests and assignments both in the classroom or her office. Excellent test preparation guide.
No Comments

Very informative class. All content is prerecorded lectures from 2009, and class consists of daily quizzes and in class assignments that are collaborative. Exams were not cumulative and accurately represent content in class. Overall, I would recommend this professor, but there are things that could be improved.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

review_text: I would recommend course for every PhD student. The course requires us to read, analyze, and present scientific papers. No exams. Instead, we did a semester-long project, with reports on our progress instead of exams. Yes, the course is not easy, but this makes us stronger researchers.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback

review_text: I have seen this guy tell people that they needed to quit rather than help them.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: A very intensive flipped class, where you come to the lecture prepared. There is a quiz before the lecture starts, graded in-class activity, separated hands-on assignments, and two midterms plus the final. BUT you will benefit a lot and the lesson recap by Dr. Gowtham Atluri are insightful.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

review_text: He is approachable. thanks!

I had him for IDA, overall he was a nice professor that makes sure you understands the material if you ask questions. It's a lot of work as you have to watch about and hour and half of videos before each lecture. However, the videos are very helpful and gowtham does a nice job going over what the videos don't cover. The tests are easy if you study.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Participation matters

review_text: Good course materials. You will learn a LOT from this course. By a lot, you probably will forget some of them already after you take your final. Be prepared that it asks for a lot of prerequisites, Linear Algebra > Probability > Statistics > Calculus. Professor is very accessible either in class or off class.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Test heavy

review_text: Dr. Alturi taught a very math heavy course, and at times it was hard to follow. With that said, he is very dedicated to making sure that his students understand what is going on. If you are willing to ask questions, he will put in the time to make sure you understand what is going on.

Syllabus - only research papers. No assignments, no midterms, no final exams, but a project you work alone on. So, I couldn't learn very well as this is new field.  Needed: assignments, group project, depth instead of breadth.  For project in 3 months, he wanted us to implement something better than research of other experts which took them years

I found lectures to be very interesting, and I thought the professor did a good job explaining complex topics. My least favorite part of this course was the homework and exams; they dealt heavily with various throughput and packet loss calculations. I found these quite tricky and struggled heavily on the exams as a result.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Worst class I've ever taken. The professor seems determined to make students fail and feel miserable. He gives one impossibly hard example in class, then makes the exam 1000x harder. One question can be a giant diagram worth most points — if you don't know it, you're done. No real learning, just stress. Most students drop after the first exam.
Concentrate on his lectures. That's all. Amazing professor, lenient grading.
Take the course if you really like to have in-depth knowledge on certain topics Prof. Brahma covers. Inspired me to take 6043 after I took his undergrad level course.
Professor's lectures are all we had to care about. He explains brilliantly. I am a fan of his classes.

Mandatory attn. for a 6:00pm night class--he showed up late every session and held us late 30+ min every time (you must respect his time, he never respects yours). All assignments required to be turned in on paper and they're 2-3qs so if you fail a problem, you're screwed. Hyper-focuses on turning networking into a math class unnecessarily.

Dr. Brahma's lectures are slow-paced. The examples he provides in class are trivial compared to the questions given on homeworks and exams. Dr. Brahma is a lackluster, unnecessarily difficult professor. Basic networking is pretty simple, but Dr. Brahma turns basic networking into a math class.
Grading was adjusted with a curve. Some evaluation methods, such as handwritten React code, emphasized theory more than practical application.

Bart forces freshman CS majors to use his terrible "Webz" curriculum for CISC181. The textbook is riddled with typos and errors, the homeworks are unnecessarily complicated, and the final project with Webz is horribly designed. It seems Bart wants to push his terrible curriculum on students instead of using regular HTML, CSS, and Typescript.

He is definitely condescending at times but only if you are asking dumb questions. He is the best computer science professor I have had. There is one homework a week and it is pretty easy. I am surprised with how structured this class was considering this was the first year they taught TypeScript. The final project was DIFFICULT.
Although the class was quite easy, Bart is a very terrible professor. He is very condescending and gets annoyed at every little thing.

I just really don't like him. The class is really boring, sorry, and he's honestly a bit insufferable. He acts condescending and makes you feel dumb for not understanding a new concept. He's previously had good reviews, but who knows, maybe he fell off.

I took this class as an intro class for CISC 108, and it honestly made my first semester so hard: There was way too much homework each week, and for this I would recommend going to the TA's as they really are the ones teaching this course too you, since Bart really does not teach at all. His lectures are pointless, but you must still go.

Online textbook for 108 is set up very well so if you stay on top of the assignments, you'll do well. It's easy to get a good grade if you get the assignments done early and give yourself time to ask any questions. Bart can come across rather condescending and passive aggressive when you ask him a question, as if he feels you should already know.

Took Honors 108 - Easy class but could have learned everything online. Lectures and labs are useless but attendance to them is mandatory. Bart himself is arrogant and has a huge ego. Acts hostile to students who ask questions and expects us to know everything already. Also makes you use his awful python libraries he designed himself for some reason

Dr. Bart made this class a very good introduction for people who don't really know anything about computer science. Very friendly and passionate about the content. Lectures are more focused on the collaborative/community aspects of CS rather than course content. Had condescending comments here or there but approachable overall. Super reachable too.

The best way I can describe him is the kid in high school that got bullied and now takes his anger out on students. Very condescending and passive aggressive almost all the time. 108 is an entry level course, but he treats it like a postgraduate course. Not bad at teaching, but his attitude is terrible.

Dr. Bart was a great professor! His lectures were easy to follow and there was plenty of practice for the concepts we learned on BlockPy (which he created). I already had programming experience before this class so it wasn't hard for me, but even if you are coming in with no experience, you'll do fine if you do the work and practice the concepts.

Lectures and discussions were almost completely useless, most of the class it taught through the online textbook. I found the exams to be quite easy and Bart did seem to be very helpful when asked questions. The online textbook was a bit of helpful and homework was also very easy.

Everyone speaks highly of Bart so I was looking forward to this class but he doesn't really teach a lot, his feedback is mixed with passive aggressive comments and the grades I earned were basically because of what I had learned over high-school.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Only problem I really had with him was sometimes I felt like there was too much work to do in one week. But even then, I would always get the work done well and on time. He is fairly accessible outside of class and very helpful when you need it. Exams are super easy if you do all the work.

The class material was interesting and I enjoyed learning it. However, the professor could be passive aggressive about the littlest things and just inconsistent with grading. A lot of people in the class had heard he was great and we were kind of surprised by how the class turned out.

Bart's class is a perfect example of an accessible class. Help is easy, re-doing an exam is made simple, the assignments are fair, and you do not need to learn how to code in order to do well in the course.  Definetly a good intro course.

I absolutely loved Dr. Bart! He really cares about his students and their success; he provides every opportunity to get help and makes deadlines and assignments crystal clear. His class was super organized and effective in teaching Python basics.

Dr. Bart is a very good professor and can help you anytime with work. His program HE MADE, BlockPy is very well made with no bugs and can help you learn how to code with a built in blocks view. The TAs were very helpful as I would go to them for help with the homework.  Overall, very, very good professor and I want to have him again.
Dr. Bart was overall a good professor, but I also did not interact with him too much either. The class was pretty easy as long as you stayed on top of your assignments.
its a lot of work but its manageable as long as you manage ur time and are willing to do it. if you don't do the assignments yourself, you will fail. do the assignments.

I loved his course! He not only shows compassion for his students but is willing to go one step further to help answer questions and make sure we know what we're doing in the course itself to help further our future. He also really cares about you doing well in the course. If you put in the time and effort, go to OH, you'll pass with flying colors.
Dr. Bart is a solid professor who really does care about his students. CISC108 is a lot of work, some of it is easy but all very time-consuming. Highly recommend utilizing office hours

I had a medical leave after attending class for 2 months and Dr.Bart was really supportive. His class is also pretty easy, as long as you do your own work you will get an A. There are a lot of TAs to support you and they were very responsive on Discord too. Overall I would take his class again

Professor Bart is clearly passionate about Computer Science education and it shows with how he interacts with students. This class is homework heavy and might be a little bit of a struggle if you are not passionate about the content. It is clear that he and the rest of the course staff care about you understanding the material and passing the class
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

review_text: Dr. Bart was a nice professor but his lectures weren't really helpful in terms of the syllabus. It's a beginner-friendly class and extensions were easily granted. There were office hours every day. They genuinely want you to learn. Lots of homework but GO TO OFFICE HOURS. Also, make your cohort group your study buddies.

I had Dr. Bart for intro to computer science and although he used the phrase "danger noodle" on the homework one time I still enjoyed my experience. The content is easy if you are passionate about it.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Online Savvy

review_text: Dr. Bart was concise during lecture, is always willing to help others in class or during office hours and checks in on the class to see how the class is doing and regrades assignments if he makes mistakes. However, I did not like was no guidance when reading the tome he created (explain at start of class can help a lot). Overall great professor!

This class was pretty good. The course started out pretty easy but around week 3 or 4 it picked up and it became very easy to fall behind and become confused. Dr. Bart and the TAs are very accessible outside of class so if you need help definitely go to office hours.
rating_tags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Took Dr. Bart's class when we were online with COVID, but he was the best professor I think I've ever had. He's super reasonable and is really great at explaining things if you're having a hard time. His class is hard, but in a good way where it challenges you just enough but isn't super easy. I would definitely take another class of his.

This is the best class I think I've ever taken. The way the class is set up on canvas makes it so theres a lot of days where lecture is really just a work period but dr bart really tries to help every student that needs it and some that may need it but dont ask. He comes up with fun creative assignments that make you care about cs. Great dude fr

Honestly, a pretty solid guy in general. The course is pretty project heavy in terms of coding, has a lot of homework, and you can easily fall behind if you don't keep up with the weekly modules. Can be a bit condescending at times, but not intentionally. He's also extremely understanding about medical/mental health problems!!

Bart is a great intro to comp sci teacher - his lectures and modules are great at explaining concepts that are easy to understand. There is a decently high amount of homework activities, but they are mostly pretty easy and you get a lot of time to do them. There are optional assignments for extra credit

As another review stated, I am also quite surprised at his low rating. Starts at the very basics slowly  extremely supportive with beginners. Clear lectures, often pauses for and encourages questions, understanding and responsive. Ask questions on Discord, lenient deadlines. Read modules carefully and apply your learning, info relevant to exams.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

review_text: Easily the best of the best, I'm surprised his rating on here is quite low. Not only is he a great teacher, but he's fun to be around, talk to, and even geek out about computer science stuff with. If you're on the CISC Discord he is open to help you out almost the entire week. Also very lenient with material and due dates.

Dr. Bart is one of the best. Really lenient with all of the work. You by default get a week extension on all of the work. All hw is due on Sunday, no quizzes, just projects. You can work in groups if you want. Honestly, don't even need to go to lectures to do the work. Labs are easy as well. Overall, a really fun class. Midterms are decent too.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Bart is a great professor for beginners. Computer Science is a hard subject in general, but Dr. Bart does a great job of truly making it an intro course. Definitely take him if you need a CISC credit.

I hated Bart while taking him freshman year, but then I realized how good of a professor he was. I came in with coding experience, but I could see how this class would be really hard for those who have not. He teaches the information very well as well as organizes the class efficiently. On the other hand, he makes you feel stupid when asking q's.

The course seems well set up and thought out so there is no questions about when assignments are due or how to pace assignments. When asked questions, they would be answered as well in a way that is understandable. Some may need to go to office hours and ask questions to be successful. Much of the work must be done both in and out of class.

He was really nice and understanding of the fact that people were at different levels and doing school during a pandemic is tough. His modules were really well related with the coursework and he took a lot of effort and time in to putting that together. I would definetly reccomend him.

Dr. Bart puts an incredible amount of time, effort, and care into his courses. If you reach out to him, he'll absolutely help you out if you are struggling. He's an incredible human being with a very unique and fun spin on computer science. Plus, his corgis are excellent.

Took this during the pandemic and was my first class at UD. Really glad I ended up with this guy. I could see how it would be hard if you're a complete beginner to code, but if you have prior experience, then it's a really easy class. Dr. Bart was really understanding and you were able to miss a few lectures for mental health without a doc's note.

Lectures were extremely boring. Grading scale was very vague throughout the year and added to the uncertainties and disorganization of the class. If you have the option I highly recommend taking a different teacher. His setup for online class is highly dis functional.

I liked Dr Bart a lot as a professor. He is very knowledgable in computer science. Granted, most of the lectures were boring and I ended up not going to them and not directly watching them, since they weren't mandatory. If you know a lot about computer science, this should be an easy A. If you don't, the class might be a little more challenging.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--Hilarious

review_text: Took the course during fall 2020 online. He gives out lots of homework that has to be self taught. He implies that you have to go to office hours and it seems pivotal to your grade. If you're not teaching yourself this course will be very hard, especially since none of the lectures had any correlation to the course work.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Participation matters

review_text: He didn't adapt well to the online format. His grading is really harsh, he was really petty when it came to points. He seemed kind of new to teaching. I would avoid taking him if you can.

Wasn't really taught much in lecture. Lectures had little to nothing to do with the content of the class. Be ready to teach yourself all of the material. I would recommend taking a different professor.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Easy but have to teach yourself everything

Took him remotely for fall 2020. Says that your grade shown on canvas is only the percentage of assignments you completed and that he would change them at the end to reflect a proper grade. He never does this. Doesn't provide good feedback and you basically have to teach yourself the material.

Dr. Bart was great this semester for CISC 108. I would definitely recommend him. The one thing I will say about this class is make sure you utilize Dr. Bart's and the TA's office hours. They are both very helpful. Stay on top of the workload and you will do fine!

Dr. Bart was an amazing first year professor. Don't get me wrong computer science isn't easy so don't go in expecting that but if you are eager to learn it he does a great job at in depth, effective teaching while doing fun assignments. Hes an amazing understanding person all around and truly cares about his students and I 100% would take him again
Prof Bart was great as my first comp sci professor at UD! He clearly cares about learning and is always available for questions even with a lot of students.

When something isn't working he changes it and asks his students what about the course is helpful and what isn't. He cares about the students' performances and wellbeing. He's very chill, he's always excited to help, doesn't make you feel bad for getting a question wrong, and he encourages you when you get it right. But there's a lot of work.
dr bart is a great guy. extremelty helpful when you need help and runs the class well. there is a discord and office hours for help 24/7.
Dr. Bart is a great professor, he takes the time to make sure you understand and is always available for questions! He was very understanding during the pandemic and overall I 100% recommend.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Bart is fantastic! He's reasonable and cares about his students. He expects you to put in work but gives chances to recover your grade. If you're behind, he'll help you get where you need to be. You can tell that his main priority is his students' education. He gives lots of small assignments but they're not too bad and help you stay on track.

Did a great job of structuring the class (both before and after the COVID shutdown). Definitely cares about his students, took the time to try to learn all 60 of our names. The material is covered in both lectures and on Canvas, so it's easy to review and practice. Really an awesome professor and a great human.

Took his class during the coronavirus pandemic. Not easy to take remotely. I feel that he tries hard to teach well, but the pace he moves at is very fast for most. If you don't actively try to learn/program in your own time -- you will fail. I'm not taking comp sci anymore, but he's a great professor if you're serious about programming.

Dr. Bart is a good professor, he wanted everyone to do well in his class. there was one policy I didn't like: your exam grade couldn't be a letter grade higher than your overall grade in the class. I thought was that kind of unfair because I completed all of the coursework for the class, but I did not pass the exam which caused me to not pass.

He gives a number of projects, but they are interesting and really make you think. That is usually how his advice goes as well: he gives you help, but ultimately makes you figure out the answer. That being said, he is not the easiest professor, but he cares about his students succeeding and learning skills that will be used in the future.

Loved this class! Bart is great at teaching without just lecturing all the time. We actually do stuff in class. Lots of small homework assignments that you can get a good grade on pretty easily if you try, and you get to make your own video games! I learned enough in this course that I can make games for fun now!

Very caring professor that wants his students to succeeded, friendly, pretty funny during his lectures. Overall this class is perfectly structured for an introduction class, his modules and lessons are designed to throughly explain programming concepts and very helpful for a beginner. Had him during the COVID-19 pandemic and he was very caring.

Great professor who actually cares. Does offer extra credit sometimes and makes lectures more involved and interesting than just straight talking. Only gripe is that he assigns a loot of small assignments, not hard, but sometimes you don't expect them. Keep your eye on Canvas.

Dr. Bart is probably one of the better teachers you will get in the CS department that actually cares about you learning, knowing, and understanding the material. He does make you work for your grade, but it is a worthwhile class. He makes material interesting because typically algorithms is not an exciting course.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Really funny professor. Had him for the honors section of CISC108. Very straight forward, can learn a lot even if you never programmed before. Gives a couple of cool projects. No clear grading guidelines, but he isn't a harsh grader. Good professor, would take again.

I'm glad he was my first computer science teacher. He explains the material really well. The only issue is that he's strict and offers no leniency in terms of grading. One of my friends lost points when they asked him to reconsider his grade. Thankfully the TAs and autograder grades almost everything. Also, the projects are very difficult.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Not a hard class if you are able to start your work early. You basically teach yourself through videos he has prerecorded. He is caring and pushes back a lot of due dates in the beginning of the semester. He doesn't do this towards the end of the semester which can through some people off. Do not procrastinate his assignments and projects!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Dr. Bart is a great professor that likes to challenge students, while introducing them to programming. He allows you to work with a partner on most assignments and makes sure youre getting work done on time in the course. He is also very understanding when it comes to fair grading and due dates. Take this class if you have any interest in comp sci
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

review_text: Very inspirational guy, cares deeply about his class

25% of the grade are open note quizzes with unlimited attempts and programming problems, exams are super easy, he explains stuff very well during lectures and is super helpful when you ask him some questions. Exams are really easy, they mostly consist of the questions from homework just altered a little bit. Great class, great professor!!!

Dr. Bart is an excellent teacher and probably the best thing to happen to the Computer Science department recently. He has a real passion for teaching and really wants to see his students succeed. He extremely accessible, and very reasonable when it comes to makes ups and that sort of thing. 108 can be a lot of work, but its worth taking with him.

Dr. Bart was my first ever computer science teacher. He was very good at explaining the basics of computer science in a way that I could easily understand. As long as you keep up with the modules you will be set. He's an awesome guy who loves his wife and Corgis. He also cares about you and will be extra understanding if you show engagement in clas

He's honestly pretty reasonable, but seems a bit new to teaching. Sometimes his explanations are way more confusing than they need to be, but the class is mostly like CISC220 review so if you did well there you'll be fine. Homeworks are set on a schedule, which I find frustratingly inflexible, but do them early and definitely talk to the TA.

If you are taking Algorithms with this professor be prepared to study. He assigns hard homework problems each week but they were graded by the TAs which is a sigh of relief compared to the way he grades his exams. He hates "point-grubbing" and apparently, that means if he graded something inaccurately that means you get no points at all. Avoid Him.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

review_text: Dr. Bart has been the most amazing teacher. He works with the university itself to give the best coding experience. He is the reason I am so passionate about coding today and definitely is able to personalize his teacher ability when in a 1 on 1 with him.

He's the most dedicated and nuanced professor and instructor that I've ever had. Typically uses the most specific and understandable language for this class. Other students and I hated DrRacket for it being outdated, but I appreciated learning a function-oriented language and recursion-focused approach that I had not gotten before in other CISCs.
Nice professor, moderate amount of work in CISC 108. Racket was the worst part but I think they removed it now. Python portion of the course was much much easier.

Dr. Bart does expect a lot of work, but it is reasonable. He also allows his students to bring a cheat sheet to each of the exams which helps a lot. Make sure to do your work every week or you will be behind and struggle. The TAs are available frequently, so don't be afraid to ask questions like I was.

He is really well-versed in his courses. He loves to answer as many questions as students want. Exams and labs are easy and straightforward if we just attend his lectures. The fact that we are also allowed to have cheat sheets on exams made this course less stressful. Overall, he is really a good professor and worth taking for CISC320.
Easy comp sci classes, lets you have a cheat sheet on the tests

Great teacher. You are allowed to bring notes to exams. Doing labs help a lot in getting A. Exams are easy. You will get more than 90% on an exam if you do the practice questions which he will post one week before every exam. He is very helpful during office hour.

Dr. Bart is good at explaining difficult concepts and is very open to student feedback. Exams are a bit challenging, but not impossible. We have short assignments due every class, but they are usually interesting scenarios. There are a few longer programming assignments that are much more difficult. Overall, recommend for CISC320.
rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Dr. Bart is a great professor, ignore the other comments! He cares and listens to our feedback (we complained that the labs were too hard and he took the time to make them easier for us). He's not the warmest professor I've ever had, but he does try to be funny and genuinely wants people to succeed! Not his fault the course is hard for freshman.

Maybe it is because it is his first year teaching, but Dr. Bart does not know how to teach this class. This is an INTRO level class but he completely skipped the basics. I spend about 12 hours outside of class doing labs and trying to teach myself out of the book. Recommend taking with a different professor if possible.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: Teach nothing
He is a respected professor. However, his exams were much harder than I expected, didn't align with the homework, and the time pressure made it difficult to finish.

Lectures can get boring but that depends on what your interests are. Overall not a very difficult course, if you attend all lectures, do your hw by yourself and put in light study hours you'll do well.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: His Exams were super difficult, expect anything he might include even if he didn't explain it before!
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Great professor, listen to his lectures and the midterm and finals will be very easy.

Many of my classmates and I learned practically nothing from this class. He's a nice guy, but a terrible teacher. His exams are impossible to study for, and insanely hard. Every project he gives is found online. He didn't grade anything, so you don't know your grade until the semester is over and final grades are due. Overall, very stressful class.

I was the one who gave him a 4 on December 5th, 2022, and honestly looking back, He deserves a 2 Idk what kinda drug I was on. I really learned everything from Geeks-For-Geeks, not his class. He grades everything in the last 2 hours of the grading window for the semester. The stuff in class isn't like the exams at all. Overall he is a D-tier prof.

I really liked Shen as my Professor although his tests are VERY difficult and impossible to study for. As long as you participate/pay attention in class, the HW assignments are easy and you will take away a lot from the class. All of the programming assignments are on Geeks-for-Geeks website so go there if you need help. He is slow to grade things
Very fun intro to comp sci class. Geared toward people who know nothing about coding at all. I knew nothing going into it and learned at a very good pace throughout the semester. 10/10
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Shen only teaches through animated, Comic Sans PowerPoints. For my friends and I, this class was a real chore. He spends A LOT of time on simple topics, and then breezes through semaphores. He was okay, but I really didn't learn much in this class besides what I knew. Coronavirus or not, this class was much the same. He cares though, and that helps
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

review_text: My friend took courses with him and he only had nice things to say; the professor is accessible outside class, recommended books and researches that are not relevant to courses. Gives more homework though!
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

review_text: Professor Shen is my favorite Computer Science professor thus far. He is very intelligent and passionate about the subjects he teaches. His assignments are challenging and the exams are even more difficult, but if you make an honest effort and invest the time required you will get an A. Show up to lectures or you'll regret it come exams.
Explain the material well, but the exam is really tough. You have to pay attention to his class since he sometimes would tell you what things will be appear in the exam.

The first thing I notice is that Professor Shen is a hard working professor. He is smart, humorous, and he gives positive feedback. He gave me a lot of good advice about my project from last year, and I appreciate his help a lot. He also worked to promote computer networks and computer science in general to children, a plus!

Shen's OS class was very tough. Exams are open everything (including laptop), but they are still hard to pass. Shen's lectures and assignment directions ar usually unclear and changes the directions multiple times when the assignment is out. That being said, he is a nice person, cares about the students and is helpful during office hours.

Very dedicated professor. Professor Shen gives very positive and constructive feedback to my research. The communication was a joy, too. I believe every student who has done research with professor Shen would appreciate his expertise on the topic and such positive feedback. Highly recommend!

I like Professor Shen! He is is a very cool professor. He has a lot of research ideas on computer networks and distributed sensor networks, and love to share his thoughts with students. I respect Professor Shen not only because of his knowledge, but also because the way he treats student with real respect. Besides, he is very nice and considerate:)

Material itself isnt that hard at all, but his projects were pretty tough but the key is to START THEM EARLY and get his help. 1/2 of your final grade comes from projects and in the end thats what saves you from his insane tests even though they are open book.
Challenging, but fun, projects. Nice guy in general.
YOU WILL HATE HIM. Test Tip: Write absolutely anything on a test question.
Projects take a bit of work, but Shen's a nice guy.  Class boring, not too hard
Thinks he's Gods Gift.  Projects are a beast.  Test are hard but he grades easy
Lots of work, but a deffinate A.   Doesn't know C++ or english for that matter.
Curves nicely, usually understandable & straightforward, projects are alright
Lectures are just rehashes of the textbook. Assignments were difficult.
Thank God he curves!  Teaches straight from the book.  Doesn't speak english.
Insanely hard exams & projects 'til he renormalizes the grades. Lectures useless
Teaches from book; boring; doesn't know C++ well (knows java); loves recursion
Dr. Lena is one of the best professors I've had! Her teaching style is clear, engaging, and fun, with great in-class activities!

She is an incredible instructor who makes learning both engaging and approachable. The course was packed with activities like auctions and game demonstrations, which were fun and effective at illustrating key concepts. She creates a welcoming environment, knows everyone's name, and is always open to questions.
She is an amazing prof; I enjoyed learning from her class this semester!

Highly skilled but covered only introductory material. More depth on distributed systems would be valuable, as there was ample time. Frequent illnesses led to poor TA-led classes. An open project was unnecessary; a programming assignment would be more suitable. She sometimes appeared irritated with questions.

Really easy class. If you participate and listen you will learn a lot. But I do agree that the lectures are boring. However she's really sweet extremely caring. There's easy extra credit. Exams are also easy. Final project also easy. The TA sucked which didn't help cause they grade everything.

The lectures are very slow and boring, but you can see that she really tries. I can tell that she is very nice and cares a lot, but teaching just might not be her thing. The material is sort of easy, but this course really lacks application, of which she asks of you for a final coding project. I along with the whole class was very confused on this.
Professor has a beautiful smile, she is very understanding and flexible and wants to help, but you need to ask. Course is very comprehensive, Hadoop and Spark are two most useful part for me.

Expect to put a lot of effort on the course but you'll learn A LOT! You don't need the text book, she did not require it once. Class starts out easy, BUT gets tougher and she tends to quickly cover things. She has a sweet personality though. Project is tough, make sure you pair up with someone good. TA is good, helped a lot on HW assignments.
Professor Lena's knowledge is remarkable. Very organized course and awesome lectures.
Nice Professor, Professor is responsive, we needed more time on project and she extended it for us. Also had an awesome TA, she answered every single question from me, wish every TA were like her

I've never written a review about a professor before and I'm only writing this because Lena is the worst professor I've ever had. She is a horribly boring lecturer and most of the material is easily learned through Google. The final project she assigns is outrageously difficult compared to the rest of the course material. Would never take again.

I had a lot of fun taking Lena's class.  think she would make a great advisor. I was working on a research project and couldn't go past the math part  and reached out to her, she gave me a lot of useful advise.

I took her OS course last fall. She is amazing! She is gentle and elegant, and she smiles sweetly and loves to help. Best thing of all she remembers everyone's name and is willing to reward you for your hard work. Very good professor, I will definitely recommend her!

If you can take her class by any means, take her. She is nice and sweet and wants everyone to learn with a pleasant attitude. She responds to your email quickly, and is willing to help. She is a very responsible professor and oh don't forget to go to her office hours if you have questions!

Professor Mashayekhy is a very considerate and kind professor. She cares about students and provides all kinds of material on Sakai. She also gives review sessions for mid-term and finals which helps a lot for those exams. If you go to lectures, do your own homeworks, also understand all review questions, you will get A.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

review_text: This was a great course that deals with the core concepts of Machine Learning. The Professor has a deep understanding of the concepts and explains them in a very clear and interesting way. I definitely learned a lot from this course.

This course focuses on practical problem-solving rather than heavy math, making it accessible and engaging. The professor explains concepts clearly, and the projects are great for applying what you learn. Highly recommended!

Outstanding course delivery that communicates every piece of knowledge clearly, I enjoyed every class but the assignments were a bit heavy and complicated, and the class included quizzes and group projects and exams, while each part was given about the same amount of weight, it also meant that the same amount of effort was need for each part
The PPT will be tough to understand but the assignment are very helpful in learning about the subject.

This course was useful because it focused on the concepts instead of heavy math. The way he taught was really good. Quizzes and exams were a little tricky, but at least I have learned many points from them.
I took 2 ML courses prior to this course but found this very helpful. Professor changed my view on Models. Conveys the material clearly. This course helped me a lot that this was my last semester.

review_text: I took 2 ML courses prior to this course but found the professor discusses the ML models in a different and good way. Changed my view of Analyzing the ML Models. Helped me a lot since this was my last semester. Conveys the material clearly.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes

review_text: professor slang is the main problem. overall course content is too good.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

review_text: Professor Peng is very knowledgeable and passionate about his teaching. He's always nice and patient when students ask questions. On the final presentation day, he even prepared snacks for us, which is very nice. The course materials are well organized and easy to follow. I would say it's a wonderful experience taking his course!

Professor Peng's expectations are high and the material is complex. However, he is always available to answer questions after class and is accommodating for meetings outside of office hours. Be prepared to spend a significant amount of time on homework and researching the course material, especially if this is your first intensive ML class.

The course lectures are up-to-date reflecting current ML practices and research. I had the opportunity to learn ML concepts ranging from beginner to advanced levels. This comprehensive content helps you build a solid foundation while progressively addressing more complex topics with hands-on homework assignments.
I understood well in class. Learning with real life example was fun. He interacts as well in class and very nicely refreshes your previous lectures along the way.

I would have liked the classes to have been clearer. While the homework was beneficial, it often felt disconnected from the material covered in class, requiring additional effort to complete. Clarity regarding the expectations for the final project would have been appreciated.

I took his graduate ML class. Dr. Peng is very concerned that we should have a deep understanding of core machine learning concepts rather than regurgitate all the small details. As a graduate course, I also appreciated that Dr. Peng focused on relevant topics like GANs and meta-learning that are still receiving a lot of attention in ML research.

Most of his classes were going over slides that were prepared either by TA or some other professors. There were no exams at all and the final grade was based on just homework and the final project. The final project included presentation, reporting, and peer reviewing. Enjoyed the class with interesting topics but will not take this course again.

Grade is based only on 3 exams, which feels unfair and too high-stakes. Most grading was delegated to a TA whose standards were extremely strict with unclear/limited partial credit. Around 20% of the class failed. I strongly suggest you run away
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Grade is based only on 3 exams, which feels unfair and too high-stakes. Most grading was delegated to a TA whose standards were extremely strict with unclear/limited partial credit. Around 20% of the class failed.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Zhang was very knowledgable and was well prepared for class but he goes too fast during lectures to the point that I can't follow along. The slides don't have much information on them either so I found that the only way I could learn the material was by taking notes on the textbook which was time consuming, but the text was easy to understand.

One of the hardest classes I've taken at UD for little to no reason. He would go over examples which had little to no relevance to our homework, pull topics from things you didn't and will not learn, and give extremely difficult homework. Zhang takes what is normally a semi challenging class into a trial of your intelligence. Good luck!
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: He not necessarily a bad professor, he just taught Intro to Algorithms as if it were a graduate-level course. Most of the homework problems were extremely advanced problems pulled from the textbook, some of which were nearly impossible to figure out. The final exam was way easier than anything else in the course, though.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

review_text: For the first midterm of the class, there was a question worth 30% of the midterm. Only around 5 people got it right, and it felt like the teacher blamed *us*.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

review_text: I am pretty sure the first HW made half my class drop, this class is really hard and Zhang does not make it any better. Lectures are pointless and he assigned what I am sure was grad-level HW from a grad-level textbook. The HW really take 2 weeks to do, but the exams are easier than the HW. As long as you well get HW grades, you will pass.

I'll get straight to the point. The lectures for this class and topics were okay, the test were fair as well. Where this class fell apart heavily was the amount of homework given. This class is a hard class by design, however the amount of homework almost made it unbearable.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

review_text: MATH242 and MATH205 aren't marked as official prerequisites for this course, but they REALLY ARE if you take it with Professor Zhang. Expect to work out of a graduate level textbook that already assumes mastery of difficult concepts such as infinite series and random variables. If you're not well-versed with these, don't take Zhang's class.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

review_text: One of the better CS lecturers. If you pay attention in class and do the work and ask questions (office hours/email) he's very reasonable and helpful with explaining. Tests are straightforward and based off homework/lecture material. The work can be a lot though.

The  professor is very easygoing, in and after class, he is  happy to help students solve the problems. Class atmosphere is very active, learning content is relatively new and cutting edge. Homework is about summary, presentation and final paper. If you do not like the exam, this lesson is very suitable.

Professor Zhang was good. You can do all labs and homework with collaborators. Zhang uses slides to teach the material. Sometimes he just reads directly off them. If you study the slides you will do well on the exams. He is helpful and accessible during office hours.
Good professor overall. Lecture and homework are kind of hard, but the exam is fairly easy and provide extra credits.
Have fun learning things on your own! Because the lectures are hard to follow. And then comes quiz/exam time and the questions are much harder than the practice assignments!
rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

review_text: I enrolled in intro to algorithms with no experience in algorithms. I recently learned that we share the same assignments at CISC621 analysis and design of algorithms. I am hardly learning due to how hard the analysis and design is. I expected an introductory class.

This guy cannot convey course content in an understandable way at all. Talks straight through the lecture writing slanted down the chalkboard with his terrible handwriting and then hands you a pop quiz at the end. Can't answer a question in a helpful way for the life of him.
His lectures are hard to understand.
Machine learning is hard and this guy didn't make it any easier. Lectures are incredibly boring with nothing but mathematical proofs. GET READY TO TEACH YOURSELF!!

Hmm where should I begin? This professor is so disorganized in his class that you would find a hard time grasping what the lecture was about. He was also very mean in giving grades because a few of my classmates got low grades because he strictly followed the curve. Dont choose his class!

There are not many homework assignments, which can be nice, but also is the only way you learn anything. The lectures are very very theory-heavy. The midterm is not as much. Midterm was crazy difficult. Tong seemed okay as a professor, but I was perpetually lost the entire semester because he didn't explain things in an understandable manner.

review_text: I took Prof. Tong's Machine Learning course and have something to say. review_text: Professor Tong's knowledge is remarkable, and I had a lot of fun and learned a lot from this machine learning course. review_text: She's good at what she does

The professor explained the material clearly and made complex topics easy to follow also was very understanding with group work and deadlines. She gave us room to figure things out while still keeping us on track. Her flexibility made the course less stressful and helped us stay engaged without feeling overwhelmed.

Dr. Leila is sweet, but the classes are SO lecture heavy that they're nearly unbearable. The amount of information that we are expected to know is just absurd.
