The professor was very kind to students, although the idea was kinda hard to learn and sometimes boring. Group working was quite often so be involved

Incredibly nice and caring professor, but her dry lectures combined with mandatory attendance makes class a drag. review_text: Probably the most boring class I have ever taken here at UD. There's only one exam (midterm) so the entire first half of the class is just lectures. She is very hard to understand, mumbles frequently, and makes things that SHOULD be really easy concepts way more difficult than they should be. Attendance is taken every day and no laptops are allowed

Prof is really nice and the class isn't terribly hard. The lectures are boring and dry though and we mostly go through obvious things like what makes a good interface and survey design. She always sounds exasperated when presenting. Not much coding except for the final project, ok class.

Might be the worst lecturer on campus. She somehow manages to talk for 20 minutes without actually saying anything. She won't allow computers in class so you have nothing to pass the time. She won't tell us whats on the midterm, and has given us no method of efficiently studying for it. HCI might be the most boring class I've ever had.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Group projects

review_text: Very dull lectures, whole class is learnt via homework, if attendance wasnt mandatory there would be no reason to show up really

Not bad but 181 is essentially a self-taught course. Can provide ok feedback but don't expect much guidance. Overall not terrible but the projects were poorly instructed and the homework was often self-taught. Not much point showing up to lectures after the first few weeks.

I feel like Clause was uninvolved in the course curriculum compared to the other professors who also taught this course. His lectures were good though sometimes they felt repetitive. He is a nice professor but feels intimidating and unapproachable at times. The class' workload was easy to handle and the exams match what was covered in the homework.

I felt as if the lecture material and actual assignments did not correlate all the time. Occasionally felt like he forgot some of us are first-time coders. Aside from that, great introductory course. He makes time to check in with students which is not common among professors. Assignments/labs are understandable and teach you a lot!

Prof Clause was one of my best professors this semester. He really knows what he's talking about and tried to make class interesting and convenient for everyone. Nevertheless, he is sometimes a bit cold when you approach him, but overall he's a pretty great professor.
He's a really nice guy. The class is really easy. All of the labs are done in pairs which is kind of annoying but it's still a pretty enjoyable class.

The 1st guy (11/4) is right. Clause teaches and answers students' concerns well. He keeps the lecture lively by taking fun breaks in between. The labs are tough, but using Piazza is a big help. He can sometimes come off as mean or unhelpful in his online posts, but the response is always in the student's best interest to help the questioner think.
He is not bad, but his course is hard.

Took him for CISC108 in fall of 2013 and finally decided to rate him. Was a great teacher and I learned so much in that class. 108 is pretty rigorous so having Clause was great, and if you have a choice, I would say pick clause because everything was explained clearly and the class was my favorite to attend,

Good professor, I enjoy the way he teaches. Attendance is 5% I would suggest going the first couple of classes and then deciding if you wanna keep going. Graded in a group project that is built on throughout the semester. I recommend him for software design.
rating_tags: Group projects--Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

review_text: didnt learn anything from his class, lectures are useless. No grading rubric at all, giving out grade depending on his mood.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Took him for Software Design, talks about none of the material of the assignments in class, lectures are useless. Doesn't even introduce any assignments or big project implementations. Roasts you for not implementing something in 5 minutes for presentations and super sarcastic. Killed my passion for coding.

Absolute waste of time. Project, lectures, and homework felt like three different curriculums. Unreachable outside of class. Completely disorganized, vague instructions, and a ridiculous insistence on using LateX. If you care about your GPA and your sanity you will avoid this prof at all costs.

Unless you have experience coding prior to this course, I would not consider this a "base level" Software Engineering course. Lectures are somewhat negligible since they discuss topics relevant to the tech world, but not topics that would help you pass the class. Make sure to study outside of class because we didn't learn any coding during class.

You will struggle if you have never coded before. I took this class twice, Professor Alipour teaches as if everyone has experience. It's possible to pass if you have the dedication to learn. The book helps but it's better if you're hands-on. Reach out to TA's, and attend lectures for examples that could be on exams. Exams are random frqs.
He doesn't explain his exams well and depends on his ta's to help solve the problem. Some tas are helpful while the others aren't.

Took the prof for Intro to Comp Sci and Programming and still felt the class was hard even with previous programming experience. He's really ambiguous about what's going to be on the exams and does not offer any study guides unless you attend his review lecture (which still wont explain much). You're practically gambling with this guy.

(CS1336)This is  an intro course to computer science, he even encouraged many non-compsci kids to take it however this class was so difficult to pass. He won't tell you what will be on the test and doesn't really break down materials in a beginner friendly way, just shows you what he does. Studied CONSTANTLY and still failed the tests.

the only way to pass his exams are if your lucky or a mind reader, your open ended questions in the exams must contain certain words and even if you have those words you will get the question wrong unless your lucky, his lecture are shiet, and he didn't release the final instruction to the final project till 1 week before final week cause he forgot

Avoid this professor at any cost! He will not specify what he wants in his "open-ended" questions. Questions appear to be easy and doable, but even if you get them right and you verify your response with the class textbook, somehow it will still be marked wrong. There is absolutely nothing you can do.

Difficult subject but fantastic professor (my favorite at KU now). He is very fair with partial credit and adjusting deadlines. I probably spent 5+ hours on the projects outside of class each week but I truly learned a lot in this class. Definitely take if you have the option to!

Genuinely one of the top professors in the EECS department. He makes sure that students are able to take the class in a way that best suits them, and when it comes down to grading he is very fair. He provides resources that are helpful and easily accessible. The class is hard, but he dedicates time outside of class for review sessions.

Its very evident he puts a lot of time and effort into teaching this class. The course content and quizzes/exams are hard, but Drew is very accommodating (within reason) when it comes to requests from students or re-evaluation of projects/quizzes/tests. Also very passionate and knowledgeable about the topics he teaches!

review_text: Worst professor I've ever had, Never take one of his classes holyyyyyyy. So many needless assignments and I took it for an ELECTIVE. What a guy...

review_text: This class was fine, although he gave us so many papers to read, write up and participate in and about midway through the semester, he changed the format up. Great professor overall, but could be better!

This class feels like a waste of time. idk why we are reading so many articles. and he goes extreme with the use of Chatgpt when students can easily get around that. The attendance policy is also way too easy to cheat, you can easily do attendance for friends and he wont know. I wouldn't recommend this class if you want to learn something valuable.
It is a waste of time. The papers you read are not helpful and do not match what you are learning.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--So many papers

review_text: Reviews are misleading. Attendance is mandatory, and he closes it by 9:03, leaving no time to answer the daily question correctly. We get three excused absences, but they're mostly needed for bad weather, so they don't feel "excused" at all. The assigned readings are completely unrelated to what we actually learn in class. Avoid him if you can!

Avoid this professor. Attendance is given 2 minutes. He'll close check-in at 9:03am when the class starts at 9am. You have 3 unexcused absences but if there is freeze rain, he'll tell you to use one of those absences because “that's what they're for”. Even the honors advisors think that's stupid. Inconsistent difficulties in homework.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

review_text: Absolutely fantastic. Favorite professor and class at KU. Super engaging, detailed, and helpful. I learned a ton, but never felt out of my depth.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Very engaging. It is obvious how much work he puts into his classes and that he wants students to succeed.

Professor Davidson is nice and funny, but he needs to explain some concepts concisely. He gives 8 projects and 4 quizzes, which are hard. Labs, check-ins, and written works are easy since you get full credit if you attend his lectures. If you work on the assignments punctually and have a good project partner, then you will do well on this course.

The material is hard, but he delivers it with passion and puts a bunch of jokes on the slides Grading is quite lenient and he resonates with students very well Professor asks for feedback several times though the semester and you can immediately see him taking in the advice received There are optional projects available (which are very rewarding)

This class will take up more time than all your other classes. Projects are very hard, make sure you have a good partner. Quiz grading was fair, he curves them a lot which is nice. He has his grading and class structure very well done. Many grades so you won't be done over one bad assignment. Class is hard but you'll prob get a B if you're > avg.
rating_tags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

review_text: If you dont enjoy working on class work for hours every day this class may not be for you. The amount of work is absurd, and not even beneficial to your learning. Most of the class is very lost and usually end up learning from each other instead of the lectures.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: This professor is truly an anomaly. A friendly, patient, and a professor that truly cares for his craft and students. He makes a difficult class easy to understand. He is willing to work with his students and will constantly make adjustments in your favor. However, there is a large workload and the class is still difficult but you'll be fine.

He is epic. Actually a good professor who shows passion for what he teaches. He indirectly encourages students to learn instead of trying to regurgitate information, Tests are very fair and he makes sure you are properly prepared. Literally nothing bad to say.

Great professor. One of the best professors ive had at KU. He cares about his students and make sure they learn the materials well. 665 is a tough course with a lot of project and lab. But you will learn a lot.

Drew Davidson is a phenomenal professor. He truly cares about how all of his students are doing and caters to every level of ability. His lectures are very engaging as he clearly puts forth lots of effort into the design of the course content. He always sticks around after class for student questions. This class is a lot of work, but it's worth it!

Hands down probably the best professor in the department. He's very organized and tells you exactly what you need to learn and when you need to learn it by. The way he teaches is also very nicely paced, especially for a topic as heavy as compilers. There is a lot of work though, but he gives ample time to complete them. He is also VERY responsive.

Class was challenging but rewarding. Dr. Davidson puts more effort into course quality and resources for students than any other professor I've had at KU. You'll learn so much in his class if you are willing to put in the work.
Lectures are semi mandatory. Have to do an assignment and turn it in if you dont attend. Good class though. Projects feel well balanced
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: He's easily one of the best professors I've ever had. He makes you work hard but never without good reason and he's always there to help if you need him. He's constantly going above and beyond for his students and always asking for feedback on how he can do better. I promise you will not regret taking a class with Drew.

Dr. Davidson is an absolutely fantastic professor. He is incredibly knowledgeable, caring, and goes way above and beyond for his students. He is without a doubt my favorite professor at KU and I plan on taking any class I can that is taught by him. 11/10, three thumbs up, absolutely fantastic. KU needs to tenure him immediately.

Drew really cares. He holds extra review sessions, he buys pizza for his students, he'll even do extra video lectures upon request. In my four years of college I haven't met a professor doing more for their students than Drew. And most importantly, he's willing to listen to feedback and adapt to make the course better. Would take again.

Im certain all those reviews telling you to disregard the previous ones are from himself. He's lukewarm when it comes to actually teaching, basically just reads off slides. The quizzes have little to do with the slides themselves. You're better off just reading the textbook and watching the author's own videos on youtube. He never answers emails.

Good professor, interesting teaching style. I think he is alright, nothing good nothing bad I would say the reviews before the exams are helpful, class is made harder than it needs to be. The topic is very interesting, he can tweak some things and be greater.

Fav EECS prof. Always greeted me with a smile when I had questions after class. Super clear lecturing & always asks if we have questions (doesn't make you feel dumb at all). Sends out announcements/reminders for quizzes. He very clearly wants everyone to do well. 3 exams - 20%, 20%, 25%. iClicker attendance - but not graded for it.
Not sure about the red reviews, but he gives great lectures and homework with quizzes for understanding. If you study, you should do fine

I don't understand those bad review. This is a wonderful professor who is cared support students' success. He made communication networks become very interesting. If you go to class and do homework then you will be fine. Plus, the WireShark labs are very interesting and helpful for understanding the concept of this class

Be prepared to learn the material independently, as this professor shows little concern for their students. While the course material is interesting, the professor approach is disappointing. He doesn't even take the time to prepare a proper syllabus. It's unfortunate that this is a mandatory course and that he is the only professor teaching it.

I don't understand all these bad reviews! He's respectful to all his students and his lectures are clear. Go to class, only about 20% of students actually show up & I bet those are the people leaving reviews
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

review_text: Previous ratings of this professor are outdated. The lectures are extremely engaging. The homeworks provide good background knowledge on the material learned in class. The tests and quizzes are more than fair. One of the top courses I have taken so far at KU.

This professor provides very interesting and engaging lectures, there are weekly homework and quizzes, however they are not too difficult. Some of the homework asks you to learn new content on your own that he has not and will not cover in class.  Overall and easy going professor.
Attendance is expected, but be ready to learn everything by yourself. Homeworks are regular. Aweful teacher but content is interesting.

I am glad to see others had a similar experience with Dr. Hashemi. He was quite unpleasant and made me less interested in the material, although I enjoyed the content. Take this course if there is another professor, but if it is only Hashemi, I would recommend waiting or learning the material on your own.

The most difficult and uncaring professor I have had at KU. He has NO interest in the wellbeing of students and whether or not they are learning, rather he is simply condescending and tries to impose his knowledge on you at any moment he can. Extremely, extremely tough grader. I would not take a course by him, ever again.
I don't think he really cares about the students. The test and homework questions are ambiguous. It was hard to know what he actually wants.

Professor Hashemi will not force you to come to class, but will call you out if he even thinks you aren't paying attention. He maintains a distinct lack of ability to lecture, and teaches this class to only let him research at KU. He cannot write test questions, homework questions, or effectively receive student feedback. The worst professor I had.

One of the worst professors I've ever had. The average and median on the first exam was a very low D, though a curve was not considered for that exam and the professor even went so far as to say the exam was actually "easy." Lectures and homework questions are often unclear and ambiguous.

He's the professor you want to take if you already know CS202. He's funny and young. His one "problem"(I dont see it as one) is that he does go on tangents/rambles a lot, but these tangents are about real life. If you're a transfer or have a knack for coding take him. The projects make you think, but u can do em in a weekend.

Brilliant individual. Has a great awareness of what's going on in the computer and technology world. The only critique is that it took a long time to receive grades on tests and homework assignment. Tests are fair.

Professor Kukla is incredibly knowledgeable about all things Comp sci and has a lot of real world experience in the industry. If youre looking to actually learn something, and not just memorize stuff, take him.

Prof. Kukla made me fall in love with Comp Sci. His lectures are interesting and engaging and it is great to learn Computer Science from someone who clearly has lots of experiences working in the industry. I have gone to him outside of class and asked for help in becoming a better programmer and he has always been helpful.

Hard class, but that's not the professor's fault. Kukla is very good at answering questions you have, and has a great sense of humor. He tends to ramble off topic at times, but will snap right back to it. You can argue test answers with him and he'll very much consider changing them if your answer was correct and mis-graded. Good luck!
10/10 professor. Really cares about his students and tries to teach for understanding rather than just memorization.

Had a horrible experience with Kukla! What others have said about him is true. He really is not in charge of the class. If you ask him any questions, his usual response is go ask or email Professor Dixon. He also will start typing the email like we can't type it ourselves. He is also not good at explaining things and goes of topic. Don't take him!!
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

review_text: Prof. Kukla is not meant for a beginner programmer. He doesn't follow the timeline on the syllabus and doesn't cover necessary topics before an exam. His lectures are hard to follow. He does not seem to have knowledge about the projects or lab assignments. Avoid him at any cost.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--TEST HEAVY

review_text: I do think he is a good professor. I would recommend finding another professor if you don't have that much background in programming. He made me hate C++, He is very well experienced for years and  he sometimes doesn't explain what he is doing because he doesn't remember what it does exactly. Puts question on exams that is not martial related
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: The issue with Dr. Kukla is really only one. He isn't really in charge of the class. He just lectures. So he often had to email the other lecturer for info on the assignments. That being said, he's awesome. Smart, funny and experienced.
Just awesome. "my fingers never leave my hands" "It's turtles all the way down"  He will remove the mystery from computer science, If you ask questions you will get great answers.

Hardest grader ever, will assign problems on stuff you covered in class for a whole 5 minutes. Doesn't sympathize if you know what you're doing, only if you've memorized what he's said, though you can easily get an A if you know what you're doing.
Very entertaining and fun. Explains thing clearly, brings a lot of examples, asks questions  and respects your opinion. Laid back and always willing to help. As a lecturer, he is pretty good.
His lectures were fairly boring, but he was a cool guy. He was easy to talk to and get help from. Sometimes he could be a little bit too casual, as in walking around class barefooted.

Explains the material very well for both beginner coders and more experienced students. He clearly still codes a lot (unlike some older professors). The class itself is difficult if you're new to object-oriented programming, but Kukla is very helpful if you ask questions. Bonus points for funny and interesting tangents during lecture.
Very cool and funny guy! Explains things clearly, always willing to help. He's very understanding and encouraging, which is really nice especially if you're an amateur programmer.

Dr. Stephens is probably the dullest lecturer at UMBC. However, his homework assignments aren't demanding and his exams look very much like the study guides he gives out. Lecture slides are posted online.

Took Numerical Computations. Most of the class had no idea what we were doing, but somehow we were able to figure it out for the homework and exams. He was very lenient and the homeworks and exams were straightforward. I also did actually learn some.

Dr. Stephens may have already retired, but just in case he hasn't... He's a clear but extremely boring lecturer. Homework takes less than 30 minutes to do (there are only five assignments throughout the semester) and exams look extremely similar (mostly the same, in fact) to the study guides he posts prior to the exams. DO THE STUDY GUIDES.

Go over every study guide he gives you. He reuses them so there will be questions that aren't on the test. He has his favorite type of question for each section so they will definitely be on the exams. Exams actually were easy and grading wasn't too bad. Lecture is really boring though. I would recommend this class for the tests alone.

Dr.Stephens is rather boring. He speaks very slow and just teaches off of slides. That being said, it wasn't a difficult class. I went into every exam thinking I  was going to fail and got As. The questions are the easy problems from the study guide. Grader can be a tad annoying though. Definitely work the study guide and go to class for examples.

Has the T.A. do all practice exams(mad typos in proofs) then is disoriented and ignorant when you ask ?'s about them.  I taught myself everything he reads from OVERHEADS doesn't do any proofs in class Self teachers definitely take. if not try someone else

YAWN. Hands down the worst professor I've ever had. Lectures are extremely boring and painful to stay awake in. He reads off power points and doesn't necessarily teach. Nice guy, I feel bad for him. He should definitely retire asap.

My favorite professor at UMBC! Stephens is funny, easy to talk to, knows a great deal about Cryptology and teaches a great class with fun assignments and projects. Grading system is awesome with lots of chances for extra credit. Handed out prizes for highest test scores, best programs, fastest cipher-breakers, etc. Really fun class.

Very boring, lectures hardly supplemented what you learn from the book. His sample problems are very cryptic compared to book examples, but if you pay attention in class he usually does them for you. Exam study guides are usually exactly like the exams and are worth it to do. Has problems with technology sometimes, seems stuck in the past.

Extremely knowledgeable about the topic.  Attendance not mandatory.  Teaches from powerpoint, and supplements with examples on the bard.  Relatively clear and goes off on tangents sometimes, not the loudest speaker.  He'll help you all you need it, and tests are very fair.  DO HIS PRACTICE EXAMS!!!

A nice guy, but very boring, goes on tangents alot about stuff that really doesn't relate to the course. He is organized which helps a lot but as for clarity, not so much, he's brilliant but not very good at teaching the material.

Not bad, but not the best either.  His is a little bit on the boring side for lectures and must have killed a few trees judging by all of the handouts he gave out, sometimes 2 or 3 multi-page handouts in a class.  He is a very fair grader and very flexible.
utilize the exam review sheets and you will ace this class.  great guy and an excellent teacher.
this guy could work for the CIA
this guy loves to joke around.
Nice guy but drier than a sand sandwich on toast. Boring!

An excellent professor.Many students are judging him for his performance during his last semester at school, little do they know about some family-related issues/emergencies that occurred to prof Edelman during the semester. He was very helpful and as others mentioned, he cared about the well-being and learning of his students. We miss you Edelman!

I also have had trouble contacting Mitch. Not as accessible as I'd like.  That being said, he is far from the worst professor I've had.  Nice guy, tells a lot of jokes, willing to clarify anything for anyone and will meet after class if needed.  Lets you know what to expect on exams and holds a review session prior to each exam. Will take him again

Mr. Edelman may have taught well in previous semesters, but he was terrible this spring!  The course was so poorly taught the CMSC Dept. interviewed students to find out what was going on!  Mr. Edelman was always inacessible.  On top of everything else, he was over two weeks late in turning grades, prolonging the end of a ridiculously run class!

Worst professor I've had (and I've had some bad ones). Its been 3 weeks since the end of the semester, 2 since grades were due, and he still has not submitted grades for my entire class (the dept. can't contact him). He never focused on class material in class and constantly changed assignments and their requirements. Inaccessible to his students.

One of the best professors I've ever had! He really cares about his students and provides as much attention as possible to them.  His exams are a bit difficult, but he usually has a nice preview of the exam 1-2 days prior to it. Very nice guy in general.  I may take CMSC 345 with him next semester, though I do not need it for my major.
One of the best professors I've had.  He really cares about his students.  It is rare to find a professor with these qualities nowadays.

I don't understand how got those great comments,he spends a lot of time telling jokes, most of them are not that funny.His lecture is boring. I didn't learn much from his class. It was just waste of time. The only thing i liked about him his flexibility to help students.

Loved him as a professor. He is really dedicated to his students and cares about how their doing. Exams are extremly hard your end grade is based upon what he thinks you deserve. Lectures are entertaining and he explains everything well. Go to the study day before an exam, it's helpful (and if you never go to class it's a very nice review).

His lectures are hard to follow, and he rambles a lot. Harsh sense of humor - try not to take his jokes personally. Lenient grader, especially if he knows you. Ask questions in person, not through email. I really wasn't crazy about him, but I can say that he genuinely cares about his job and his students.

TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HIM! He is a genius and honestly cares about how the students do. Barely used the textbook. Go to class and pay attention to what he is doing! Explains things very clearly and is very open to any questions you may have. Curves the exams and the course in his own way to help you out. Does not put too much emphasis on proofs.

Knowledgeable and good at teaching. Doesn't take class too seriously. Genuinely interested in students' success. Willing to explain ad nauseum. Keep up and study and exams will be no problem, but, as noted, they are curved. Wish he was full time faculty! Edelman is a safe bet for 341.
The kind of guy who will explain things five times if the class doesn't quite understand.  Always willing to clarify for you.  His exams are difficult, but always graded on a curve.
Bad sense of humor, but a fairly easy class.  He's a bit boring of a lecturer.
Knows the material very well. Tries to make learning interesting. Office hours not so good. Some of his jokes could use some work... but a very good teacher in general

I took CMPE650 with professor Mohsenin. She is one of the best professor I've ever had. Her teaching style and lectures are great. All the homework prepared me for a very interesting final project which was designing a solution for a real-world problem and I learnt a lot from doing it. I absolutely recommend taking this course with her.

Professor Mohsenin is undoubtedly one of the most outstanding instructors I have encountered in my entire graduate program. Her teaching approach is stimulating and engaging, and the courses she delivers are consistently current and applicable. The final project that she assigned to us for course 650 was very enjoyable and educational.

I was actually looking forward to 415. One of the things I appreciated most about the course was the challenging and thought-provoking projects. They were designed to push us to think critically and creatively, and I learned a lot from them. I also recommend attending the lectures, as she covers a lot of important topics.

CMPE 650 Course taught me how to design a CNN accelarator in verilog for it's course project. The Professor was very helpful clearing all of my doubts and at last with the discussion with her, I could finally complete my project and design a CNN accelarator which is great. I would like to advice anyone who is willing to take her course, go for it.

I had a great time in 650 with Tinoosh. The project and the assignments were really very interesting. I would recommend that you start them sooner as they would require some significant amount of thought and effort.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

review_text: She waists your time. Avoid her.
Forced to take her for some courses. Very unorganized and all over the place.
She is actually the most unorganized teacher I have ever had. She does not know what's going on half of the time.

really helpful and if you are interested will make this course the joy of the semester. Grading is good. the projects are really helpful to get good grip over FPGA. if you dont have any prior background in verilog, dont worry she would make everything clear. pay attention,give time and this would be best CMPE course ever.

Good professor. Pop-quizzes & Lectures will help you prepare for exams. Write quickly during the exams, you might run out of time. Fair grader. Projects can take up a lot of time so start when they are assigned. She is a very interactive teacher so you won't fall asleep and she is very approachable if you have questions during office hours.
Such a difficult course ...

By far the worst professor I have ever had in my life. The topics covered are niche, abstract, and difficult. I would consider myself an above average student and his teaching along with the content has left me more confused than I have ever been. Please for the love of god never take this class. Don't touch it with a 100ft pole.

Professor Winter is so far the toughest grader yet. He ignores emails and is disrespectful every time we meet up with him. He has many lectures which are filled with many small details that are difficult to remember. He may be a knowledgeable man, but he does not know how to teach a class. Wouldn't recommend it to anyone!

I just used Antlr (ANother Tool for Language Recognition) to parse view ddl's schema information at work.  This class has real world applications.  Sometimes if things are hard people just want to write classes like this off as useless.  All CS classes have applications or they wouldn't bother putting them in the CS program.

Took this class remotely in Summer 2022. He is a strong believer in all-or-nothing grading. If you do not complete the assignments in their entirety, you will not get any points. One plus is he did massively scale the grades, but it was not enough to keep me from failing. Avoid this professor if you can help it.

Professor literally lied about his available hours, grading criteria, and multiple other things, that affected a group project worth a collective 30% of my grade across the course. There were no rubrics or point by point breakdowns for any assignments. The exams did not ask questions that could be gleamed from the lectures.

Professor Winter is clearly a very intelligent man, which is why it's so unfortunate that his teaching is so subpar. He's very inaccessible, and will belittle you for not understanding things and doesn't give feedback on mistakes, expecting you to find them out on your own after you were unable to do so when it was worth a grade. Avoid if possible.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

review_text: This course was conducted differently than the 4000 level reviews I'm seeing. He was caring that we understood what was going on and was easily accessible for questions if you were having issues with anything. I also thought it was neat he used his own coding system to teach us as beginners. It made coding interesting for me!

Great class, very interesting. Reallllyyyy hard. You have to really take in everything from the lectures and rewatch them sometimes. The group project is really tough and sucks if you don't have a good group. Winter is pretty quick on question responses and grading stuff. People fail this class but you really get a lot out of it if you work hard.

Has a semester long preassigned group project that builds on itself. If your group is poor, Victor does not care. If you ask questions, Victor will talk down to you until you stop. If you meet with him, he will say things then forget five minutes later. A very mean and harsh man. Gave random .pdfs for books and never mentions them. Short lectures.

I think Victor Winter has a bad rap because his course content requires people to actually work hard and he grades harshly. I don't know why people expect less from a senior level class where you build a programming language. He knows his stuff and has good lectures in his online class at least. He was respondent to email and helpful to me.
Worst professor I have ever encountered
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Tough grader

review_text: Do not take this course. Ever.
Try to avoid him.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

review_text: Dr. Winter definitely knows his stuff, but he's easily one of the worst and hardest professors at UNO. Expects you to learn and "elegantly" use an entire new programming (that isn't used in any other class) for the group project and assignments: SML. He *loves* SML, but it's the worst programming language I've ever had the misfortune of using.

Worst professor I have ever had. Shame on UNO for letting him continue teaching. Belittles you if you don't understand the material and does not want to teach. If you ask questions he will not fully answer them and if you still do not understand he says you are not taking his answers and you are not listening to him. Horrible professor.
He is not accessible outside of class, gets mad when you ask questions, and treats you like you're stupid if you don't understand anything. I have never had a worse professor. Avoid at all costs.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: This was a brand new course to replace algebra 1310 it is an amazing course by an amazing teacher. Dr. winter is a tough grader and he pays attention to detail But his explanation of what he wants is spot-on and he's more than willing to help you if you need it. This was the only math class that I've ever done well in.

I took his class in this fall semester. For the group project, we happened to have some bugs. However, we submit it on time an willing to fix bugs in two days. But he did not give us any chance to fix it.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

review_text: You can work your butt off finishing his assignments, milestones and quizzes on time. But if you don't do good in his midterm. You are screwed! He makes this class very difficult to pass. It is also useless class, so prepare to work hard to learn something you will never EVER need. My congratulations UNO for letting someone like him teach. TERRIBLE
rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Tests are tough

review_text: Toughest class at UNO for CS students. Prepare for the worst. Tests are 50% and extremely hard. Unfortunately, he is the only one in charge of lecturing programming language so it's inescapable. Good luck
Great prof! Worth taking this class. You'll learn a lot!

From the first class he tells you, he runs this class as Boss (him) to employees (students) relationship. And also he tells you that he will not spoon feed you, lightly saying he won't help you AT ALL. But if you DARE to go to his office and ask him questions, he will make sure to confuse you so much that you will never bother him again. TERRIBLE

An incredibly tough grader. A good portion of the class was below 60 percent. Clearly posseses an encyclopedia of knowledge, but rather critical of minor mistakes on homework and exams. No partial credit given for solutions other than true. The class should be 5 credits given the workload by Dr. Winter. Good guy, very tough teacher.

He is absolutely ridiculous, everything he says is vague, nothing is clear, nothing he says is helpful and just riddles of figure it out yourself, he says he runs his class like a business and if he did that he would be out in less than a week, impossible to reach and when you do he indirectly calls you an idiot, no constructive help just vague

Grading criteria are vague. Major points missed for minor mistakes (insists on perfection.) Low accessibility outside of class. Belittles students who have difficulty understanding material. Found it difficult to go to class just to have him talk about how horrible we all were. Obviously brilliant, wasted intelligence.
He is not specific to the point, he wants something in assignments and he explains something in class.

Winter thinks in circles and talks in circles.  He believes he is inherently superior to everyone and is condescending to students.  He uses his class as a soapbox and an opportunity to boss students around so that he can feel like he's important, since he couldn't make anything of himself at Sandia Labs.

Dr. Winter knows his stuff, and is not afraid to let you know about it. The techniques he used to teach the class sometime felt like borderline scare-tactics. That being said, don't make this class harder than it needs to be. His power points are decently put together and are good for reference if you can't quite follow his wordy lectures.
Hard. I took discrete math. I honestly don't understand anything.

He believes that if you could be replaced by an English major, you shouldn't pass his class. He's tough, but it's worth it when you reach higher level courses. If you receive a bad grade, he encourages you to challenge it if you feel it was unfair. His assignments are time consuming since he only accepts LaTeX-format documents for homework.

The guy is horrible.  Mid term and final are nothing like whats covered.  Project isn't related to class work.  I wouldn't recommend him to anyone.  Worst professor i've ever had to deal with.  He's so full of himself and grades very, very subjectively.  Can't stand him and I'm glad I'll only have him for 1 class.  Stay away!

Knows his stuff, definitely in my top 5 recommendations at UNO, if you've had a class with Clark, Konvalina, Mahoney, or Fulkerson and liked them, you'll probably like this guy too.  The only complaint I have is his insistence on use of LATEX.  You spend more time typing your assignments than you do working the problems.

Clearly a very smart guy, but he struggles to be clear. Lots of stuttering in his boring lectures make it hard to understand the content. Other than that just make sure you have a good understanding of the content as the quizzes are mostly easy if you are following along with lectures. Tests are somewhat difficult, the questions are unclear at time

I took his class entirely online. Each week consisted of watching video lectures and maybe a test or quiz. He talks slow enough you can watch them in 2x speed. The textbook is written by him, but you honestly don't need to get it. You don't even turn in homework, just watch videos of him going over the answers.

It seems that this class was restructured this semester. 20% of grade is just watching videos. 50% is 6 quizzes with lowest dropped, 30% are two exams. Course load is fairly low, just need to study. He is an understanding professor and quick to respond to emails. The lecture videos can be a bit dull, but are helpful, 1.75x speed makes them bearable

I took an L in this class. Quizzes make up a large portion of your grade and require Lockdown Browser w/ camera. You will need to study using outside resources because the lectures/textbook material aren't the most clear.
rating_tags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: This UNO computer science professor is truly one of the best. Incredibly helpful and dedicated, he goes the extra mile to ensure students understand the material.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Test heavy

review_text: Prompt emails. Provides the exam Topics that detail essentially the exact questions he expects you to answer on the exam. Lenient grader; If you at least try to write an answer and show what you know about what is being asked, you'll likely get points. His lectures are short & concise. If you had a problem in this class, the problem is you.

He replies to emails fast which is good, especially if you are taking it online. However, he makes you do assignments on Multisim which is a $70 software that is only available on Windows. The way he sets up Canvas is confusing, and he doesn't give a lot of practice problems either. So you have to go elsewhere to learn the class materials better.

I took the online class which is very lecture heavy & the book is one he wrote. Lectures don't always explain the concepts in the clearest way & you will probably need to find outside help because the content is very difficult to grasp. Only 5 assignments & exams when I took the class so you really had to get a decent grade on everything to pass.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: He responds to emails within minutes, which is great for a completely online class. However, if you take it asynchronous, he will expect you to take your exams during a specific time and will not budge, even if you have another class (or an EXAM) during that time. Overall, homework and lectures weren't bad.

Literally one of the few Computer Science professors that I would take again. Dude gives awesome feedback, replies to emails timely, helps break down assignments, gives solutions after homework is done so you can see how to properly do the assignments. Overall, dude needs a raise and I wish more teachers in Computer Science were like him at UNO.

DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS, i repeat, DO NOT. Took his class online, and he's the worst professor ever. He doesn't help you whatsoever, and when you do ask for help, he'll tell you to watch his posted videos. You also have to purchase a book online that HE wrote to do the homework. No feedbacks from him either for homework or tests.

This is one of the worst classes I have taken. This was a totally online course, but had scheduled quizzes and exams on Friday mornings for what seemed like absolutely no reason. He requires you purchase a book that he wrote and is probably the least reviewed academic book I have ever seen. The teaching style doesn't give any value to class content

Surprised at the good reviews here, must be a better professor in-person. Our grades consisted of a few homework assignments and 2 tests, but half of our grade was from 5 multiple-choice 15-minute quizzes. Class averages were consistently in the 30% range and he offers NO feedback, won't even let you see which ones you answered correctly.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

review_text: This is definitely the worst class I have ever taken. He posts assignments without warning so if you don't check Canvas daily you're out of luck. He didn't post an exam schedule until after we were a month into the class (and there is no hw schedule). The textbook he makes you buy (that he wrote) is confusing and unorganized with bad examples.

Extremely disorganized and makes you buy a book he wrote. Uses a TA and grades tough with little feedback. Online classes require you to constantly check if homework is posted, as schedule is "TBA." Have to randomly show up to take tests on zoom in front of a group at specific times not mentioned when you sign up for class. Doesn't extrapolate.

Farhat is a super nice guy and always available for questions. He is lenient in grading and curves a lot. The class is just watching lectures (if online), doing homework on the lecture, and a few quizzes/tests.

He is really nice and it is very easy to success in this courses. Although they have 2 quiz and 3 tests, they are not hard. He even gave some students to retake test at home if they didn't do very well. He also dropped two lowest score of my homework.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: He is a great professor. He cares about what you learn, and will slow down if you don't understand the material. It's just 2 quizzes, 3 exams, and homework. Homework is heavy but just 2-4 problems each assignment. Tests are like the homework assignments. ***DONT BUY HIS BOOK, IT CAN BE FOUND ONLINE FOR FREE*** Easy A if you pay attention.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

review_text: I took it partially online and I highly recommend it. He talks slowly and methodically, but you can watch the videos at 1.25 speed to go a little faster. You can't do that in person. The whole class was just watch a couple lectures, do homework on said lectures, and take quizzes after a set of lectures. Then you have a midterm and final.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: This class was pretty straight forward and Hassan was pretty lenient with grading quizzes. Overall really good professor.

Professor Farhat really does care about what he teaches and makes sure to explain it well to students. He constantly asks if anyone has questions during lectures. There is homework due for almost every class, but it's not too hard. Attendance isn't technically mandatory, but you have to turn in your homework during class. He doesn't use Canvas.

Dr. Farhat is one of the nicest professors I have had so far.  During the semester, I had a lot of personal conflicts, and he always helped work around my schedule for tests.  His lectures are very dry and long, but if you pay attention the tests are not too hard.

He is the only professor who can teach this class . You have to take it .Homework is ok. Quizzes are announced 1 week in advance and they are not too tough. There are 3 tests and the level of difficulty is average.  Overall, it's an ok class for me. You can get a B easily if you are a little bit lazy
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Dr. Farhat is the nicest teacher and willing to answer any questions. I wish there were more opportunity to ask questions after class.

Class started off nice but then we crash landed. Tests turned into nightmares. Very hard and complex version of material discussed in class showed up in test. Sometimes we never talked about the stuff, also terrible inductions in test.

Attendance not mandatory, but succeeding without going to class seems impossible. There is no book, only the material given in lecture. Test topics are given ahead of time. No homework, no quizzes. 5 tests are the only graded material. Material is fascinating, but requires high familiarity with discrete math. Excellent teacher.

Writes everything on the board and gives reviews for every quiz/test. Did not have required text when I took. Reviews everything and makes best effort to be sure everyone is understanding the material.

Great professor. He writes everything on the board but almost everything is clear. I liked the way he taught the class. He doesn't baby feed you like many other professors. Instead, he requires that you look up and review content on your own. This causes students to learn more. Follow the review sheets and you'll be fine.

He is a very good teacher. He writes everything on the board so you have to take notes, but he goes slowly and explains everything thoroughly. Homework assignments were short and usually very easy. Overall an easy A if you go to class.

Dr. Farhat is an awesome professor! There is no textbook and everything is written on the board. He also repeats everything that he written. His accent is a little hard to understand at first, but gets easier as time goes on. The class is all memorization and an easy A. He doesn't want anyone to fail and tries everything to make everyone succeed.
Farhat is easily one of my favorite teachers that I have had.  Good lecturer, provides plenty of notes and examples, always eager to answer questions.
No Comments

CIST 1010 was a decent class that made me aware of campus resources I otherwise wouldn't have known about. Attendance is mandatory.   Dr. Dorn is pretty friendly, and makes his expectations clear. He is willing to help you outside of class.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

review_text: This course is meant to be an light intro to college and Professor Dorn takes it way to seriously. He grades assignments toughly when they should be graded for completion. This is generally an ok class but Professor Dorn makes it harder than it needs to be.

He does basically nothing all semester. He was too lazy to grade anything until a week after the semester ended so you had no idea how you were doing. Then when he finally grades your assignments, he does it extremely harsh for things that should just be busy work. Definitely has a superiority complex.

I definitely learned Java, that's for sure. Class operates like a 5 credit course. I didn't know a thing about programming before taking Intro to CS1 and this ramps it up to a new level. If you have a job/many non-college responsibilities take this class with easy classes to keep yourself sane. Because his course piles on an ungodly amount of work.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

review_text: The lectures help very little, homework takes an ungodly amount of time and what you're supposed to do is vague. This class is a freshman level class and it is treated like it is a graduate level. This class is 3 credits, lab mandatory, with the lab being 0 credits. Honestly, change majors if you have to take this class. It's not worth it.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: Time expectations and work load felt unrealistic for 3 cr hr. Semester felt like immersion experience, but numerous resources were made available to help students stay afloat. Lectures were mostly engaging. Dorn is bright, enthusiastic, and eager to help students succeed. I feel I would have learned more if not so stressed the entire time, though.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: Dorn expects students to spend 15+ hours working on Java II outside the classroom. The class is extremely difficult and time consuming. If you're not so knowledgeable about Java then you'll have to work extra hard just to stay afloat because there is no lack of work. Avoid if possible, but it's usually required for IS&T majors.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: The course material is easier than previous semester. He would love to help you solve the problem. If you do assignment earlier every time, you will feel better.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures

review_text: If he taught Graduate level or Senior level class he would be perfect. However, it is Freshman level and is ridiculously time consuming. The amount of time you will spend on his java 2 class for the meager 3 credits is not equitable. Avoid if possible.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

review_text: Coming from a high school java class was a huge jump to the level of java II. It was a very time intensive class, however Dorn gave plenty of examples and assistance that helped me a lot. There are a lot of specific rules and tough concepts that java has, however Dorn will give plenty of activities and examples in lecture that help a ton.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--TEST HEAVY

review_text: Java II is a hard class, but Dorn was a very good lecturer and made class interesting. If you work on the class a little each day, it honestly won't take up too much of your time, and there are a lot of resources out there to help you succeed in the class. I think UNO should make this a 5 credit class for the amount of work required, however.
rating_tags: Respected--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Java 2 is definitely a "weed out" class. He expects a lot from you. Honestly, way too much. Assignments are very vague and exam averages are low (60%). If you are a below-average to average student, you won't pass. Students who had trouble with Java I will have to go above and beyond. Textbook isn't required. Expect a lot of time with this class.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Java 2 is a hard class regardless of who teaches it, and I think Dr. Dorn does a pretty decent job at being accessible and doing the best to answer questions presented to him. He shines in the lectures and I personally really like his approach to them. Textbook is useful but not "required", and expect a lot of time spent on homework.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: The hardest class I have ever taken. All HW is graded by an auto-grader. It is very bad at telling you what you did wrong and will give 0's even though code matches test cases and is working. A lot of work each week as well. Some weeks I spent around 40 hours just trying to not fall behind. Instructor was also not very responsive to questions.

My class grade right now is a 91% with every category at a 98% and above besides my 60% exam grade. I feel like many students struggle in this class. The class average is around a 75% from that being said. HUGE advice is to start on your homework assignments the day they come out and DONT partner up on hw assignments. You won't pass if ur average.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Dorn is the head of the Java department at UNO, and Java 2 is one of the hardest classes I have ever taken. I am a senior and this freshman level, intro class is harder than my senior level classes. He expects students to spend AT LEAST 15 hours a week on just his class. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Avoid this very poor educator. Dr. Dorn has no respect for his students' time (according to the syllabus, he expects students to spend 15+ hrs/wk outside class on this course - imagine if every teacher designed 3 credit hour courses this way). Class time was worthless, you have to watch video lectures for credit anyways so take the online class.

This instructor tries to be funny sometimes during the class, but one it comes to grade your work he would be someone else. Expect to get F at the end of semester for no reasons. The class is very tough. He's the second worst teacher I've ever had. Expect to spend entire week on one assignment, and then get %50 because of the hidden test cases.

Avoid taking this class with Brian Dorn. Avoid taking any class with him. If you a weak, he will take advantage of you and fail you to offset the budget so you will have to take it again. This man is by far the most terrible teacher I have ever had. He plays favorites and thinks he is the god of all computer related information.

Get ready to spend your entire semester on one class. Worst teacher I have ever had. He was my instructor for Java II. This guy doesn't even realize his code doesn't work in assignments until 2 days before the due date. He is very disrespectful to students and carries a haughty attitude.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: Java 2 at UNO is pretty tough anyway without the professors destroying all of your hard work. You'll spend hours and hours on an assignment only to get a 50% on the assignments because of "hidden test cases". The other teacher isn't any better. If you have to take it, make this the only tough class this semester and go to the learning center.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Tests are tough

review_text: Does a great job engaging students during class, but does not write reasonable tests by any means. Expect the average test scores of the class to be in the 60s. Make sure you work on getting good scores for your homework and participation grades, because your test grades won't be great.

Dorn does a great job engaging his students during lectures which is his strength.  He encourages participation and it helps if you are interested in the material.  The homework and tests are graded relatively tough.  Expect to be able to explain your thoughts clearly and concisely.  Explaining in generalities does not work.

Overall Dorn is a decent teacher.  Yes, there is a significant workload, so be warned.  Lots of time-consuming assigned readings, many of which weren't that helpful.  He could be clearer with his expectations on assignments and how he grades.  Several very time-consuming group projects.  He knows the material well and is willing to help, though.

Tough only in regards to the amount of work. The content is interesting and it's manageable.  When the entire class has problems understanding something, he will take the time to address questions thoroughly, especially before exams.
The worst professor i have  ever had.
Class is fun. But a lot of work. 4 exams (really?). Too many assignments, projects and exams ruin the entertaining part of the class.

Yeah, I agree there is a lot of work to do, but I consider them very valuable, I did learn a lot in this course and it's very useful in the future. But I do think for the current course load, this should be a 6 credit class.

There is a lot of work in his class but most of it is kind of fun.  He is definitely comically overbearing, but you get used to it.  I have no idea how he grades the tests.  I did horrible on one and alright on another.  If you're not intimdated by him, he is really good at answering questions.  He is a really good teacher.

Very very heavy course load. AVOID! 30+ pages of reading before every class. 7+ project sized "lab" assignments each due within 1 week. 3 long "short-answer" exams.  1 big sized project. 1 research project(Grad Only). Class participation credit. Curve given only by redoing exam essay question at home. High maintenance, do not use 100 point scale

Ricks designed the course with a lecture and sprint day each week. He focused 50% on teaching course topics and 50% working with student projects. He is very energetic and includes humor in his lectures. He mentioned you need to dedicate "HOURS AND HOURS" a week to the project to get a 100% on the weekly progress, but he only means 3-5 hours.

Professor Ricks took an interesting approach to the class, teaching it in sprints with self-graded progress reports, as in 2510 there is only one project that you develop as the semester continues. He gives great lectures and makes it clear he cares. Make sure you're invested in the class though, without motivation it can be easy to fall behind.

Dr. Ricks is the best for 3320. However, One project I typed xyzThing instead of xyz_thing. The test case failed because I changed my local test file to match my code. My mistake, but I never knew until he told me- way past due. He kept my grade at 50%- not because it was late, but because he assumed I copied the code from someone he had last year.

Dr. Ricks is a great professor. Class always felt engaging and challenging, and Ricks was great at helping with any struggles along the way. He's very kind and makes sure to connect with every student.
Had a great time. Ricks is an awesome professor, and he goes out of his way to help. Had a few assignments then a final project. Nothing to complain about.

Dr. Ricks is an outstanding professor, who expresses interest in individual students. If you have an issue with a project you're working on, he offers you help during your sprints. However, you need to put in the work outside of class if you want to present quality projects. Overall great professor, and a funny guy.

Dr. Ricks' sprint-based teaching is not for everyone. Most of the material in this course to justify the 4000-level status is what you learn by yourself in each sprint if you decide not to apply the bare minimum. At that point, you might as well self-study instead of spending money to take the class.

A great professor I've taken multiple times at this point. Course load is usually fairly light with you working on your own projects throughout the semester. YOU GRADE YOURSELF. Just show progress whenever he checks in with you, show up, and you'll be fine.

Dr Ricks is very helpful, caring and very good at teaching. He doesn't just read from slides but explains the concepts and has great animated powerpoint to help you understand how things work. Every assignment is explained so you will know what he wants. Mondays were sprint days and in class coding and 1 on 1 with him. Thrusdays were lectures.

He was accessible and helpful with homework questions. Lectures were good, and it was pretty easy if you did the work outside of class. I didn't have much experience with Python, but he made it easy to complete the assignments. I'm not a computer science whiz, but this was a fairly stress-free class. Start early on assignments and you'll be fine.
If you have question this teacher in not very helpful at.
Not very helpful, plus not reachable by email he has banker hours
Just not a good teacher

I loved the structure of this class - you basically get an A as long as you complete all of the assignments, which took a lot of pressure off for me and allowed me to actually spend time learning the content. He's a super funny guy who really cares about your success and understanding in the class - I'd take any class with him!

Really cares about the success of his students and takes time to make sure everyone understands what we're doing. One of the only CS professors I've had that understands tests are not a good way of teaching for Computer Science, and grades based on participation and your final project. GREAT professor and fun to listen to. Feel like I learned a lot

Ricks is honestly a really good guy, the class is sprint based meaning no traditional tests or homework. Everything is off of self chosen projects, as long as you take advantage of this and keep yourself somewhat busy the class will be a blast
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Graded by few things

review_text: Very knowledgeable and provides good feedback. This was a special topics course, but CSCI-4620 will be similar. Attendance isn't graded, but most of the material is delivered through in-class examples.  Thankfully, he does his best to keep class engaging. Source code is provided, but slides/notes are generally your own responsibility.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

review_text: Although Dr. Ricks was a new teacher, he was better than my previous tenured teachers. He truly cared about teaching his class. He had a genuine passion for his job. Be prepared to ask questions and go to the tutors. Although the class was non traditional, it was one of the best classes I have taken at UNO.

This review is over Rick's Spring Java 2 course. Overall I felt like a guinea pig and that's not a bad thing. We got to try many different learning styles for his first semester at UNO. If you want a teacher who is perky, cares about students, and really has some neat lessons that set him apart from PowerPoint professors this guy is the way to go.

Dr. Ricks was a little disorganized however it was his first time teaching Java 2. He changed the assignments a lot from the given criteria however it usually made them better and helped develop my knowledge of Java. The assignments were hard sometimes but it's not high school they should be hard. He really invested time in knowing his students.

Java 2. Not a bad instructor, just a non-traditional leture style, which he warns of at the beginning of the semester. If you talk to him, he is very understanding. His first semester was disorganized, but he will be an asset to UNO once settled in. Made my last programming class at UNO manageable.  Get lab help on the assignments & you'll be fine.

Intro to Computer Science 2. Dr. Ricks would preface about the lecture for the first 10 minutes of class and then jump right into coding. Ricks teaches this class differently than any other professor teaching the course. If you already know java, then you'll be fine. Dr. Ricks knows his stuff, but doesn't convey it to the students well.

She is honestly not that bad of a professor. I took her course last semester and she didn't assign us any pop quizzes or homework. It was mostly lecture notes and quizzes about the chapters. The class format can get a little messy sometimes with due dates being changed, either pushed back or dropped entirely. Class by itself was relatively easy.

A lot of these other reviews are simply... not my experience. No pop quizzes, no late policy, nothing. She did pretty much read straight from the textbook but her exams were all open book and pretty easy.

She often steals content from other people and universities. She is not knowledgeable about the courses she teaches. Furthermore, she knows nothing about IT nor how to use it. If you ask her any IT related questions, she will not be able to tell you the correct answer. I would recommend NOT taking her courses.

As noted by other reviews here she imports practically all of her materials from UC Berkeley which is fine, but she doesn't seem to have a good grasp of the knowledge herself and often struggles to explain the information. Even when I asked her questions outside of class for help, I rarely got a straight or helpful answer. Avoid if possible.
Used material from cs 188 of UC Berkely. Homework and programming assignments are all from cs 188 which are hard. Exams are easy and give bonus points.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: She honestly doesn't really care about students. She gives a lot of surprise quizzes on the material she teaches the same day. When she teaches something, she gives one example and expects you to know the rest by heart. Canvas modules where dis organized, she said we don't need book but then said we did. Reviews are easy, test are hard.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Beware of pop quizzes

review_text: She gives pop quizzes pretty much every class and they are HARD. Be prepared to teach yourself as she introduces topics on quizzes, and in lectures without fully explaining them.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Test heavy

review_text: She provides classnotes that cover all the topics of quizes and exams. Discrete math is hard in nature, so you really need to go to the lectures and do your assignments. Then you'll be fine. She's very willing to help students after class.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Just avoid this teacher at all costs. Simply baffling teaching and testing style. She simply reads the notes almost verbatim out loud while drawing on the whiteboard and then immediately gives you a graded quiz over the information she just tried to teach. If it sounds ineffective it's because it is. Also has a weird "late to class" policy. Avoid.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Very very tough teacher. Class material is tough in general, she did not make it easier. She has an accent that can be very hard to understand, and her handwriting is very terrible for notes. Don't expect to learn anything in class, you will have to use the internet to actual learn the material. Pop quizzes will destroy your grade. Dont recommend.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

review_text: Gives out pop quizzes almost weekly right after lessons before you can even study it, even on the first day of class, but can use notes. Accent and bad handwriting makes it hard to follow her lesson. Goes into more detail on subjects than other profs, avoid if not interested in subject. Nice though and has good office hours. Grade is mostly tests.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES

review_text: One of the worst teachers I've had at UNO.  She doesn't seem to have a good grasp of the course material - she had trouble explaining many things and even admitted to having to Google some stuff to understand it herself!  Pop quizzes given at the end of most classes before we had time to really understand the material.  I would avoid this teacher!

Discrete Mathematics-boy, you're in for a surprise with this teacher. I can honestly say, that this is the worst teacher I have ever had. Ever. In my entire academic career.  It's not that I didn't do well in the course - I got an A, but that was just from reading the textbook. This teacher will not give you a clear explanation for anything.
Great teacher, always willing to help students.
Good instructor. Easy grader. Do the assignments and you will be fine. She made her class enjoyable.

Professor Lierler is a wonderful instructor! She is entertaining and keeps the class involved in a class that would otherwise be boring and tedious. She can relate to students (she herself is a recent grad) and understands that the subject can be difficult at times. Definitely recommend her.

She is a young teacher. I think she is knowledgeable, but is the worst teacher I've ever had. She changes her teaching methodology every couple of weeks. She focuses on things that are meaningless and totally misses the important material and concepts. Absolutely out of touch with students and what they need to succeed. Avoid at all cost!

Exams that are worth 60% of your grade contain questions verbatim from the slides. However, there are hundreds of slides and you are limited to 5 pages of notes. Also has zero accountability as he locks the grade calculation. Students drop due to difficulty and leave groups stranded for the semester project. I have no idea what my grade will be.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Graded by few things

review_text: The professor is a nice person, but struggles with teaching. His lectures are hard to follow due to mumbling, and he doesn't engage with students during class. Questions are only answered through email. Surprise quizzes and labs at the end of class make it hard to keep up, especially with back-to-back classes.

Dr. Song is a nice when I talked to him but other than that he is terrible at teaching. Most of the class is spent copying code from his slides and changing it for whatever assignment you need it for. The 2 exams are 60% of your grade and are both extremely difficult and oddly fair as he seems to curve them. That being said his grading isn't clear

Hands down the worse professor ever. Material you will learn in class is absolutely useless and obsolete. Very hard to understand when he lecture. Asking him questions is useless, feels like he a prompt of responding to these question and offers little to no help. In general take my advice and AVOID this class.

The course material and project are helpful and give you handful skills. Homework is not that hard even you don't have much experience on Java. Attendance is required otherwise you will not get points.

Dr. Song is great. His class is not hard but you have to do homework carefully. There is a group project in this class and you will be fine as long as you follow the course schedule. The tech stack of project is based on what you learned in the lecture. Btw he is a fairly lenient grader.
Overall the course material is useless and half of the semester we learned by ourselves by reading papers, he didn't teach anything
rating_tags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Group projects

review_text: Lectures and projects feature very useful practical skills that you can bring up in a job interview. There was often in-class work that you had to come to class to do - if you didn't come to class, you didn't get the credit. The in-class work was also good practice that reinforced the practical skills we would be using for the group project.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

review_text: If you pay attention in this class, you can pick up some solid and useful coding skills. By the end, you'll have a couple web app servlets, backed up by SQL databases, running on an Amazon Linux server that can be accessed from anywhere on the web. This framework allows you to implement almost any web apps you can think of. Save the slides.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Poor English, useless materials and teaching.

Dr. Song is by far the worst professor I've had at UNO. He just reads right off the slides that he downloaded for the book and never explains any of the concepts that are discussed in any meaningful detail. His slides use out of date examples that won't work on newer machines, so Dr. Song has to find weird hacks to make his examples work. Horrible.

Dr. Song makes me realize how much UNO really cares about their students. They care enough to hire someone who can barely speak English and is hands down the worst professor I've EVER had! Ez A , but you won't learn anything

Dr. Song didn't seem to care about this class at all.   In no particular order; he frequently cut off class early and walked out without answering any questions, he didn't respond to a single email I sent him, assignments due in February weren't graded until late April, he was very soft spoken and didn't engage students at all.

Taking his class was so boring. It seems like he is knowledgable about the subject BUT he goes over the material super quick and doesn't pause to check. He doesn't release lectures before the class so it's hard to follow along. lets you use cheat sheets on test, for this reason I hardly paid attention to lectures and passed by using cheat sheets.

Song is one of the most disorganized professors I've ever had.  He posts assignments and announcements in random places with no rhyme or reason.  He has several PPT lectures for every topic.  He is constantly adding new requirements and changing grading criteria on the fly.  He also has no technical experience, and cannot answer questions.

Horrible Professor! He didn't teach anything! His lectures went over *barely* a tenth of the content! Everything else had to be self-taught from the book! Terrible communicator. Over a thousand dollars down the drain on a class that an AI could have taught! Stole homework from other sources, like the book! Almost nothing original. Astounding!!!
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

review_text: I actually took him for CSCI 2040 Intro to Mathematical Proofs, didn't explain anything well and would get sidetracked often and the class itself is just a blatant money grab from the university. He graded the final a month after the class was over and in total there was only 4 assignments. Terrible Professor
rating_tags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

review_text: Very disorganized, covered lots of high level ideas that did not seem within the scope of this class.

Does require lots of time to do research and read the book. He didn't do really well for online class and seldom reply email. It is hard to understand his question, He made some mistakes in the homework and test.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Skip class? You won't pass.--TEST HEAVY

review_text: Really good professor. He encourages problem-solving and provides a decent number of real world applications. I just wish we were shown more real world situations in which we could use these advanced data structures in programming (this is mostly due to the textbook).

Great teacher. Has a bit of an accent, and somewhat trails off when he speaks, but overall quite understandable. Explanations were pretty good, and he is very flexible. Approachable and fair grader. Homework isn't very often, but is time consuming. Tests aren't too bad if you study a bit and take notes.

Pretty decent prof.  Homework assignments were infrequent, graded fairly, and were sometimes quite time-consuming.  Very approachable and understanding, but didn't always manage class time well.  Overall a very good teacher, and if you do the homworks the midterm/final should be no problem.
