Subu is an awesome teacher. Very patient and approachable. Expectations for homework and exams were very clear. Subu is not hard to understand at all, and will gladly repeat a word or write it down on the board if you ask him. Only complaint is that he does not always return assignments in a timely manner.

Subu is actually a really good prof. Nice, helpful, good about answering questions in class. He's not always quick with responding to emails and he's slow to return graded material, but he's a pretty lenient grader. Did require a lot of time outside of class, but was overall a very pleasant experience.

To start off, I am also speaking for most of my class. This prof. was difficult to understand and seemed to know what he was talking about, but only assigned 4-6 programs all semester.  He was disorganized and did not manage time well.  Quizzes and exams covered concepts that were never discussed in class and resulted in extremely difficult exams.

This professor, while a little intimidating at first, is really good at what he does.  All of the algorithms/data structures that you are required to know, are broken down in front of you, so you know them when you apply them.  If you need help, go talk to him, as he is extremely helpful and nice.

3 exams, lot of homeworks. You will struggle to understand her as she can't speak proper english and be articulate. How she became a teacher is a mystery. She recycles material from UC Berkley but can't explain it. Get all the homeworks right and survive the exam and you will pass the class. Avoid at all cost if you can.
Had Artificial Intelligence CS4450/8456 with her. She has a different accent. She just took all the material of UC BERKLEY AI COURSE. Wish her teaching was also the same level. Avoid if possible
Never understood a single concept. Exams and Assignments are so hard which she never discussed in class.
CS 4450/8456 Teaching is not upto the level which is enough for her assignments and Exams.

She is willing to help students, but she has a pretty big disconnect from students and often doesn't understand what they are asking her. Her tests are also really hard and ask questions about things we haven't even talked about in class. Avoid if you can.

She has an accent and it is very difficult to understand her. You will barely understand her lectures, so get ready to read by yourself. She should consider easy going as her lectures are boring and very hard to understand. Skip if you can.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Tough grader

review_text: Really hard to understand the material, overall, needs to learn lots of material by myself
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: She has an accent, sometimes it is a bit difficult to understand the lecture. Homeworks and Projects are not tough, she gives partial credit if your answer is somewhat related. Exams are easy too, I would say an easy A!
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Group projects

review_text: Had Dr. Summa for 2120 and 2125. To pass his class you really just need to pay attention during lectures and understand your homeworks. Dr. Summa is ready to respond to email, adjust to your schedule and has flexible office hours. His lectures are easy to understand and makes sure the students are actively paying attention. Would take again.

He's a great teacher and explains things very clearly. In order to do well you must study everything he teaches crystal clear 100% especially the study guide. Tests are all short answer. Only negative is he gives a slight feeling like he's burnt out or tired of teaching and comes off rude sometimes. Overall fantastic for learning and great teacher
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

review_text: Exams are hard because the material is hard. Harder than Java 1-2. Other institutions tend to offer this course near the junior/ senior point of curriculum. At UNO it's a per-requisite for MOST C.S classes. You must pass in order to progress. Do all of the assignments, don't miss out on the points, use them as a pillow for the midterm and final.
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Summa is a good professor. However, due to the way classes are scheduled, it is a lot of material; do review a week before the exam again to clear any confusion. This course is not like Java I or II. Set aside a day to dedicate to reviewing the material. The homework is difficult and does not really get asked in the exam. Do not count on a curve.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things

review_text: It's advised to take his class online. Exams are held both online and offline, posing challenges in ensuring fairness. Moreover, the assignments are typically demanding, requiring a strong foundation in JAVA programming. To achieve good grades in exams, it's recommended to watch the ZOOM recordings 2-3 times and take notes.

I agree he could be more organized but everything he says in lecture is what you will need for exams. I highly recommend re-watching lectures. Also, if you really want to do well, just read the book. Be prepared. Especially more-so for 2125. Having taken other cs courses in other schools I can tell you he's one of the good ones.

Great lecturer but not very organized. Two of the four homework were changed the day before they were due and a week after I had submitted them. Tests are few and short and getting one question incorrect can result in a bad grade. More than 50% of my grades were not returned till the day grades were due. Nice guy, good lecturer, bad administrator.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

review_text: Read the textbook/watch youtube videos bc you'll be tested thoroughly on topics covered. Go through the code he writes and practice doing it yourself, pay attention in class, and do your hw and you'll do well enough to pass. You need to put in way more effort than that for an A, and this is coming from someone who breezed through Java I&II w/ an A.

Dr. Summa knows what he's talking about, problem is some students don't. His homework is hard so make sure to get help. He'll only help you so much even if you need it further broken down. Study for test. This class is not an easy A, unless you know what you're doing. Overall he's not bad but you'll definitely work for a good grade.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: Wow! Great Professor. I usually don't rate professors but I had to make an exception for Dr. Summa. He makes sure his students understand the material, always pauses to ask students if they have any questions. Although he does speak in paragraphs rather than sentences, it's easy to understand the material from him. He has a good sense of humor too!

Oustanding Professor, adjust your schedule to take his classes: Java 1, 2 and Data Structures. You will come out understanding everything, top-notch teacher. Beyond that he will work with you on any personal issues you are having (stay in touch), always responds to emails in a friendly and helpful way, and the bonus point options are plentiful.

Dr. Summa is decent professor. For every class he reads straight from the powerpoint and some classes create a code in front of you. He's a good teacher for those who can pick up things easily. I did good in his class, there were a few things i completely understood and other classes I'm completely lost. If you're a visual learner you'll be fine
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

review_text: I would say dr. Summa is a good teacher, he gives good lectures. As long as you show up to class and study for tests it will be an easy A.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: I already love computer science but taking him made me like it that much more.

Summa is a great professor, he explains things well. I came into computer science not knowing ANYTHING about coding and what not and having Summa made me more confident in my knowledge of the subject. Be sure to pay attention to his lectures and you can always ask questions. Don't slack off or you won't do as well!

Summa is awesome. His lectures are in depth and clear, with the subject matter he chooses being interesting enough to keep you interested (i.e. no boring bank account example programs with this guy). He will type out entire programs as examples to concepts he goes over in class and ensures no one leaves confused, so long as they're willing to ask.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Hilarious

review_text: the teacher is awesome, he is funny too and has a great personality. he loves his job and really tries to make sure his students understand what is going on. all that aside, some students including myself just cannot get the material down... it can be so mild bottling that you might tear up while doing the assigned homework. and i never feel stress

One of the best teacher so far in my CSCI classes. He lectures with power point(i know get boring at times), but he goes over key concepts that you will need to learn. Homeworks are pretty difficult so make sure you don't wait last minute to do them. Other than that, his test are pretty straight forward if you understand the material in class.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: This is the guy for any CSCI major. Lectures are good, homeworks are challenging and his grading system benefits you. I guarantee you will learn valuable skills in this class. His tests show you a very good benchmark as to how much you actually know and what you need to work on. If you pay attention in class and study for tests you will do fine.
Summa is a really nice guy and will thoroughly explain things if you are lost
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: He uses slides to guide his lectures, which are posted on Moodle. I felt this was ok, since we talk about a lot of concepts for more sophisticated programming. The course is not a coast class, so get ready  to work and study those concepts- he gives several short-answer questions on exams. I recommend him because I learned a lot, and he's nice!
rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--EXTRA CREDIT

review_text: He is one of the best UNO has to offer. The classes are hard work but Dr. Summa will work with you.

From someone who needed this as a requirement with no previous programing experience, this class was very difficult. The few times I went to him for help I ended up feeling like I was stupid, which doesn't help moral. There are help desk times for class, but are generally the same time every week so if you have class during it, your out of luck.

Dr. Summa is a great teacher who explains everything clearly and encourages students to ask questions. He posts the power points he uses online so you can reference them on your own. The class is difficult and you will have to study but he is always available to help. The homework is the hardest part, but he gives lots of time for each assignment.

Class is not easy if you have no prior programming experience, but Chris does a good job explaining everything. Don't get frustrated. The HW problems are far from intuitive, but he give a lot of time to complete each assignment. Ask questions. Recommended.

I have to say that after taking Dr.Suma, I have come to understand that he is one of the nicest guys you will ever meet,Im not saying his class will be easy, it was for me, you will need to study, His test are hard, but if you study you will be fine, his Home work assignment was fun to write, all in all i really enjoyed his class,highly recommended
Very good teacher. He is very concerned with a students progress and is more than willing to help you should you need it. Course material is to the point and on topic. I would highly recommend him.
Ridiculously unclear in class, on homework prompt, and test questions and if you ask him a question, regardless of "is this a or b," his answer will be incomprehensible.

The professor as a person is very sweet, but as a professor he's not great at all. Reads off ppts all the lectures, slow talking even at 2x speed, constantly cancelling class and uploading lectures recorded 2 years ago, and doesn't get back to you with graded work until all the way at the end of the semester. Don't take this class with him.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: Boring, monotoned lectures off of useful PowerPoints. Long, but sparse, homework assignments, which he didn't grade until the end of the semester (20% of grade). He gives sample tests from previous semesters as study material and allows a single page cheat sheet on exams (the other 80% of grade). Overall, pretty easy class to pass.

Hes a great professor. The lectures consist of PowerPoint slides that are very clear. For the tests, he provides old tests to study from that are very similar to the tests he gives out. Overall, very clear and you will actually learn a lot about algorithm analysis.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

review_text: The worst class I have ever taken. I didn't learn anything in this class. Boring lecture, lengthy tests, last minute homework. And never got back the homework grade in the whole semester.

This professor has a passion for the subject and appears to want us to succeed. He is very thorough on test reviews so take accurate notes and ask questions. What he teaches is on the tests, but so much comes from the textbook, which tends to emphasize how important certain topics are compared to slides. Read the book and go to class.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS

review_text: He is very amicable with students.

Dr. Depano is ALWAYS available to help anyone who needs it. If you have real life problems that are causing you difficulty, he understands and will be fair and accommodate your situation within reason to make sure you don't drop the course.

This class is an easy A if you have previous programming experience.  If not, you might be in trouble.  He is very helpful if you ask questions though, and he's probably one of the friendliest and smartest professors at UNO.  He takes FOREVER to grade tests and homework, or at least his grad assistants do.

His focus is not in C++. He is a java guy. A few times in class he even asked questions he didn't know to the problems. If you want to learn and understand. Don't take him. Otherwise, I don't recommend at all.

I agree with everyone else. This teacher is not effective. Worst teacher I have had thus far. Tests are very easy, little to no participation required in class. If anything, the lab is the most useful part of the class. Never grades material and bores one to death with lecture material.Sadly, he seems as uninterested as the students.

If you like hearing your teacher when they talk, knowing when things are do, enjoy teachers being punctual and on time, as well as silencing and not answering their phone during class, avoid this guy. In addition to the overall pointlessness of the class, he is not an engaging teacher. The lab is useful, his lecture is not.

This class is very boring. You deff dont need to buy the book. The test questions come stright from the practice tests. He is VERY UNCLEAR about what he wants from us in class. He never grades anything on time. No one knew what to do for the homeworks and no one knew what the heck was going on most of the time. Dont take this guy.

He's ok. His class is very boring. If you study from the power points, half of what you study won't be on the exam. He's always late to class, which takes away from test time. Despite all this, his practice test study guide has the EXACT same questions as his exams.

For an intro class, it was ridiculous. He gets TOO detailed/talks too much. Waste of money on the book; he made his own chapters. Tests are UNBELIEVABLE. Short answers that require a full page of writing, T&F, and mult choice. Very unorganized, randomly cancels classes without telling you. Never grades things on time, or at all.
Excellent instructor! Extremely helpful and prompt response to emails!
he is awesome. easy grader. but his classes are boring. i usually taught myself by reading the book.
One of the best in the Dept. Awesome man. Nobody in the dept. been as helpful as Dr. Depano.. And no, he doesn't pass everyone...
He passes anyone.. does not set the bar high at all..
No Comments
He's a easy teacher.

He's ok. I've had better. His slide shows are kinda helpful and the face that he posts them on hiw site is good too. I dont pay that much attention in class because he's boring, but I teach myself by reading the book and the slide shows. I got a B in 1583
No Comments

I love this man. Simply I love him. God bless his ability to teach because he taught in a way which is super hard for me to forget the material. I took networking and telecom  with him — he's a straight up GOAT.
Professor Nur is very sweet and understanding. Show up and listen, and you'll know what you need to pass. No outside reading required (but, as any other csci course, practice coding!).
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Caring--Graded by few things

review_text: I'm not a fan of him refusing to let you take a test at a later time if it's not a medical emergency, but he was very lenient when it happened to me. He's really easy to talk to and very understanding. Clearly explains things to the point where someone like me with no previous csci experience understands everything.

Although he gives an exam recap for each of his exams, and is always open for questions, in lectures he explains 1+1, hw's is 10+10, exams are 25x+25x. He explains everything he gets in the exam but always likes to spice things up. Overall he's super nice and cool and wants students to succeed, LOTS of bonus opportunities , definitely recommend!
He a boss

Dr. Nur is a perfect example of a great professor. Although lectures may be difficult for some, he will always be there to help if you ask for it. Do not be afraid to reach out to him, he is on your side.. As a professor and as a normal human being!! Love this guy.

Professor Nur is very caring and helpful. His tests are very easy and come straight from his exam review. Little programming is required. Attend classes for the pop quizzes as it contributes a lot to the final grade. Its an easy A class with professor Nur. Would like to take him again.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Beware of pop quizzes--Caring

review_text: Game Dev 1 was, hands-down, the most fun I had during my computer science BS. It felt like a "just for fun" elective class at a times. The 2 projects of creating original games were real work, for sure, but how often do you get to play your favorite video games for homework? Dr. Samuel bring panache to his lectures and syllabus.

I went in with 0 knowledge of any programming languages, but his real life examples helped me to understand things very easily. I'm extremely shy, but he is very approachable and doesn't make me feel less/weird. He made it a point to learn everyone's name since the first day and I really appreciated that. I love the sense of community in his class.

Literally the best professor I've ever had. He goes very in-depth in his lectures, covering all the material in the course and then some while still keeping everything understandable. Very invested in students. He made me interested in the subject itself (it's now my major) and I honestly can't recommend him enough.

Dr Ben is engaging and animated, keeps material interesting, and spends a good amount of time reinforcing fundamental building blocks of programming and making sure students understand. Homeworks are straightforward with room for personal creative expression(which he loves) and hes always available in his office to help with anything

His speaking style, often sprinkled with qualifiers, can be difficult to follow for some (but not all) students. If you're a Comp Sci beginner, this class is packed with a lot of dense material regardless of which prof it's taken with. Expect to work. Dr. S is willing to give extra help, and won't get frustrated if you don't understand at first.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: She changed the course from what it used to be, now with more weight on tests instead of building the actual compiler (assignments). Her lectures are super boring and her slides are poorly written and will sometimes contain wrong information for something that will be on an exam. Assignments are very unbalanced, unclear and don't guide you at all.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: I took this class in 2024 and loved it. The labs are tricky, but manageable with OH. The professor is very engaging in lectures and super passionate about security. I loved to learn from her! This class is on a uni-level standard. Definitely challenging, but in the best way possible. Don't let other reviewers fool you, take this class!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Sturton's 590 (formal methods for security) is a great research-focused CS elective. No programming; homework is reading research papers. Grades are from ~10 very doable quizzes and a group research project. She takes a while to grade but is exceptionally kind and helpful in OH. Recommend if you liked COMP 455 or prefer discussion-based classes.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Accessible outside class

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: 0 detail of slides. Practice tests? Oh u mean polleverywhere, yes pollev questions r highly related to tests, do that. Labs are busy work with screenshot, I hope they can come up with some actual original up-to-date labs that teach u stuff. Exam grading is like Teemo, annoying, u would get points off for somethign stoopid. Professor is chill, tho.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: I really liked this course and I thought she was very kind and knowledgeable. One thing though is that there is a LOT of material for the exams. Other than that, solid course.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Please do not take this class with her. Every time I step into this class, I fear something terrible might happen. Her lecture slides have less detailing and poor formatting, and it's a matter of whether you know the content or not. The labs are sometimes lengthy; only OH can save you on that. Other than that, please refrain yourself from this.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Great crash course for cybersecurity! Professor Sturton is a caring professor who you can tell loves to teach the material. If you attend class, take notes, and read the textbook you will be okay! Labs were not really a stretch from the material, but did require outside research at times. 5 FREE late days given for the labs & drops lowest lab!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: A good lecturer. Interesting class. But lectures arent recorded, and I could not remember information from class, so I dropped in fear of knowing that I cant do well on the exam if I cant remember infromation from class. Slides arent that detailed either.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: The most boring and useless class I have ever taken. The professor has a terrible lecture format with slides that contain very little information by themselves. It's not even that hard a class, the professor was just so atrocious I didn't want to do the work and wasn't interested. It's a shame, because I though computer security would be cool.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Would avoid classes with her. Content is interesting however her teaching style effects the experience negatively.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: it's the way i'm feeling i just can't deny. you need to remember a lot of useless information for the exam.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

review_text: OK lecture and assignments but bad logistics. We had to wait so looooong before we received any feedbacks on our midterms, labs, and finals. university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: I actually found the material to be really cool which is the only reason I didnt loathe the class. That being said, Sturton is a genius and nice person even if a bad prof.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Smart in the subject, but incompetent as a professor. university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

review_text: Good teacher with horrible assignments.  His lectures are quality, and fun, but his homework and project assignments are riddled with typos, mistakes, and confusion.  Meeting him during his office hours will just confuse you more.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Doesn't communicate expectations clearly, and expects a lot of strange/annoying things in assignments.  Hard to contact for help/issues via email.  Doesn't communicate well with TAs.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: This guy takes too much time teaching stuff you should already know.  The class is just annoyingly slow.  He does make up for a bit with a sense of humor, but that doesn't change the fact that he's just not teaching much. Also, he missed one of our review sessions.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Very easy class if you do exactly what the assignments ask. The grading on assignments is very lenient as long as you know what you are talking about. He takes attendance on random days. Only a midterm and a final, and you can make a cheatsheet.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Not a great class, but not too hard. I skipped a lot of lectures because I found them boring and not helpful. There weren't too many assignments and they all built off of each other. review_text: Nothing in this class is difficult besides the exams, but even those don't make up a large portion of the overall grade.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Don't really understand what all this hate is about. This professor was better than others in the CS department and his expectations were really clear. I mean if you just read his announcement, he practically told you each question on the final. His tests weren't bad. His expectations were clear and the class was easy if you study and do your work.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: OK class not considering exams which are extremely difficult for no reason.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Don't recommend to take this class. People who had taken this class told me that it would be a good elective, but it wasn't -- exam questions are tricky and professor doesn't know how to design exams.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Poorly designed quizzes and midterm. Otherwise, the class is worse than mediocre. The problem is that professor doesn't teach you anything during lectures and then asks you questions on midterms that are not even doable in an hour. No clue what the quizzes are for for each assignment. 6 assignments, 6 class works, 6 quizzes, midterm, final - bad!!!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: This is my first time writing a review on RMP because it's the first class I've taken where the material wasn't actually taught. The midterm and quizzes are poorly written, and with Tarik essentially running the class, it's no surprise it has turned out so lackluster.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: If you do an assignment perfectly you get an 80 + your score on the quiz. Quizzes are way too hard and the midterm was super specific trivia/gotcha questions that barely applied what we learned. Instead of applying the android dev skills we are learning, we are expected to memorize tiny specific details for quizzes and exams. Don't take this class.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Painfully difficult class to succeed in.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Assignments are straightforward at times, but sometimes they require knowledge beyond the scope of what is taught in the class. Midterms and quizzes are tough. My guess is that the TAs, not Professor Nijorn himself, create the midterm and quiz questions. This might explain why many students perform poorly on these assessments.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Exams and quizzes are made by Tarik and other TAs, but Tarik can't create effective midterm questions. Many students did poorly on the midterm, and having a quiz for each assignment feels excessive, especially given the poorly crafted questions. Avoid this class if possible; it's frustrating to pay tuition for a class that isn't properly taught.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Not an easier elective as it used to be. He made the class more difficult, and I don't think he really knows the stuff he's teaching well. Midterm was very difficult, and I can't imagine how difficult the final is going to be.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Nirjon was good, no complaints. His lectures could be boring but they basically give you a walkthrough for the assignments which build off the last.  Class is easy if you attend lecture and complete the "Class Works".  As stated below grading was a little frustrating as it took all of class period for the TA to get to you. Overall great class
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: programming assignments were fair and not very difficult, they are pretty much cumulative and so is the final project. Dry lectures, but nice fella. the grading system for the assignments was pretty frustrating b/c they need to be manually checked off but the prof or TA and you end up sitting around waiting. exam questions try to trip you up though

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: This is an easy class if you put in the work. And you will be learning more on your own than from the professor. But don't be mistaken, you will get a lot out of this.   By the end I had a functional mobile application that I am very proud of.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Nirjon was great!! I genuinely learn SO MUCH in this class, but hardly felt stressed about it. We spent the class learning about API's, databases, sensors, and we ended by learning how to program an Alexa (so cool). It is all tied together in a final project that you work on throughout the year. Such a good talking point for job interviews. 10/10!!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: His lectures were concise and homework assignments (3) were straightforward. A lot of time during class to work on Class Work assignments and Professor Nirjon always seemed eager to help on any assignments/project components. Midterm and Final Exam were open-book and easy. He's understanding and lenient, I would definitely take this class again!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Nirjon tries to make sure the students are learning.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Nirjon is alright. 590 was the Android Dev. class and the assignments were time consuming but interesting and worth it. The quizzes however were ridiculous. Nobody did well on them and they quizzed us on strangely specific things. The latter half of the semester was just graduate student presentations (ugh) but overall a relatively easy A/A-

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: goat. super chill, derives ideas bottom up. dense but well organized lectures

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: The exams for this course are difficult if you don't have a very strong math foundation. However, the assignments and teamwork are very helpful. You can learn a lot from them.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Test heavy

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Do not take this class.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Prof. Oliva is fantastic. Amazingly intelligent, articulate, and he stands out as one of my favorite professors at Carolina. That being said, he teaches a difficult course—be prepared to work. There will not be much hand holding and lectures will focus on key theoretical concepts instead of implementation.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Junier Oliva is by far one the best professors I have ever had. The class itself (Machine Learning) was extremely difficult, requiring somewhere about 5-10 hours per lecture to supplement, understand, and digest the material, especially for some later lectures. However Oliva makes sure to teach the nuance and it makes for a very eye-opening course.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: He covered a good number of introductory ML topics but I don't think I took away much from the course in terms of understanding. Inconsistent grading criteria and a large gap in knowledge expected between class and the final project and exam. Other than bits of theory and derivations, I almost feel like self-study would be more productive.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: The professor is passionate about the subject but he doesn't realize it is under the CS department. The only coding component is Google Colab assignments you'll figure out on your own. Lectures and the midterm are entirely math-based with no programming instruction. Avoid if you want to learn practical Python skills for building and training models

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Definitely not an undergrad level course. You need a good foundation in stats, cal, and linear algebra to take this course. Otherwise, I don't think you would understand much of what he is saying. So I rec you don't take this class if you don't have enough prerequisites. Otherwise, he is nice and passionate about ML.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: What's the probability of this professor understanding that the students taking the class have other things to do? Probably another annoyingly long mathematical equation involving various interpretations that would be infinitely easier to understand if the professor actually taught the class instead of dumping random equations every lecture.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: He's nice and enjoys his specialization but he's teaching this course to undergrads at a grad level. Lectures filled with formulas that nobody understands how to work with. Idk how to actually train a model, what a tensor is, or how to use tensorflow. Only take this if you wanna research. Don't take if you wanna be an ML engineer, Udemy is better.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: One of those classes where you go to lecture and watch as 1 person understands everything, and the professor takes this to mean that everybody gets it.   This is a MATH/STOR course, not a COMP course. Heavy on the symbols, low on connections to real life. Little programming.   Strong assumption that you memorized all of LA, Probability, and Calc 3.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Homework and tests could be stressful for this course but the teacher is very approachable and responsive to students' questions and concerns. He seems to be very passionate about his field and is an excellent resource for anyone who really wants to learn

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: This is a GRAD level class not undergrad. He taught the exact same material as his COMP 755. Possibly the worst comp class I have had at UNC. In order to do well on exams/HW you have to already know ML/LinAlg well. He has no problem assigning an exam, project, presentation, and homework all the same week. He curves but not worth the stress at all
rating_tags: Group projects--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Szafir was denied tenure at CU Boulder in March of 2021. Instead of fulfilling his teaching duties for the remainder of the semester he offloaded lectures to TAs, stopped grading and stopped answering emails. He quiet quit before the term even had a name. Dan Szafir is still a laughing stock back here and we'll always remember it despite formality.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: This is a review for COMP 581 - Intro to Robotics Great class with hands-on group projects where you build your lego robot and program it to complete tasks. The material covered in the lectures and exams is relevant to help you build your robot.  My only complaint is that sometimes the lectures move slowly covering basic introductions for too long.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Why are there negative reviews? Because there were no quizes or exams, and no one studied regularly. When professor asked students questions, no one answered because they did not study. Because of this, Professor would never offer a course again where there is no quiz or exams. And its the students fault.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Definitely one of the few professors who really care about students.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Group projects

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: A hidden gem in the CS department!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: One major project over the course of an entire semester was a bit underwhelming.  We wasted a lot of time doing nothing really besides talking and spinning our wheels. The class was easy, so it gets some points from me on that side, but the course wasn't good.  I had harder classes as a freshmen.  Dan is a nice guy though. All around average.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Awesome professor. Learned a lot about research from this course. Doing the research project was fun.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Human Robot Interaction course. A research based course. Awesome Professor, good as a Kris Jordan. No midterms, quizes or exams. Projects, discussions, papers and presentations. If you are interested in an intro to research course and research related to Robotics, this course is for you.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: This review is actually for COMP 581 which was not listed. Professor Szafir was an excellent intro to robotics professor. The most challenging part about the course was the robotics assignments in which you have to design your own controller for a robot. However, pay attention in class and you will be fine as he gives EC and allows retakes.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: I'm confounded as to why Dan Szafir was the professor for VR and Game Dev. He had no prior experience with game development and his knowledge of VR was only conceptual in nature. All of the technical skills needed to build our assignments were self-taught. Dan also had no examples of his own VR work to show us at all, only previous student work

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: I feel like a barely learned anything from Professor Szafir's class besides just, "Oh look,this engineering concept from a previous course applies here too, but only sort of and to actually implement it, it requires information never covered in class at all" Basically, prepare to teach yourself half of what you'll need on your own. Lazy Professor.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Stan was a very kind and considerate professor however was not the best lecturer. The lectures were mainly watching professors code and follow along without a lot of explanation as to why things were done. This course would be much easier if you have an understanding of programming beforehand, however is not impossible without any.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Stan is a good professor but the class design for Comp-116 was not good at all. It was all lectures, including the recitation, where the class would only involve copying what the professors are typing down. With it being a lecture heavy class, there was absolutely no engagement. Also, there was a big leap in difficulty in the assignments.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Professor Ahalt taught this class with Professor Krishnamurthy, and I loved it. For me, the workload in this class was very manageable. Both professors were extremely understanding of students struggling with mental health and other issues that kept them from completing work. I would highly, highly recommend this course.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Dr. Ahalt taught this class with Dr. Krishnamurthy and together they were an awesome dynamic duo! The workload isn't tough and it's easy to end with a good grade, but the best part is listening to two great data science minds work through problems in lecture. You get a great intro to Python, and I would highly recommend taking the class!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Goated

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: A fantastic professor and cool guy. He taught this semester tag-team with Dr. Krishnamurthy, and both were great! The course is very well designed. Lectures are helpful but not required if you read the book. Course is easy not in the sense that it's a joke but that you'll know how to code by the time it's done. Highly recommend!

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: One of the better lecturers I have had at Carolina. He used a lot of mathematical terms to explain some of the concepts and then explained it in a more general way. Overall, I think he was one of the most enthusiastic professors I have had when it comes to being excited about the material.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: One of the most passionate and compassionate teachers I've ever had at UNC.  The work is substantial but relevant and interesting.  The final is a creative group project which turns out to be pretty fun.  He's a really cool guy that really just wants you to learn code and it's importance in our world.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: The best professor I've had at UNC. He obviously cares about what he's teaching, and that the students learn the material. He asks for input changes the pace of the class based on what students tell him. It's easy to understand him and to get help from him. As an added bonus, he's actually an entertaining professor even in lectures.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: She's an amazing lecturer. Yes, she might come off as rude but she is genuinely not trying to be based on my long conversations with her. She helped me a lot with understanding NLP concepts. She's definitely a gem of the CS department given her experience and the fact that she takes her job seriously.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Made the course a lot difficult. No lecture recordings, no practice for exams, homeworks are difficult. I would avoid taking a course with her.

review_text: The content of the course is good. The Professor was rude.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: A good lecturer, but not a good person. Half of the class could not get their projects right, and fundamental mistakes were caught. It proves how much less support she gave the class. Dont take.

university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: She can clearly explain concepts fairly well. She is incredibly unresponsive, though. She mostly uses Piazza for group discussions, however we don't get an answer for days or sometimes even weeks. There are some challenging concepts in the subject, therefore having a supporting lecturer would be more beneficial.
