university: University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
review_text: Dont take unless you are ready to go through some brutal crazy hard stuff.

This is an interesting professor with an interesting teaching style. It's a hit or miss. Projects and labs are doable. Grading is delayed significantly (someone prefers to spend time here instead!), up to a few months for grading a midterm, so do not expect to know your score anytime soon.

The professor strictly enforces academic integrity policies. In one case I was aware of, a violation had immigration consequences involving a student visa and ultimately deportation. Make sure you understand the rules and follow them.

Every SWE uses AI to develop and write code.  He doesn't think that is the case, he would've been a good prof 10 years ago but his philosophy is outdated in modern CS and he honor-coded and failed 60% for getting help with AI to write code. Midterms were fair though, and he is extremely knowledgeable in the subject.

To preface- I was not one of the 60% of people caught cheating. Every bad assignment I handed in was 100% hand-rolled. I frankly didn't like Billingsley's class format at all (was destroyed by the final), but he deserves to teach this class. He is very passionate about the subject and gives great lectures. His AI policy was tough, but very fair.

Billingsley is a fantastic lecturer. He broke things down to very simple topics, then built everything up with great examples. He quite literally tells you exactly what to do on the labs and projects. His exams are very reasonable and practical.  Just don't use AI. It's cheating. Can't believe people were mad about getting caught cheating.

Do not take him for 447, his 449 class was fine but he went on a witch hunt for his 447 class. The department removed him from teaching any 447 classes spring semester because of the witch hunt. Nice guy at first, but be weary.

It's so blatantly obvious that Billingsley came to this site and gave himself good reviews with some of these ratings and comments, lmao. Don't be fooled, be aware of this guy because he switches up. He will be out to get you. I mean, why would he say nothing and wait till the end of the course to "catch" over 60% of the class? say something first

DO NOT USE AI. His midterms were easy if you put effort into studying. There will be some reviews giving him a bad rating for failing due to using AI, but I believe that it was more on me to be honest. He cares and will pass you if you dont use AI and understand the concepts that he presents to you.

DO NOT USE AI IN THIS CLASS. DO NOT USE AI IN THIS CLASS.  I'm really comfortable with the material taught there, and I exclusively used AI to speed up debugging, but he had this huge witch hunt near the end, and (correctly) caught me using some AI on the labs. Because of that my grade tanked. I think it was a little bs, but a good wakeup call.

Professor Billingsley is a great lecturer. He makes quite a bland topic very engaging. He has a incredibly strict AI policy. Many students were automatically failed for using AI on projects. The majority of the class is graded based on exams which for a computer science class kind of sucks. Professor is great, I am slightly unhappy with his grading

This professor lowkey is mentally unstable and has rapid mood swings. He would snap and get angry for kids trying to find seats, no one answering, etc. then wonders why ppl don't go to office hour  Scribbles all over his iPad, jumping from one end to the other making it hard to understand and follow, doesn't post any notes afterwards. Good luck!

IF HE THINKS THAT SOFTWARE ENGINEERS DONT USE AI IN THIS DAY AND AGE HE IS OBLIVIOUS!!! LIKE NO ONE IS WRITING MIPS CODE FROM SCRATCH ANYMORE, GET WITH THE PROGRAM. SERIOUSLY NEEDS TO CHANGE HIS AND THE UNIVERSITY'S AI POLICY BC I BET IT WILL ONLY GET WORSE THROUGH THE YEARS

I have personal beef/admiration for him. Props to him 4 catching everyone and sticking with the act of failing over half the class, but also he is a part time professor - there is literally no need to go so hard on students that use ai for debugging. Also, my king, why r u making us take a cumulative final for the first time in 8 years. He hates us

Good lecturer, but gets angry at students for not answering questions. He said at the end of NOVEMBER that he suspected that 10% of the class used ai on a lab that was due beginning of OCTOBER. like literally why wait to tell us until a week before finals, just send out a warning then. Complete switch up this sem, pretty difficult

Does not post his notes until 3 days before the exam, all of the notes you have to hand write so coming to class in mandatory. labs have good directions, but projects are pretty difficult. exams in my opinion were pretty difficult too. don't even try using ai in this class, had so many meetings the week before finals and failed many students.

Great professor, provides you everything you need to pass labs, projects, and exams. He really wants you to learn the material which is why is he so strict on AI usage and the reason for these bad reviews recently. Def still the best professor for 447.

Avoid tbh. He takes forever to grade tests and projects and waits until the last week in the semester to do so. He also decided to catch over half the class for using AI this semester and waited until literally the LAST weeks of the semester to do so (after the withdrawal deadline). He basically set half the class up for failure by doing so.
he's the best professor for cs0447. engaging, knowledgable, fun, VERY clear lab and project instructions. not always the most approachable, but he was fine aside from that

Its hard to tell, Jarrett is definitly a fun guy for you can see from his lecture. He tried to make everyone get involved and understand course yet he is too strick on giving out help more than instructions he gave for labs and projects. He is unstable and chaotic from my perspective.

He is so terrible- DO NOT TAKE. He went through something last semester because he completely changed he entire course and failed over 70% of the class for slight uses of AI. He is having 100+ academic integrity meetings during finals week to make sure everyone fails his class. He was good in previous sems but is terrible now
rating_tags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

review_text: hard to approach with questions and not very accomodating
love this guy. most clear directions for projects and labs great guy wants u to succeed just lock in!!!! ATTEND LECTURE SO WORTH IT ONLY LECTURE I ATTENDED ALL SEMESTER
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Test heavy

review_text: Jarrett makes this class very easy, and even though sometimes I really wanted to sleep in his lectures they were very good. Projects and labs, esp asm ones, were so fun! Exams are also easy if you carefully review everything and do his exercises.

Billingsley is a great professor. Out of all the teachers for 447 and 449 he is definitely the one you wanna take. His lectures are clear and entertain you better than most. The work for 447 isn't too crazy, but not easy either. Make sure you get everything done correctly and on time. Exams are definitely reasonable, go over the slides.

He has an attitude a lot of the time but other than that he's pretty helpful over Discord and stuff. Labs and projects were honestly a lot harder than I expected but satisfying when you finally figure it out. Exams were fair but his grading scheme makes it kinda hard to end with an A

Not sure what the guy below me is talking about, I'm assuming they're referencing 447 instead of 445 lol. I do agree that Jarrett is kinda overhyped, but still is decent. He gets a lil heated during class if no one answers his questions lmao. Exams are weighted at 50% (25% each) and you need to score a 88 average to even be considered for an A.
rating_tags: Test heavy--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

review_text: Not good, not bad, just average.I don't understand the hype around this prof, but all in all, he's okay. The class is graded on 2 exams. You need an 88 average exam grade (Midterm and final) to score an A in his class (94) with a 100% on everything else. His labs are decently fun; asm = bad, logism = fun. Projects are hell, so start early or fail.

Jarrett's a funny guy. You can definitely tell he's a little tired of teaching the same subject constantly, but he is very good at it. Makes everything very easy to understand and basically walks you through everything. Would recommend.

This is my second time taking this professor, and I love how he can take difficult topics and make it easier to understand. From what I heard, he's gives out the easier homeworks/projects than the other profs so I totally recommend. Keep in mind that his exams are hard, so you have to do well on them to pass the class.

I recommend Jarrett highly for 447. This course focuses on assembly language & circuits, which Jarrett managed to make fun through helpful lectures and labs. He has a website that displays the content taught on what days. Make sure to review his materials page and stay on top of running his example files.
Jarrett is a genuine goat, I wish I could take him for 449
Jarrett is one of the best professors I've had at Pitt, period. It's a crime he's only a part time instructor. If I could take him for 449 I would.

Jarrett makes some topics that can be dull very interesting. Although lectures aren't mandatory, I would show up because he is very entertaining and gives amazing insight/information. Expect to spend some time on the labs, projects, and studying but nothing that isn't manageable.

Jarrett is one of the best CS professors at Pitt. His lectures are engaging and he breaks down the material really well. The projects can be challenging, but using unstuck ai and the class resources makes it manageable. Overall, a great class to take.

THE BEST lectures and slides I've seen, made class genuinely fun to go to. Very instructive and gives great feedback on tests. Exams had lots of point opportunities, but final harder than midterm. Labs and projects were instructive and none that difficult. Did not expect to like this class but Jarrett made it my favorite this semester.

jarrett is so funny. his exams are fair and doable if you work on exercises given on his website. i liked that the slides had lots of visuals in them. he's good at explaining things with analogies. i didnt feel that way but i see how people might think he's condescending. i definitely think you should take a class with him cause you learn a lot!

Makes the best slides in the department, good lectures, and doable projects (just follow the instructions). Exams are detailed so make sure you review and fully understand. But he struggles to control his mood and sometimes loses his mind/picks on people on minor issues. C'mon! Relax bro!

No one mentioned that his teaching style is more challenging for non-native English speakers. He writes his own materials instead of using a textbook, which makes it harder for me to fully grasp the concepts. I found it even more confusing to me when he tried to simplify things. Exams are dry and biology-like, and without allowing a cheat sheet.

One of the best lecturers in the department.His slides are great. Projects and labs feel like busy work, but sometimes are quite interesting. If you go to his oh, or ask a question he is quite intimidating and might even be aggressive. Do work without chatgpt/google and go to TA's oh. Prof is kinda paranoid about cheating.

Jarrett was a cool professor and I definitely recommend you take him over some of the other people in the department. Pretty good lectures, knows the content, very accessible via Discord, exams were very fair, the only knack is that sometimes I felt he could come off somewhat condescending and I think it made people scared to answer questions.
callous, and a bit condescending — in a bid to come off as personable, kinda does the opposite
Labs and projects were fun and doable. Jarrett is very accessible outside of class. Tests were very fair.

I'm making this rating because I despise Misurda's 449. I took Jarrett for 447 and loved his lectures and especially his lecture slides. I was lazy so missed out on getting Jarrett for 449 and was stuck with Misurda. His style of teaching is terrible so I ended up skipping all the lectures and just used Jarrett's 449 slides to learn instead.
Chill guy but weird sometimes. Overall best teacher for 447/449 teacher.

Literally one of, if not the best, CS professor at Pitt. Great, funny lectures that teach the material really well. Projects and labs are pretty much a breeze because of the great instructions he gives. Literally such a good professor, take him if you can!!!

It was a hard class for me, but there is no other prof I would rather take it from. Very knowledgeable about the material, excited to share that knowledge, incredibly helpful, and very reachable. Maybe the best teacher I've ever had. It's not a filler class, but if you need the requirement then this is where you want to get it.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

review_text: Jarrett is funny and doesn't talk down to students. Lectures are great and make difficult subject matter seem simple. Likes to baby you on projects and labs, which can bite you sometimes if you want to do something on your own, but not a major issue by any means. Class ramps up towards the end, so make sure to show up and listen.

Really easy class with step by step instructions given to you for the labs and projects. Exams were also super easy if you just study the powerpoints. Jarret is amazing at getting back to you through discord and will answer almost any time of day. He puts so much effort into teaching which can be seen through all of the resources he has on his page

Jarrett is very organized and he's definitely more connected to students than other professors. His lectures are clear and easy to follow, but try not to miss any. The projects were reasonably difficult, but Jarrett is very accessible outside of lecture and even outside of office hours. Definitely the best choice for 447.

Jarrett is a great professor and is very funny if you are into his humor. However, he is very strict and his exams are very hard. His reviews do not help at all and same with his materials on his page. He is also very strict with cheating so make sure you do not collaborate with students in this class even if you are just discussing the projects!
Best option hands down for 447

This class is definitely the CS class at Pitt. Jarrett makes it pretty interesting and I do find that I have learned a bit more about programming fundamentals that are somewhat helpful in other CS classes. I preferred the first half of the course to the second but regardless he does an excellent job lecturing and engaging students + easy class.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

review_text: Best Prof I have had at Pitt. The class is fair and teaches you a lot. The first midterm was a lot harder than expected, but the final was very easy which made up for it. I wish others in this department could learn to be more like him.

Billingsley puts so much effort towards the success of his students in CS 0447. Goes through the effort to handmake online interactive exercises for every set of slides. Find me another professor at Pitt who does this. Great sense of humor, effective teacher. Take this man's class!

clear lecture, exam content was carefully reviewed and lectured and is on ppt, if u fail u didnt review all materials provided. provide practice problems that reflects similarly to exam short answers, v organized. discord/TA/virtual office hr very helpful, course staffs reply/address dm questions on time with clear explanations. best prof for 447
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

review_text: He posted all of his slides online and recorded lectures if there was a reason to.  His labs and projects basically had walkthrough tutorials besides the project 2, but project 2 had slides that helped.  His discord server always had him and TAs ready to help with any issues with labs and projects.  I would recommend him for CS classes.
He is really funny and makes the class interesting. He helped me out a lot with the project and understanding the concepts. Highly recommend!

Jarrett's honestly the best professor I've had in the CS department. Exams can be difficult if you don't study. Labs and projects are an appropriate difficulty, and honestly I had a lot of fun doing them. Join his discord, that's the best way to reach him (He's good at answering email too though). Some won't like his sense of humor, but I do!

Such a great professor with a very well-organized course. Everything you could possibly need is easily accessible on the course website he made, including exercises that are really helpful for understanding the content and on exams. I wish all of my professors would structure their classes and teach like he does.

Jarrett was literally was such an awesome professor. His lectures and slides were super engaging with lots of jokes and prepared you super well for labs/projects, which were very easy to complete. He cares a lot about his students and is super accessible and friendly. I felt I genuinely learned a lot in his class because of how well he taught it

The class was extremely easy, as every assignment step was given on the page, so there is no thinking involved. The exams aren't really difficult either, as all the exam material is covered by the slides so you can just look over the slides for when you need to study. This is probably the easiest CS courses offered.
rating_tags: Respected--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: Amazing professor. Engaging, available and funny. Lecture slides are top-notch and the visualizations really help with learning. Only nitpick is that the lab/project instructions are definitely too good. Each step is written out to the smallest detail which leaves little to imagine or learn from. Its an easy but very interesting class.

Billingsley is genuinely funny. Go to lecture when you can but he also posts the notes online if you miss it. He answers lots of questions in a mocking tone which could upset some people. Very helpful and active in discord, and even if he mocks you, he will answer your question exactly how you need it answered. Tests aren't too hard. Use his site.
Easy material. Very good lectures. Labs & projects are easy and have clear directions. Test are medium difficulty but overall easy class.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Online Savvy

review_text: Jarrett's structure of the class is really organized! He's also extremely clear on his expectations for students and is accessible outside of class. Sometimes the lectures get boring though but that could just be the content
rating_tags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: Jarrett is the best. He's always accessible outside of class to help on labs/assignments. His lectures are really informative, focusing on the "big picture," concepts that you should take away but also having some depth on the content. Exams are entirely from his slides, so study them (lots of slides). But he does a good job of noting what to know

Absolute goat. Very good at explaining concepts and lectures were generally pretty engaging. Labs and projects basically had step-by-step guides on how to complete them. Exams aren't multiple choice but they're still pretty easy otherwise. Very easy to at least pass the class if you enjoy computer science and put in basically any amount of effort.

Jarrett is a fantastic teacher. Slides are easy to understand and extremely straightforward. Learned a lot in lecture and occasionally had fun doing the labs and projects. Super available online in his discord channel also. He can make it way too easy at times, but overall I enjoyed learning from him.

Easiest CS class at Pitt. Projects are mad easy but the class is graded by pretty much nothing except the projects and exams. Exams are easy but you don't get very much partial credit, you basically either get stuff right or wrong. Jarrett's always online to help but I didn't need any.  The guy has redditor humor, you either like or hate it.

Probably the best CS prof at Pitt. Awesome lecturer, unparalleled availability/helpfulness outside lecture. You learn the material well w/o being super stressed. Compilers was a surprisingly useful class and made me a much better coder. 1 minor gripe, gives too much starter code/project instructions so you don't learn quite as much as you could.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

review_text: Most of the other reviews are quite accurate. He is really relaxed and explains concepts well. He is super accessible through his discord and is overall a very fun guy. He wants you to understand the "big picture" but doesn't hide any important details. Great class, great professor.

Super nice guy. I loved his lectures because he was so good at explaining complex concepts plainly. All the assignments are really easy if you don't put them off and the exams aren't bad at all. Sometimes It felt like I wasn't being challenged enough in the class because his expectations are pretty low, but he's still a great professor.

Good overview of architecture and actual implementation/design that be can built upon (might be a little light for some but he is a good teacher). He demands clear questions and communication (can be irritable and brusque at times). If you can't pass his class with honest hard work, you might want to reconsider computer science.

Jarrett is a great lecturer. All his lectures are interesting and his slides are engaging and extremely helpful as he allows you to download them off his website. He is also very easy to reach out to on discord, which is great. He also made cookies after our final! Great professor. Would 100% take him again for another class.

GREAT professor. Very casual relationship with students, not intimidating (made us cookies for after our final :)). The exams were difficult, but not impossible. Labs each week were pretty clear and easy to complete. Projects were difficult but taught me a lot. Overall a pleasant experience. (Lectures did get kinda boring sometimes but oh well).

Best CS professor I've taken so far. It's often hard for me to stay engaged with CS lectures but I never had issues with him. Humorous and always has his explanations concise and well presented, I rarely felt lost. He is extremely accessible to help with projects outside of class over Discord, and very approachable.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

review_text: Jarrett is great. I did not expect to enjoy the course material, but his assignments were actually a lot of fun. He gives plenty of time for assignments and is available outside of class. He doesn't make things any harder than they need to be, which is a nice change of pace, and I feel as though I learned a lot from this class. 10/10.
Jarret is phenomenal. Super understanding and lays out everything clearly. Takes (imo) boring topics and made them interesting. Overall amazing class.

I'd take any class he is teaching, he's every bit as good as the other reviews say. Engaging lectures that really help understanding. Extremely accessible outside class and always willing to help. Grade mostly based on 2 non cumulative exams and 2 big projects which were great learning experiences and fun. Pitt needs to hire him full-time!
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: TAKE A COURSE WITH BILLINGSLEY. Seriously one of the best lecturers at Pitt. He genuinely cares about your learning and wants you to succeed. There are so many resources that he provides to get help in his class. TAKE HIS CLASS!!

Billingsley is a top-tier professor. It's clear he takes his job seriously and really goes the extra mile for his students. Whether it's messaging him on Discord for prompt homework help, his AMAZING slideshow lectures, his interesting and engaging labs or just his overall approach to the topics he teaches, he is a FANTASTIC choice. My GOAT prof

Jarrett might be the coolest prof you could have at Pitt. The dude cares so much about the wellbeing of his students. His lectures are fun (which I gotta give him credit for since we were online only this semester). His class is challenging, but he gives you more than enough resources to excel here. Love this dude.

This was the first professor at the University where I felt like I learned something other than for a grade. His lectures are fun to watch, and the tests are graded extremely fairly! Take Jarrett if you want to learn CS and have a good time doing so.

Jarrett was easily the best professor I have taken, and possibly will take. He takes relatively boring lectures, and makes them very interesting. He loves what he does, has a passion for his students. He is the most human professor, and his personal Discord server makes communication SO easy. TAKE HIM IF YOU CAN HE IS AN ABSOLUTE GEM!!!!

I loved Jarretts lectures but he seemed to tap out halfway through the semester. Its nearing the end of the semester and our midterms havent been graded. He stopped giving assignments, which was nice because I could focus that time on other classes but I feel like I missed out because his assignments are so good.
rating_tags: Accessible outside class--Graded by few things--Amazing lectures

review_text: very cool. made hard topic easy.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

review_text: Billingsley is one of those gems of a teacher that only come around a few times in your entire life. He takes topics as dry as computer processors & assembly languages and makes you look forward to learning about them. He's also super accessible outside of class on his personal Discord server. Definitely take him for every class you can.

This was the first time Jarrett taught CS1622. This was over the summer 2020 semester, during COVID19. He has a personal interest in compiler design, and it really showed with how detailed he made the animations/demonstrations on his slides and in lecture. We were supposed to have 5 projects creating a compiler using Rust but we only had 2.

He uses Discord (not mandatory) to be very accessible to students outside of class. We were supposed to have 4 projects but he only assigned us 2. Tests were very straightforward from reviewing the slides. Has great slides as well.

Jarrett has constantly proven to be one of the best lecturers at Pitt. He has taken a topic as dry as Compiler Design, and made it something worth looking forward to every lecture. He has a great mind for these topics and is more than willing to share his knowledge with you. He is a very fair grader and gives projects that help you learn
Take him! He's the best Pitt has to offer for CS professors. You will never go wrong with Jarrett. He will remind you about how fun it is to learn CS when you don't have to suffer.

Jarrett is chill and relatable. His lectures are great and it's clear what's important to know. Projects/labs are very explicit+easy to follow and if you're stuck you can contact him on his discord. Fair warning: he does get burnt out at the end of most semesters. Your grade will be based on fewer things than you think and grading may be slow

Jarrett is the only professor who I've ever been excited to attend lecture. He did suffer bad burnout this semester and only gave us 8/10 labs & 2/3 projects, and we didn't get our 2nd project or exam grades back yet, but he really showed me that there are actually some cool people in this awful field of CS. He'll hold a special place in my heart.

Best professor I've taken. Lecture is easy. Lab and project instructions are clear to lead you finish them. He wants every students in his class know what they are learning and he know how to do it. Amazing.

His class is really funny and interesting, while he does not have many policies and he is really does not care about that as long as you learn something. You can attend any section of his and no attendence, but his class is really fun you should never skip it. Well-organized and creative slides and projects. Surely take his class!

He is one of the best CS profs I've taken at Pitt. He lectures are super organize and his powerpoints are easy to understand as long as you attend lectures and pay attention. Fair exams. Super chill guy. Sometimes gets worn out and doesnt follow the syllabus for labs and projects. Def recommend!!

Jarrett is one of the best CS professors I have had. He takes a hard concept and breaks it down Barney style. He is a very good teacher! Towards the end of the semester, it seems he loses his mojo and doesn't fully commit to all the labs and projects as originally promised. Some may see that as a plus because its less work in the end.

Billingsley usually an amazing professor but he dropped the ball this semester. In CS447, he only assigned one project despite promising there'd be more. In CS449, he only assigned 3 projects, when 5 were promised. In fact, the last project was given on Wednesday before Finals week and due on Sunday (5 days). Hasn't grade midterms yet.
HE IS AMAZING. STOP SCROLLING ALREADY AND TAKE HIM!
Jarrett is an amazing professor who does an amazing job. If you are taking computer science, try your hardest to get him.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures

review_text: good professor. makes good cookies

One of the best teachers in the department. C programming can be daunting but he presents the information in such a palatable way. After so many rough CS classes, makes coding seem fun again. You can tell he cares and wants everyone to learn. He's hilarious and easily understanding if you have problems. I'd take him again for any class.

Jarrett's lectures are not just helpful and easy to understand. He tries his best to relate to his students and puts humor in his lectures, lab and project descriptions, and even tests. Every assignment was fair and I learned a lot from the class. Attendance for recitations and lectures are optional but highly encouraged.

Jarrett is one of my favorite professors I have had. He is an adjunct professor and should have tenure! His class allowed for you to really understand the material taught and if you don't understand something, he will help you to understand it. He also understands the current plight of college students which makes him relatable.

Lectures were very helpful and easy to follow (if you go to them). Never went to see him outside of lecture but he was always available. Projects were very difficult but graded fairly. Never went to labs and did well on all of them. I am taking him again for 449 because of the quality of his class. Best CS professor I've had yet.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Amazing lectures

review_text: Jarrett is a really great guy. His lectures are entertaining and super informative. He's super caring and easy to talk to if you have any issues or need any help. The exams, labs, and projects were all fair. Nothing was extremely difficult, but nothing was a joke. I really learned a lot in this class.

Jarrett is the best CS instructor at Pitt. He is very accessible through office hours and a class Discord server, gives funny but clear lectures, and encourages participation. Readings optional, but I found them helpful. Attendance at both recitation and lecture is optional as well, but you'll want to go to lecture.
Jarrett is the man. He is very fun and his lectures are great. The projects are fun, and the tests/grading is very fair. I would definitely recommend him for this course.

Is probably the best teacher I've ever had. Explains things to you in ways that are easy to understand. If you have questions he answers them in a very straightforward manner. I literally cannot fathom why anyone would give him under a 4 on this website. Class well taught. Very easily accessible. Bravo Jarrett.

Jarrett is so far my favorite CS professor at Pitt. He makes great easy to understand notes and the projects and exams are more than doable. He has a Discord server set up every semester for any of his classes, so his accessibility is probably one of the best. Highly recommended!

Jarrett is great!!!!!!Take his classes. I took his cs447 and cs449 this semester. He is a great lecturer; his lectures are interesting and you can definitely learn a lot; his slides are well-organized; he's willing to help not only in office hours, but also on discord; anyway take his class (go to his lectures and start on your project early)
Tough grader. But he is a good teacher. The lecture is good.
Makes class easy. Exams and quiz's are straight forward. Is always available to ask for help through discord

The project was undoable. Every lab and project description is long-winded. And I still can't say I am able to finish it by myself until now. His lecture is not totally useless but almost. The ppt slides have no focus and are arbitrarily representing whatever he's going to say. You know you always have other choices.
He is a tough grader. The lab is extremal hard.
Amazing teacher, I very intentionally took him again for CS449. Easy going and a reasonable grader, he is almost always available in one way or another to discuss problems.
Humorous and productive lectures. Extremely accessible outside of class (discord) and always willing to answer questions. Pitt needs to pick this guy up for full-time.

Jarrett is one of the best professors I've had at Pitt. He cares about his students actually learning the material and he went to Pitt for undergrad so he understands the struggle. He explains things very well in small pieces, and is always available for students outside of class (even at ridiculous times). Really lays things out in an easy way.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: With Jarrett, no question is dumb. Even if you sleep in class and need help during the labs bc you weren't paying attention, he'll help you and be patient. He's an extremely easy grader and it's actually really hard to fail this class. If you can, please choose Jarrett to teach you for any CS course. He's hilarious and passionate about what he does

Jarrett is the best professor I have ever had. He is an amazing lecturer, his projects are fun relevant to the class. He is always willing to help you out and make sure that you know what is going on. Overall he is just a great person. I know I am taking every opportunity to take him again.

Materials are not hard. Go to classes and you will be good. Tests are easy and he really gives good grades. Assignments are also easy.  The projects need some work, debugging takes longer than I thought.  I did terrible job on time management during finals' week and did not do well on final project so get A-, but still strongly recommended.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria

review_text: Better than every professor in that department. He makes the class enjoyable and explains everything in his own words rather than the textbook's. Very fair (and generous) with grading.  Also qualifies for most chill instructor I've ever had. Take any and every class you can with him.

I've now had Jarrett for both CS0447 and CS0449 and I would take this man for ANYTHING. He is caring, funny, approachable, knowledgeable, and enthusiastic. I received an A in both classes and feel like I've never understood the material better. He creates a discord chat server where students ask him questions 24/7 and he is always ready to respond.

Easily one of the best teachers I've had. Very fun lectures, great personality, and a walking memelord. When the tags say "Accessible Outside Class", the reviews absolutely mean it to the 10th degree. This ain't just because of office hours, nor emails. If you have a chance to take a course with him, do it.

Jarrett is the best professor I've ever had. He is almost always available, and full of energy.  He gets to know all of his students and is really passionate about the subject matter. His lectures are fantastic and full of personality. I highly recommend everyone take at least one class with him.

Jarrett is one of the best professors I've had at Pitt. His lectures are funny and interesting, but what makes Jarrett stand out the most is how much he cares. He goes above and beyond what is required to help his students, and is almost always available. The CS department should give Jarrett as many classes as he wants, whatever classes he wants.

Yes, he is that awesome of a teacher. Super upbeat, knowledgeable, and down to earth. He also makes a point to be approachable outside of class for any questions students may have about all things CS-related, even if they are not for the specific course he is teaching. Honestly, every teacher needs to be like Jarrett

The BEST professor you will ever have. Get to know him and treat him with respect and dignity and he will return the favor. The class isn't an easy A, but if you take advantage of the material and pay attention in class you will learn a sh%&* ton and you will enjoy his banter. He created a discord server for our class and it was invaluable.

If you have the opportunity, take a class with him. Probably the most understanding professor of remembering what it's like to be a student. Will work you on anything you ask. Has personality which makes lectures interesting. Have a sense of humor, allows for a lot of anonymous discussion boards in class to ask questions which quickly go down hill

Even though attendance isn't mandatory, he makes you want to show up to class since his lectures are so great. He is very caring and goes out of the way to make his class not only passable but also fun and informative.

Jarrett is not only an awesome teacher, but he is a great human being. He is open and honest with students, and he is probably one of the most human-like approachable professors I've ever encountered. His group chats are very useful, and he was very responsive to any questions I've had. He is harder on himself more so than the students.

Jarrett is the best teacher I've ever had. He cares a lot about students and works hard to make an environment where students feel comfortable learning and want to learn more. He goes out of his way to make himself available to students and stresses that it's okay to have questions. I recommend highly and I plan on taking any class that he teaches.

The best Jarrett ever. Delivers material in an involved way, going in depth to show you what you're learning and how it applies to other concepts and how it is implemented. Full of personality, very human and friendly. Ever lecture was a rollercoaster of emotion. Currently taking every class I can manage to take with him. 10/10
The best professor I have ever had. He explains everything clearly, use useful presentations, and always ready to help.
Jarrett is lit. He baked us cookies and responded to project questions at 2am (not even the day it was due). You won't regret taking his class.

Legend. Performs triple and sometimes quadruple backflips during lecture to keep students' attention at the off chance they look away from his glorious beard. Very friendly and welcoming, and is always (literally always) willing to assist you. Has a Donkey Kong t-shirt. 10/10.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

review_text: Jarrett was an amazing teacher.  He's goofy and fun, while still getting across the material in a very effective manner.  His power points were awesome and had a lot of effort put into them in order to provide quality examples, and were available online.  Attendance is non-mandatory, but recommended.  You can get around it, but slightly harder.

Always available outside of class via email or office hours. His lectures are filled with personality which make them entertaining and easy to follow. Very fair grading and clear criteria. He's very passionate about computer science and it shows in his lectures.
rating_tags: ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: By far the worst experience I've had taking a class at Pitt. Projects were the busiest, I often got the highest scores, but truth be told I didn't learn anything about AI from the projects. Easily the worst organized class at Pitt.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

review_text: Not harder in terms of content than 1550 or 1501, but get ready to spend time 20+ hour per project in the Python debugger. Lost some blood and sleep worrying about the projects, but at the end of the day, I'm glad I worked through them. Tough grader, but curves assignments and exams somewhat. Good lecturer and kind-hearted imo too.
Worst class ever, absolutely horrible in every aspect.

Don't believe anyone who says that her style of teaching works for this class. She simply wasn't prepared with any of the material and wasn't prepared to teach this class. With that said, she could have taken measures to make it a better experience, but instead the exams, projects and grading were made super-ambiguous and extremely difficult.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

review_text: No doubt this class was difficult, but her style of teaching worked for me, but that might not be true for everyone, so its really going to depend on the individual. Her heart is in the right place and she clearly cares about teaching. Assignment 1 is really cool and interesting, but the 2nd project was pretty confusing. Would recommend

Lectures are clueless just like all the students. Projects are literally the hardest of all the CS classes I've ever taken. Exams are the most unpredictable. Everything is graded so harshly in this class. That's literally all you need to know about this class. Take it at your own risk.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: I will give Dr. Walker the benefit of the doubt that it is her first time teaching this course, so I accept the issues with the course content. However, this does not make up for the extreme difficulty of both exams and projects throughout the semester as well as large amounts busy work. Take the course in a year or two once it has been figured out
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

review_text: Erin Walker is simply not a good teacher. She can't teach the subject of Artificial Intelligence, or probably anything related to Computer Science. She can't effectively explain course concepts by herself, almost like she's learning about them along with us! I will give her a 2 only because she actually shows some signs of mercy every now and then.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

review_text: This AI is literally harder than 1550 and 1501. First class where I felt like all my efforts had to go into AI, otherwise Id easily fail. She posts homework late (yet still expects them on time), grades projects like Satan, and has no qualities of a good Pitt cs professor. DO NOT TAKE AI WITH HER.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: Definitely take it if you want to destroy half your brain cells. More than half the class has no idea what's going on and the assignments are the hardest ever. The hard part is not even AI stuff. Projects are super ambiguous and unorganized. Heard she's ok for HCI though. Extremely tough grader.

Would not recommend. Extremely unorganized, the class is extremely boring to the point where counting ceiling tiles is more entertaining. Assignments are unnecessarily difficult and much of the class is spent doing worksheets that we do not finish. Grading on exams and projects was so ambiguous that I thought it might have depended on the weather

You're gonna have a bad time. I'm not sure what she's like for HCI, but this AI class blows anything I've taken at Pitt out of the water. She at least cares & is a nice person, which prevents me from giving a 1. Her projects (in my opinion) are much more difficult than that of 1501 or 1550 (both with Farnan for reference).

I've had Dr. Walker for 2 different classes. This was her first time teaching AI, and so there were some issues with clarity, organization, etc. so I think this might contribute to difficulty. The class is extremely difficult and she does try her best to help students (maybe next semester it'll be better).Projects were the hardest I've had.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: dont take her, hardest teacher ive ever had at pitt and i still dont know anything about AI!!! really bad grading criteria

Blame Pitt for giving Mrs. Walker this class to teach. If you look at her other school ratings, she tought HCI. But since Pitt is so desperate for teachers, they gave her AI. She is clearly out of her depth. She isn't stupid but being able to teach a subject requires a deep familiarity w/ it. In the future, only take a class of hers if it is in HCI
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: take if you like TONS of busy work. expect 3x the work of any other class and very harsh grading. she skipped at least 4 classes for 'PhD commitments'. as the substitute, the TA sat in corner, silent for full 75 minutes. otherwise, TA was invisible, except when nitpicking & slashing points from projects. you wont get an A, take if you hate your GPA

Doesn't teach. 85% of lecture time is spent on completing worksheets. Claims you learn more, but most fail. The grading: very ambiguous & unforgiving. Class becomes a gauntlet of busy work that too concerned with finishing, results in very little learning. If you don't attend, finish worksheet & question each class, you lose points. Avoid.
rating_tags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

review_text: Assignment/exam grading was way, way too subjective and unclear. I found that the more work I put into the class as the semester went on, the poorer my grades became in this class. Don't expect any grades to be returned to you in a timely manner. By finals week, I only had roughly 25% of my grades done. Avoid this professor at all costs.

This was Prof. Walker's first semester, so there was more administrative overhead than I expected. Other than that this was one of my favorite courses @ Pitt! Walker is super passionate and deeply knowledgable about HCI, and that translated to her lectures. She also focused on learning by doing, not just talking, which was effective for me.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Participation matters

review_text: good luck to anyone taking him. you get absolutely no guidance on labs/assignments, lectures are very monotonous/boring and have LITTLE TO NO correlation to what is on the exams and assignments. you are basically teaching yourself in this class
The course material is pretty dry but he is just not a good lecturer. He is not very difficult but you def won't learn much from his classes.

I am taking the class CS2756. The professor is patient and approachable. The lecture overall is interesting. Some concepts have been covered in other lectures but he provides different views. The homework questions are very helpful for understanding. The professor gave good advice on course project.

He explains things clearly in the lectures, and is very patient in anwering questions during office hours. I learned a lot from this class. The class covers many topics but is relatively easy. The design of labs and lectures seem to be a bit disconnected though.

Jia does not seem to care about this class. We were told to use Ed Discussions for posting questions, but I have not seen him answer any threads all semester. The recitation and project requirements were very vague and the TAs were no help. The class itself you can pass without going to class, and after one lecture you won't want to go anyways.
His class is a joke. Easy but such a hassle. Unclear requirements, never responds to emails or a discussion thread he told us to use, etc. Just shows up and does the bare minimum.

The lectures can be dry and drag on, but overall the class was bearable and the slides/recitations were helpful enough to complete the assignments. The TA was a bit disengaged throughout the semester. Exams were online and weighted minimally, so most of the grade is based on assignments and the term project.
The entire class has felt unorganized and it's difficult to get ahold of either the professor or TA for clarification. Really do not recommend him

Lectures are pretty dry. He's not super accessible and seems to not even be knowledgable as to what happens with labs and assignments. All assignments seem hastily put together with often unclear instructions or details on what's expected in the submission. I get the impression he doesn't care about teaching at all and just wants to do his research

Xiaowei is an OK prof. You can tell that he's super smart, but he goes through dense lecture material really quickly so it's easy to get lost or fall behind. However, his assignments don't go as in-depth as his lectures do, so it's not hard to do well. He is also always willing to answer questions and is very caring and accommodating as a person.

He was really monotone when lecturing the whole class would end up doing something else rather than tuning in. The material was interesting nonetheless but could've been eased down cause this was definitely geared toward grad students. Either way he was really approachable and knowledgeable! Exams are hard study!
It's an interesting course. But only for students who are eagar to do stuffs for frontend

This teacher wasn't very nice to students and the material wasn't explained very well. The material seemed interesting though so with a different professor the class may have been better. Don't really recommend

This is the first semester he has changed the course, where we have two exams and assignments, we are his guinea pigs, but that should not be an excuse to teach the material in such a dry and boring way, I have learned nothing so far and its a shame cause I was excited going into the course initially, goes on tangents! Exam was hard!

This person is very arrogant and sometimes says shocking things that shouldn't come from a teacher. Occasionally, you can learn something from the feedback, but compared to the pressure he puts on you, it's not worth it.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

review_text: Prof was a nice guy, decent lecturer. All my complaints are more about the course material. Was expecting to learn more about UI, but the entire class was pretty much just an app development process. Every assignment was group based where you planned and made a Flutter app. I spent way more time working on backend and planning than learning UI.

He's an okay (but not great) lecturer, but honestly came off as arrogant and was disrespectful at several points during the term. HW were fine but not that useful. Would not recommend taking with them.

Dr. Biehl has a bit of a monotone voice for lectures, but certainly teaches well. The class is pretty easy, but you still learn a lot, definitely recommend taking. Quizzes are pretty easy if you do the readings, project is well-scoped and doable.

Jacob Biehl is one of the best professors I have ever had at Pitt. He makes lectures genuinely interesting (real world examples, guest lecturers, etc) and makes it clear he wants you to be successful. Homeworks are easy if you do the readings and the project is fun and requirements are very clear. If you have the chance to take him, do it!!!!

Dr. Kovashka knows a lot about the Deep Learning field, and may not always be able to simplify the content to a beginner level super well. However, as a CS student, I believe you are doing yourself a disservice by not taking this class. The content is super relevant and Kovashka will answer any questions you may have and is very accomodating.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things

review_text: Hands-down an incredible professor. MATLAB homeworks are very time-consuming and math-heavy, and the pencil-and-paper exams are extremely difficult, but both Kovashka and the TA (Erhan Unal) graded very generously. I HIGHLY recommend taking linear algebra beforehand; otherwise, be prepared to do a lot of catch-up when it comes to the math.

The course is extremely basic for a graduate course. People without any background on Image Processing can take the course. If you are a graduate student(MS or PhD), you might be disappointed. The home work is more of a deep learning tutorial rather than a proper vision course. The math is minimal and there is no building up models from the start.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--GROUP PROJECTS

review_text: It's true, the slides have a lot of math, but if you've taken linear algebra, calculus 3, and an optimization class, it's pretty simple math. A ton of homework, and mostly theory based where you implement the actual algorithms. If you're competent, it's an easy A. If you're incapable of doing math, it's a B.

Get ready for a lot of math, including calculus and linear alg. I felt she went into too much depth with the math. But the tests were surprisingly easy and not so intense. You won't use libraries--you'll write ML algorithms in MATLAB from scratch. Very theoretical/research-oriented, does not teach how ML is done in industry. Very fun, caring person

Adriana's computer vision is great! Homeworks takes some time(4-6 hours for the hardest one), but they are doable. Tests are easy, no tricks, you can get around 90 as long as you truly understand the materials. Review the ppt, go to office hours if there is confusions, and you are good to go. I really learned a lot from this course. Recommended!

Computer Vision!  This class is very difficult, but I learned a lot . As someone else said, there is a TON of math-heavy programming homework, and exams are very hard. But... it was all graded very leniently. Show up, put in a serious effort, and you will be okay.
LOTS of homework
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: The problem I have with him is availability and project guidance what made the condensed semester even more difficult then it had to be. For one, he offers little to no guidance in office hours and barely answers any emails. He's left me and my partner with virtually no guidance along with other groups only a few days before our project was due.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Test heavy--Group projects

review_text: One of the hardest CS classes I have taken. The projects are in C/C++ and are crazy and very complicated. I hated the projects but the lectures were amazing. Learning about computer networking was very interesting. The midterm/Final were very hard since this class has so MUCH information.
rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--Amazing lectures

review_text: Tests were definitely pretty hard (averages consistently in the high 60s) and he was pretty strict with grading. He's very organized and the projects weren't too bad. I did learn a lot but he's not the best lecturer. A bit robotic but going to class does help. He also isn't very available (office hours only once a week for a couple hours).

The worst. Every time I asked him a question he gave me cryptic answers and acted like he didn't understand what I was asking. Several projects were only tangentially related to what we were learning, and the exams focused too much on edge cases and other minutia instead of an understanding of the material. I wish the TA did lectures instead of him

If you can take Prof Lange for CS449, do it! He's a very reasonable grader, and a great guy. I know some profs can make this material harder than it needs to be, but Jack is incredibly fair.  The class is dry, but this prof is the best option. Highly recommended.

Great first run of the unreal class- Sanjay is incredibly well versed and is happy to tailor the course material to the class's interests. Tech setup is a little janky to begin, but now seems to work. Lectures are long and dense, and hard take on paper. Marking up the code on the slides and saving the annotations is key. Programming: 6-10 hr / wk

435 w/ Sanjay is one of the best structured classes I've ever taken. It does take a good bit of effort, but the lectures/assignments are really interesting and even fun. I did think some of the coding questions on the final were harder than they should have been, but overall I really enjoyed this class-- recommend it for a tech elective!

Class-wise: clear/detailed slides, practice exams exactly like the real ones, and github actions tests on the PAs make them pretty easy (1-2 hours each + OH). I definitely learned a lot! As a prof: def one of the most chill/funny in the entire CS dept. I think he's chronically online or smth LOL just like us fr. also very kind and helpful in OH!
Everybody knows how good Sanjay is. Take him if you can!!!

Grading works differently in this class than in the normal grading system, but his rubrics are always very clear and specific. Overall, a very easy class if you follow the directions exactly. He has lots of industry experience and gives lots of helpful advice about getting into the games industry. The textbook is optional.

Sanjay is such a great guy. Lectures are laid back and informative. Office hours are even more casual. Students feel free talking to Sanjay about anything. Material is interesting for the most part. PAs and exams are all manageable.

Great professor! Very informational lectures. The assignments are not easy; you're expected spend 5-10 hours each week on it, but they are well designed to help you learn. Would strongly recommend those interested in gameplay programming to take it.
Nice person but a really bad teacher. Manymany homework, and hard exams. Would NOT recommend

Unreal class was amazing for its first semester. Labs were split with 40 min follow-along video, and 4-8 hours of work each week. A lot of first-hand experience with coding in unreal. Q's were answered quickly on Piazza, and OH was easily available. Everyone is provided a VM to run UE5, so accessibility was great. Take this class if you can!

Sanjay is a good prof and really kind, but to be honest I really don't recommand this compiler course. It sounds cool when you first heard about it, but things really fresh your mind when you start taking this course and find how abstract and unuseful the knowledge is. Seriously, I started realizing how these things are used after the midterm.

Sanjay is the best. Pro C++ has been one of my favorite classes at USC. Homeworks really drilled the concepts and the midterm and final was super practical. The class was not easy, but Sanjay taught the material well, engaged the class so it was extremely doable. The PAs were a bit tough, but just doing them was strong prep for the exams.

Had a really good experience in ITP 380 w/ Sanjay, so I decided to take ITP435: Pro C++, and this class is even better. Not only does course content cover things you would not normally learn w/ classes like CS103/CS104/ITP365, its also is so applicable and intuitive. No other class at USC has prepped me better for Industry than ITP435.

Easily the best Professor I've had at USC! ITP380 is a really interesting class and he does an excellent job teaching it. The assignments are straight forward and easy to follow for the most part, and are also extremely cool. Take this class if you're willing to put in 3-4 hours of work every week and have some C++ experience.

Easily the best professor I've had at USC. He's a real pro in the gaming industry, and knows exactly what he's talking about. Even though 380 is assignment heavy, the assignments are 1) Easily doable if you follow the PDF, 2) There's a unique regrade policy, and 3) Him and his TAs are easily accessible.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: He's hilarious and did a great job during COVID-19 online classes. Sanjay is brilliant so the content's easy for him. But, he'll happily answer any questions if he glosses difficult over something in class.

Sanjay is an awesome teacher for compilers and c++. He's always down to crack jokes and even streams on twitch. Lots of homework, but it also counts for a large portion of your grade. If you like to skip class, the powerpoint slides are excellent and have step-by-step instructions for all topics covered.

Sanjay is an awesome professor! Highly recommend ITP380 if you want to make cool projects in C++ and learn more about the video game industry. You make one new game per week, and you have a week to work more on the assignments and ask for a regrade.

Sanjay is a really great lecturer. He's super animated and has the rare ability of working humor into the otherwise dry sections of the IT/CS material he's tasked with teaching. His office hours get super crowded, so plan ahead if you need help, if you show up at the last minute you'll run out of time.

Sanjay is amazing. The labs (the best part of this class) are interesting and require you to put some serious efforts into them. Getting A is not hard because of the new grading method. This is the class to take if you are interested in gameplay programming.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: Course materials weren't too hard(pretty easy actually), but it took me a good amount of time to finish the weekly lab assignments. Overall a good introductory-level class. He gave solid opinions/feedback and was willing to spend time to help his students out. Attendance was only mandatory for the lab session.

Awesome professor! Helpful at office hours, always cracks jokes during lecture, and is super chill. He's very fair and the ITP 435 is pretty manageable if you start PAs early. Most importantly, he helps you understand what working industry is like and will answer you honestly with no BS. Definitely take any classes you can with him.

He is a respectful and caring professor, willing to debug code for students on piazza. However, he assigns way too much homework. There are TWELVE PAs for the class, most of them took me 8 - 10 hours to complete. Thus, this class easily becomes the most demanding class. I also find it hard to understand some of the concepts he presented in lecture

My favorite professor at USC. His class is useful and well-organized. One of the few classes at USC that would prepare you for tech interviews. Easy A if you do all the assignments and review them before exams.
rating_tags: Lots of homework--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: One of the best professors at USC. Clear lectures, reasonable assignments, fair exams, and a humorous teaching style. The course covers many useful aspects of modern C++. Some of the materials are harder, but by doing the assignments you can soon get hold of it. If you pay attention to the lecture well and ace all the assignments, an A is secured.

Sanjay is a great lecturer. Programming assignments could take many hours so plan your time wisely. Tests were a little tough but he curved the final grade. I learned a lot of new C++ concepts that were not covered in CS103/104. Definitely recommended! Also don't cheat on homework. He uses Moss to catch plagiarism.
rating_tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK--Clear grading criteria--Amazing lectures

review_text: Sanjay is by far one of the best professors Ive ever had. He was one of the first lecturers who made game programming click, which convinced me to continue pursuing Computer Science (Games). Im very thankful I had his classes. Even as a graduate, when I need to review any concepts, I usually re-read his books.

Very useful class if you want to ace those game programming interviews. Not the most engaging lecturer, but super chill. Goes through material super slowly. First midterm was easy, second midterm was low-key kinda hard, final was in-between. Otherwise, not a class you will stress too much about.
If you've never taken Sanjay's class, don't tell people you study games at USC
Not the most charismatic lecturer, but he does his best to get everyone engaged. He's always accessible if anyone has questions, and has a very well organized class.

The midterm is a little bit tricky but if you listen carefully in the first half of the semester it won't be a problem. Homework only contains 5 labs and they are so easy if you follow instructions in the lab guide. You could use UE4 engine to write your own game as project. Better if you know about linear algebra and C++ programing skills.
Great teacher
He is a funny guy who likes video games and to help his students. Honestly one of the nicest people, let alone professors, that I have ever met.
Favorite class & teacher so far
Good teacher
Cool, funny guy. Makes class fun and interesting. You do the work, you'll get an A. Exactly what a professor should be.

The nature of Pro C++ is to expose you to different techniques in the C++ language beyond your fundamental data structures. The programming assignments are really well-designed, and Sanjay is just a cool guy. Lectures are long and might put you to sleep, but the slides are really good that it doesn't matter anyway. Exams aren't hard.
Great teacher, explains everything clearly. The class was a very good learning experience, very worthwile.
Decent professor decent class, better than most progeamming classes.
The worst professor ever in USC. Do not take him

Great professor, clear, easy to understand. Helpful if you ran into a problem. Good teacher overall. But, I feel like the lectures and lab did not contribute as much as I expected. Having previous understanding of the math involved from the CG class, and basic geometry from HS, I didn't learn much new. Labs were following a easy tutorial.

Sanjay's lab assignments are so well designed. The learning curve starts easy but ramps up as class goes on. Paying attention in lecture and studying outside of class is definitely needed for quizzes and midterm.

If you're a games major or interested in games, take Sanjay's ITP380 class ASAP. He teaches you material you need to know for interviews esp in linear algebra for games and programming. Can't give Sanjay higher than a 5 so time to rate him hot.

Sanjay is probably the best professor I've had at USC.  He really hit the nail on saying how USC math makes you hate math lol.  In fact I found out later on, all of the linear algebra I needed for industry was learned through this class.  Take this class very seriously because half the test questions are from interviews.
Awesome teacher, definitely deserving of his 5.0 rating.  He should teach other teacher's how to teach.

Sanjay is THE game programing professor at USC. His lectures are informative and he occasionally tells an anecdote from industry. His labs are well designed to build up the skills a game programmer needs.

Sanjay is a great professor who really helps you learn. His classes aren't tough to do well in but I learned more from him than any other professor at USC. He's really good at presenting material in a way that makes you interested in it and he gives you tons of resources to do well. He's also extremely accessible and helpful.
He's silly, comedic, and useful.

Sanjay is a great teacher and I think he is changing USC's games program for the better. It took me a while to figure him out, but now that I have, I have a ton of respect for him. Talk to him, listen to him, and realize that he is a nerd just like you and me - but a very knowledgeable. You won't find a better professor in the program.

Sanjay is an awesome professor .. His lectures can get boring, but still, pay attention because he gives you info you'll need for the test. The projects are fun and he is very insightful whenever you need help.

Sanjay provides insight into the wonderful world of game programming. His lectures are almost poetic in nature.  Sanjay's delivery is most appealing.  Unfortunately his teaching skill does not translate to SC2. He sucks at that.

The class feels very disorganized. The class material is poorly documented and kinda confusing. The homework contains multiple ambiguities and errors. The TA could not even come up with the correct code himself yet graded us harshly. Also, I do not find the class material interesting.
It's a lot of learning but it comes with a lot of work
This is the WORST class I have ever taken in my life. The university made me have a mandatory counselor meeting because of how stressed this class made me. It's not worth it, pick ANYONE else.

Hardest class I've ever taken by far. Taught like a math class, but with programming HW. If you are comfortable with vector calculus, linear algebra, and probability, you can get a ton out of this class; If not I would definitely look elsewhere. I second what others have said about the HW & exams.  HUGE final curve. Enjoyed the class overall.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

review_text: There are better ML professors who teach more with less pain. His lectures are hard to follow with arbitrary math terms. Homework takes 40+ hours for a B, exams average 15-30%, and it is a ton of stress

Antithesis of an easy A. Slow grading = little feedback before exams, no cheat sheets on very difficult tests, and homework code could take hours to run. He is very accessible and passionate, so you could learn a lot. Linear algebra, probability, multivariable calculus familiarity needed. Would only recommend if machine learning is a priority.
rating_tags: Graded by few things--Accessible outside class--Online Savvy

review_text: I like Dr. Ruozzi, he's a really cool guy but man he does not go easy on you. He's a very smart guy who knows what he's talking about, but I don't think he knows how to bring things down to an intro level class. Homeworks are hard and time-consuming, and the exams are awful with a massive curve. Love him, but there are easier professors.

Stats for AI and ML had super challenging homeworks and the lectures were difficult to understand because it is content that is not well documented in textbooks and youtube videos, he assumes that you already have mastery of algos, prob and stats, and multivariable calculus at start of the course, a lot of people dont have this
rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

review_text: Prof. Ruozzi is an amazing professor overall; however, there's a big caveat when it comes to how he teaches 4375, which is that he teaches it like you're grad students. It's therefore extraordinarily difficult, being easily the hardest class I've ever taken.  You'll learn a lot, though! Plus, he curves really hard, so trying your best helps :)
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lots of homework

review_text: GOAT of a Professor. Really smart, nice and knowlegable. Always answer questions. Tough course but definitely worth it.

His lectures go in-depth and are very good. Don't save the projects until the last 2 hours! They are much more enjoyable if you spend more time on them, lots to learn and feel satisfied about. Tests are very hard, for good reason. The average midterm/final scores were about 25%. Make friends in the class and study together! Difficult, but rewarding

review_text: Very tough class, need to spend 4 to 5 days to complete each assignment. It's worth it.

review_text: His lectures are awesome and descriptive(math heavy). Second part of the class(after midterm) becomes more fast paced. He makes sure that you study well for exam by giving conceptual homeworks. Some of his favourite topics- Convex Optimization, Duality, VC dimension, Gaussian mixture. My advice- Use office hours, piazza & class hours to ask doubts.
rating_tags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Test heavy

review_text: Very tough!
Pick him if you want to learn avoid if you just need a grade

Ruozzi teaches ML as optimization and does not shy away from the theory, so this course is math-heavy. It is the hardest course I've taken at UTD but also one of the most fulfilling. The homeworks usually took me ~20-35 hours each. The workload is gruesome if you want to do well, but if you survive it you'll be highly knowledgeable in the subject.
If you want to actually learn ML go for his class. Otherwise content on youtube is good enough for you, compared to other profs like Anjum and Anurag.
He's by far the best professor for Machine Learning you can find at UTD. Seems like vibhav is overrated and Ruozzi is underrated. Makes you work for it but this course will be worth it.

Professor is knowledgeable on the subject. The class is difficult. You need to know MatLab/Python as you'll be implementing the algorithms you see in class yourself for the homeworks. Don't expect to score high on the exams (iirc he said the average for the midterm was in the 30s-40s). Curves. If you just want a peek at ML, might pick someone else.

This class is definitely difficult, and the really motivated will find plenty of room to go very deep into the material. The professor is highly invested in the subject. Just know what you are getting into, and give yourself time to learn the material.

He is a phenomenal professor for ML. He makes sure that you understand everything and answers all your questions patiently. The assignments are really time consuming and tough but, they are completely worth doing because, the amount of knowledge you gain from them is very high. His exams are really tough!

The professor is very clear, knows the subject and makes it engaging. He is always willing to take questions and is accessible. The course is math intensive but doable if you are willing to work hard. One of the best courses I've taken!
rating_tags: Get ready to read--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Amazing lectures

review_text: Very knowledgeable and answers plenty of questions. However it was by far the hardest class I had ever taken. Exams are very difficult and grading is tough.
The toughest class I've had. No exam but the assignments are heavy. Consider carefully before taking this class
rating_tags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

review_text: Took Statistical Methods in Data Science, and believe me this is the toughest topic that I've ever came across. Maybe it suits best for PhD folks, but not for Masters! No exams but those 7 assignments are no less than 7 final exams! Beware, if you like the subject, ignore me, else please save yourself.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--SO MANY PAPERS

review_text: course content is tough and math heavy, requiring solid math foundation in algebra, analysis, probability, convex optimization and writing proofs. homework load is very heavy and tough, too. rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tests are tough

review_text: He is a really good professor, but he grades really tough and gives a lot of problem sets and the exams are very difficult.
rating_tags: Tough Grader--Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

review_text: very knowledgeable and patient in answering questions of all kind. course content is very hard and math heavy. was unable to understand everything and struggled throughout the whole semester. fortunately managed to understand a couple of the most important concepts, which was enough to complete all the assignments.
rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.

review_text: One of the most amazing professor of Machine Learning at UT Dallas. Learnt a lot doing assignments.  Would definitely recommend to a person who wants to go deep into machine learning.
One of the most knowledgeable professors in the field of Machine Learning. Very quick to respond. Assignments take up your time and exams are tough as well.
VERY TOUGH

Explains the theory behind every algo with examples. HWs will make you really work hard. Always available to clarify doubts. Exams will be very tough. But good thing is grading is relative and if you took the lectures and HWs seriously you need not even study for exams. Best professor to understand the subject and not just learn to apply diff algo.

Professor Ben is brilliant, quick-witted, and an amazing lecturer. While lectures are a tad quick-paced, the explanations are precise and well-defined. Put in hours to understand the slides. Homeworks are challenging but graded fairly. Attempt them yourself to do well on the tests. Pay attention during homework review for good tips.
rating_tags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

review_text: Professor Raichel is by far the best professor I have seen in UTD. This is a proper Masters' course where there is a lot of content. Unlike others his focus is on learning rather than grading.

He clearly has great knowledge, but sometimes lacks charisma while teaching. Grading is tough - since it's an algorithms class, make sure you always mention the correctness criteria for any algorithms on the homework. Not too many graded assignments, so each assignment counts. No exams, only assignments and end-of-semester presentation.
rating_tags: Tough grader--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

review_text: Professor Raichel's class is very good and he does everything on time. Syllabus is completed well before the exams and we get sufficient time to do homework.
